title: How must you indicate your intention to use the ADR process?
text: You must indicate your intention to use the ADR process in your Notice of Appeal (NOA).

title: What exception exists to the R2.5 million threshold rule for vendors on the payments basis?
text: Should the increase in turnover be solely as a result of enterprise assets being sold because of a permanent reduction in the size of the business or due to the replacement of capital assets or abnormal circumstances of a temporary nature, the payments basis may be retained despite exceeding the R2.5 million threshold.

title: What is the EFT payment limit set by banks and PASA, and what should vendors do if they need to make payments exceeding this limit?
text: The limit for credit payments made via EFT is R5 000 000. EFT payments in excess of R5 000 000 must be cleared with your banker.

title: Are timeshare schemes included in the exemption for services to members of sectional title, share block or old age schemes?
text: No, the exemption for certain services to members of a sectional title, share block or old age scheme funded out of levies is not applicable to timeshare schemes.

title: What are the requirements for each separately registered enterprise, division or branch?
text: Each separately registered enterprise/division/branch must: (1) retain the same tax period as the main branch (except farmers in certain cases), (2) submit separate returns and payments, (3) retain the same accounting basis as the parent vendor and keep its own accounting records, and (4) remain registered until cancelled by the parent body or until the parent body's registration is cancelled.

title: What is the phone number for the SARS Service Centre?
text: The SARS Service Centre can be reached on 0800 00 7277. International callers may contact the Service Centre on +27 11 602 2093.

title: Are syringes and vet's fees included in the VAT exemption for remedies or medicines?
text: No, syringes and vet's fees are not included in the VAT exemption for remedies or medicines.

title: Can vendors send cash through the post to pay SARS?
text: No, vendors should not under any circumstances send cash in the post. SARS does not accept payments via the post office, including by way of a postal order or money order.

title: What is the de minimis rule threshold for VAT input tax deduction?
text: The de minimis rule allows full VAT deduction when the apportionment ratio is 95% or more.

title: Where must the input tax be claimed in a VAT return?
text: The input tax must be claimed in field 18 of the VAT201 return.

title: What is the purpose of the SARS official card?
text: The card is used to demonstrate the authority of a SARS official to perform functions or duties. This ensures that SARS officials are not impersonated and that the public is satisfied that they are dealing with a SARS official and know the identity of that official.

title: What are the two bases on which vendors can account for VAT?
text: Vendors are generally required to account for VAT on the invoice (accrual) basis, but the payments (cash) basis is allowed in some cases. The payments basis is only available to specified qualifying vendors and is subject to prior approval from SARS.

title: What is the increased compulsory registration threshold for foreign electronic services suppliers effective from 1 April 2019?
text: The compulsory registration threshold for foreign electronic services suppliers was increased from R50 000 to R1 million effective from 1 April 2019.

title: How is the input tax deduction calculated in tax period 1 according to Example 17?
text: The input tax deduction in tax period 1 is calculated as R400 × 15/115 = R52.17, where R400 is the amount paid immediately to the non-vendor.

title: When does cancellation of registration normally take effect, and what is different for voluntary deregistration?
text: Cancellation of registration normally takes effect from the last day of the tax period in which a vendor ceases trading. However, in the case of voluntary deregistration, the Commissioner will decide the date of deregistration and the final tax period.

title: Why is BB not required to register as a VAT vendor despite having annual income exceeding R1 million?
text: BB is not required to register as a vendor because BB is providing exempt services of caring for children in a crèche, even though the annual income exceeds the R1 million threshold. Exempt services are not subject to VAT registration requirements regardless of income level.

title: How should a tax invoice be formatted when a supplier makes mixed supplies that are treated differently for VAT purposes?
text: The tax invoice must clearly distinguish between the various supplies and indicate separately the applicable values and the tax charged (if any) on each supply.

title: What is required in a VAT class ruling application?
text: In the case of a VAT class ruling, a description of the class members is required.

title: What exceptions are excluded from the expanded scope of electronic services under the new ESR?
text: The exceptions excluded from the expanded scope of electronic services are telecommunications services, certain group company transactions, and educational services, which are similar to those contemplated in the exemption under section 12(h).

title: How must VAT returns be submitted when a main registered vendor registers a branch for foreign entities?
text: The main vendor must submit a VAT return to account for its own VAT, and a separate VAT return must be submitted for the branch or division to account for the VAT of all the other entities. This distinction ensures that supplies made by the main registered vendor can be separated from those of the other foreign entities.

title: What is the de minimis rule in VAT input tax deduction?
text: The de minimis rule allows a taxpayer to deduct the full amount of VAT as input tax when the apportionment ratio (taxable use) is equal to or greater than 95% of the total use or consumption.

title: What is the scope of the deemed supply under section 8(8)?
text: The deemed supply in section 8(8) only arises in respect of cash indemnity payments made to, or on behalf of, the insured party under a contract of insurance.

title: What are the channels through which a person can apply for VAT registration?
text: The application for registration must be made through one of the following channels: eFiling; or at a SARS branch, via virtual, audio or walk in appointment made via the SARS website. Alternatively, an authorised registered tax practitioner may make the application on behalf of the applicant, provided the application is accompanied by the power of attorney.

title: What is the time of supply for the recipient when payment is made through a card inserted into a machine?
text: The time of supply for the recipient is when the coin, token, paper money or card is inserted into the machine, meter or device or when payment is tendered through other means such as swiping the debit/credit card.

title: What documentary proof must be retained to apply the zero rate for cartage services?
text: The following documentary proof must be obtained and retained: the supplier's copy of the zero-rated tax invoice, the recipient's order or contract between the supplier and the recipient, the transport documentation as required for the relevant mode of transport, export documentation as prescribed under the Customs and Excise Act (for example, the SARS Customs Declaration), proof of payment in respect of the supply of the movable goods, where applicable proof of payment of the transport costs by the supplier where a cartage contractor was used to deliver the movable goods, and where the goods are transported by road or rail, proof that the goods were received by the recipient in the export country.

title: What information must be disclosed regarding past input tax deductions in a ruling application?
text: A statement in the ruling application must indicate whether input tax has been deducted in past tax periods using any method other than the one prescribed in BGR 16, and if so, from when that method was applied.

title: What is the VAT rate applied to exports under certain circumstances?
text: Exports are taxed at 0% under certain circumstances.

title: What are the two main types of penalties discussed in Chapter 11 of the VAT 404 Guide?
text: The two main types of penalties are administrative non-compliance penalties and understatement penalty.

title: What happens if a taxpayer's input tax deductions exceed their output tax liability on the VAT201 return?
text: If deductions exceed the total output tax liability on the VAT201 return (and any other amounts owed to SARS for past tax periods or other taxes), or if there is no output tax for that particular tax period, the excess will be refunded to the taxpayer.

title: From what date does proviso (xiii) to the definition of 'enterprise' clarify that supply of the right of use of ships, aircraft and rolling stock by non-residents is no longer regarded as an enterprise in the Republic?
text: From 1 April 2021, the supply by a non-resident of the right of use of ships, aircraft and rolling stock in the specified circumstances will no longer be regarded as an 'enterprise' conducted in the Republic.

title: Why was section 20(8A) introduced regarding repossession and surrender of goods under an instalment credit agreement?
text: Section 20(8A) was introduced to deal specifically with documentary requirements in connection with the repossession and surrender of goods under an instalment credit agreement, because due to the hostile nature of repossession and surrender, the debtor is often reluctant to cooperate, there was difficulty in obtaining a signed VAT264 declaration from the debtor, and no actual payment occurred so no proof of payment existed.

title: What is included in the definition of commercial accommodation for VAT purposes?
text: Commercial accommodation includes board or board and lodging supplied together with domestic goods and services (for example, meals, laundry services, the use of a telephone) in a house, flat, apartment, room, hotel, guest house etc.

title: What records must be maintained by a vendor when goods are repossessed or surrendered under an ICA?
text: Records must be maintained by the vendor deducting the input tax as per section 20(8A) and the further particulars prescribed in BGR 63.

title: What are the acceptable methods for submitting a VAT Ruling application to SARS?
text: VAT Ruling applications must be submitted to SARS by e-mail to VATRulings@sars.gov.za or fax number 0860 540 9390. Submissions received by any other means, such as submissions dropped off at SARS branch offices or e-mailed to any SARS official, will not be accepted.

title: When is the supply of goods under a lay-by agreement deemed to take place?
text: The supply of goods under a lay-by agreement is only deemed to take place when the goods are delivered to the recipient.

title: Which guide provides details on the VAT treatment of share block companies?
text: VAT 412 – Guide for Share Block Schemes provides details on the VAT treatment of share block companies.

title: What types of documents do NOT constitute official publications according to the VAT 404 Guide for Vendors?
text: Documents that do not constitute official publications include: this guide or any other guide issued on a specific matter; media releases, published articles; and operational practices or procedures.

title: What are the two main restrictions on decisions that can be given under section 72?
text: No decision can be given under section 72 which has the effect of reducing or increasing the liability for tax levied under the VAT Act, or is contrary to the construct and policy intent of the VAT Act as a whole or any specific provision thereof.

title: Where can vendors find more information on the TA Act?
text: Vendors can find more information on the TA Act on the Tax Administration webpage on the SARS website, which includes documents such as the Short Guide to TA Act, 2011 and Interpretation Note 68.

title: Can a VAT Ruling be subject to objection or appeal?
text: A VAT Ruling is only binding on the Commissioner and cannot become the subject of an objection or appeal, unless it concerns the refusal by the Commissioner to approve a special method of apportionment proposed by a vendor to calculate input tax on expenses for mixed purposes as contemplated in section 17(1).

title: What is the minimum period for which a vendor must use a selected fixed day or date for the cut-off rule?
text: A vendor must use a selected fixed day or date consistently for a minimum period of 12 months.

title: What are the contact options available if you need further information or clarification on VAT matters?
text: You can visit the SARS website, contact the SARS National Service Centre (0800 00 7277 for local calls or +27 11 602 2093 from abroad, between 8am and 4.30pm South African time except Wednesdays when available from 9am to 4.30pm), have a virtual consultation with a SARS consultant by appointment via the SARS website, contact your own tax advisors, submit legal interpretative queries to TAAInfo@sars.gov.za, or submit a ruling application to VATRulings@sars.gov.za.

title: What is the minimum consideration threshold that determines whether a full or abridged tax invoice should be issued?
text: According to the examples, an abridged tax invoice is used for consideration less than R5,000, while a full tax invoice is used for consideration more than R5,000.

title: Which court case is cited regarding supplies not made for consideration?
text: KCM v Commissioner South African Revenue Services [2009] ZATC 2 is cited regarding supplies not made for consideration.

title: What is the apportionment ratio used in Example 21 and how is it determined?
text: The apportionment ratio is 60%, which is based on the turnover-based apportionment method.

title: What adjustment applies to temporary letting of newly developed dwelling units from 1 January 2018 until the end of March 2022?
text: Any temporary letting of newly developed dwelling units from 1 January 2018 until the end of March 2022 would be subject to the adjustment under section 18(1) based on the OMV of the property.

title: What is the VAT treatment for accommodation charges when a person stays longer than 28 days in a hotel or similar establishment?
text: When a person stays for longer than 28 days in any hotel, guesthouse, inn, boarding house, retirement home, or similar establishment, only 60% of an all-inclusive charge for accommodation and domestic goods or services will be subject to VAT. If charges for domestic goods and services are separately itemised and do not form part of the all-inclusive charge, these supplies will attract VAT at the standard rate on the full value.

title: What are some examples of entertainment expenses mentioned in the guide?
text: Examples of entertainment include staff refreshments such as tea, coffee and beverages, catering services for staff canteens, Christmas lunches and parties, golf days for customers and clients, beverages and meals at product launches, entertainment in restaurants and theatres, and capital goods such as hospitality boxes, holiday houses, yachts and private aircraft used for entertainment.

title: What was the main legal question addressed in the Wenco International Mining Systems case?
text: The case dealt with the question of whether a non-resident person was carrying on an enterprise in the Republic or whether that activity was conducted by a separate 'branch' in South Africa for VAT registration purposes.

title: What is an association not for gain?
text: An 'association not for gain' is essentially a religious institution or other society, association or organisation (including an educational institution of a public character) which is not carried on for profit and is required to use any property or income solely in the furtherance of its aims and objects.

title: How can vendors who submit returns manually obtain a VAT201 return?
text: Vendors who submit their returns manually are required to request the VAT201 return from the nearest SARS office in person, in writing, or telephonically via the SARS Service Centre on 0800 00 7277.

title: What information must appear on the tax invoice for zero-rated supplies?
text: The VAT registration number of the purchaser must appear on the tax invoice.

title: Under what section can a vendor apply for a VAT ruling to use alternative documentary proof for a deduction?
text: A vendor may apply for a VAT ruling under section 16(2)(g) requesting the Commissioner to confirm that alternative documentary proof held by the vendor may be used to substantiate a deduction, should the vendor be unable to obtain the prescribed documentation under section 16(2)(a) to (f).

title: What are the prerequisites for a vendor to make a deduction on irrecoverable debts?
text: There must have been a taxable supply for a consideration in money, and the vendor must have already accounted for the supply in a VAT return.

title: What is the time of supply of imported services?
text: The time of supply of imported services is the earlier of the time that an invoice is issued by the supplier or the recipient, or any payment is made by the recipient in respect of that supply.

title: What are the only circumstances that may be considered beyond a vendor's control when requesting remission of interest on late VAT payment?
text: Only the following circumstances may be considered to be beyond the vendor's control: a natural or human made disaster, a civil disturbance or disruption in services, or a serious illness or accident.

title: What form of relief does SARS provide for understatement penalties under the VDP?
text: SARS may grant relief in respect of a portion of the understatement penalty (USP) incurred in respect of tax defaults voluntarily disclosed for which relief has been sought and granted under the VDP. The relief is in the form of a reduced or zero percentage penalty as compared to the higher percentage applicable for similar behaviours had the vendor not voluntarily disclosed the tax defaults.

title: What alternative option do non-resident group companies have regarding registration?
text: Any of the non-resident group companies may elect to register independently as a vendor in South Africa, and not form part of the branch registration under the new dispensation.

title: Which cooking oils are covered by this list?
text: Vegetable cooking oil is covered, excluding olive oil.

title: What recourse does a vendor have if SARS decides not to remit or reduce an administrative non-compliance penalty?
text: If SARS decides not to remit or reduce the administrative non-compliance penalty, the vendor may object to this decision under the Dispute Resolution provisions of the TA Act as outlined in Chapter 14. The objection and appeal lies against the decision not to remit the penalty and not against the penalty assessment itself.

title: What action must a vendor take if their business undergoes major restructuring or significant changes in the nature of taxable and non-taxable supplies?
text: The vendor is required to approach the Commissioner to confirm whether the current method of apportionment is still appropriate.

title: What must an agent furnish to a vendor if the tax invoice is held by the vendor's agent?
text: The agent must furnish a statement to the vendor containing certain particulars about the supplies made to the vendor. This statement must be furnished within 21 days of the end of the calendar month during which the goods were supplied.

title: How should a person calculate their turnover if they operate multiple enterprises or branches?
text: A person who operates several enterprises, or who operates an enterprise in branches or divisions cannot avoid the liability to register for VAT by considering the turnover of each enterprise, branch or division individually. Instead, they must keep a running total of the turnover in respect of all their enterprises for the past 12 months, subject to certain exceptions.

title: What are examples of services according to the VAT 404 Guide?
text: Examples of services include: commercial services such as electricians, plumbers, and builders; professional services such as doctors, accountants, and lawyers; advertising agencies; intellectual property rights including patents, trademarks, copyrights, and know-how; restraint of trade; and cover under an insurance contract.

title: How does the VRA handle VAT refunds of R300 or less?
text: For refunds of R300 or less, the VRA will issue the qualifying purchaser with payment in the currency of the RSA which can be used in the international departure lounge; alternatively, the refund may be effected by EFT.

title: What discretion does the Commissioner have in determining different parts of consideration under section 10(22)?
text: There is no discretion afforded to the Commissioner as to how to determine the different parts of the consideration as contemplated in section 10(22).

title: What must a supplier do if they are unable to obtain the required documentary proof within the prescribed period?
text: If the supplier is unable to obtain the required documentary proof within the prescribed period, the supplier must account for output tax in respect of the supply in field 12 of the VAT return in the tax period in which the prescribed period ends.

title: From whose perspective must the 'fair and reasonable' result be perceived as appropriate?
text: The result must be perceived as 'fair and reasonable' from both the vendor's perspective and the Commissioner's perspective. The result must also be capable of being justified as appropriate in the vendor's circumstances.

title: Under what circumstances can a vendor not make an input tax deduction for a debt that has become irrecoverable under an ICA?
text: A vendor may not make an input tax deduction in respect of a debt which has become irrecoverable under an ICA if the goods supplied in terms of that agreement have been repossessed by or surrendered to the vendor.

title: Under what conditions can SARS officials conduct unannounced inspections at a vendor's place of business?
text: SARS officials can conduct unannounced inspections only in instances where a SARS official has a reasonable belief that a trade or enterprise is carried on at the premises to be inspected. These officials are not allowed to enter any domestic premises or dwelling which is not used for purposes of carrying on a trade or enterprise.

title: Why is the tax fraction 15/115 used instead of 14/114 in calculating C's input tax adjustment in February 2023?
text: The tax fraction 15/115 must be used because the time of supply for the adjustment is after the VAT rate increase from 14% to 15% on 1 April 2018.

title: How are diesel refunds treated in relation to VAT?
text: Diesel refunds are offset against any VAT which may be payable for the tax period concerned, or alternatively, will increase any VAT refund if the input tax for the period exceeds the output tax liability.

title: What are the three instances in which a reduced assessment can be issued?
text: A reduced assessment can be issued in three instances: (1) when an assessment is successfully disputed, a dispute is settled, or a judgment is awarded with no right of further appeal; (2) when an error in the assessment is undisputed and readily apparent, with no objection or appeal necessary; and (3) when a senior SARS official is satisfied that the assessment was based on the failure by a third party or employer to submit a return, an incorrect return submitted by a third party or employer, a processing error by SARS, or a return fraudulently submitted by an unauthorized person.

title: What are the two bases on which VAT can be accounted for according to the guide?
text: The two bases on which VAT can be accounted for are the invoice basis and the payments basis. On the invoice basis, you add the consideration from all sales invoices issued in the tax period for taxable supplies, regardless of whether payment has been received. On the payments basis, you add all actual payments received in the tax period, whether for supplies made in that period, a past period, or a future period.

title: What are the available tax periods for VAT submission to SARS?
text: Available tax periods cover one, two, six or twelve calendar months. Tax periods end on the last day of a calendar month, though vendors may apply to SARS for their tax period to end on another fixed day or date, limited to 10 days before or after the end of the month.

title: Within what timeframe must changes to a vendor's address, banking account, or representative be communicated to SARS?
text: Changes to the address, banking account, or representative of a vendor must be communicated to SARS within 21 business days of the change.

title: At what percentage is VAT included in the total consideration of R570?
When did the new dispute resolution rules come into effect and what did they replace?
text: The new dispute resolution rules came into effect on 10 March 2023 and were promulgated in Notice 3146 (GG 48188). They repealed the previous dispute resolution rules under GN 550 in GG 37819 of 11 July 2014.

title: What is the relationship between the TA Act and the VAT Act regarding tax registration?
text: The TA Act prescribes the general obligations that a person must comply with when registering for a tax, while the VAT Act sets out when a person is required to register.

title: Can the sale of fixed property where a business is conducted be treated as a going concern supply?
text: No. A contract for the sale of individual physical assets such as fixed property cannot be treated as a going concern under section 11(1)(e). When a vendor sells the property from where its business is conducted, the subject of the supply is the fixed property and not the enterprise, so VAT must be charged at the standard rate.

title: In which field of the VAT201 return must the recipient account for output tax on valuable metal supplies?
text: The recipient must account for output tax on the supply of valuable metal in field 12 of the VAT return, in the tax period in which the tax invoice is held or created by the recipient in respect of that supply.

title: What is the primary condition for approval of self-invoicing procedures according to BGR 15?
text: Approval for self-invoicing procedures will only be granted in the case of those industries and transactions where an effective self-invoicing system has traditionally been followed in the past. Approval will not be granted if the purpose is merely to facilitate the obtaining of a tax invoice, credit or debit note by the recipient.

title: What can the Commissioner determine if a vendor fails to notify SARS within 21 days of ceasing to qualify for the payments basis?
text: The Commissioner may determine that the invoice basis should be used from any tax period, including a past tax period, where SARS is satisfied that the vendor has ceased to qualify for the payments basis and such vendor failed to notify SARS within the prescribed 21 days.

title: What is a key disadvantage of the payments basis of accounting?
text: A key disadvantage of the payments basis is that it may be more difficult to implement accounting systems to manage, administer and calculate accurately (for example, reconciliation with income tax returns and adjustments). Additionally, VAT may only be deducted after payments have been made to suppliers.

title: What are the two conditions required for a vendor to obtain separate VAT registration for branches, divisions or enterprises?
text: The two conditions are: (1) an independent system of accounting for each business must be maintained, and (2) the entity must be capable of being separately identified, either by the nature of the activities or the geographic location.

title: When goods must be manufactured or assembled, what is the export deadline?
text: When the goods must be manufactured or assembled, the goods have to be exported within 90 days from the date of completion of the process.

title: What is the key difference between a commercial invoice and a tax invoice?
text: A commercial invoice can be any document notifying the purchaser to make payment and can be issued for transactions concerning taxable and non-taxable supplies. A tax invoice is the normal document required by a vendor to deduct input tax and is issued only in respect of a transaction for a taxable supply. A tax invoice must contain all details prescribed in the VAT Act.

title: Which capital assets are mentioned as VAT-applicable items?
text: Capital assets such as furniture, production machinery, installations, motor vehicles and equipment are subject to VAT.

title: What types of church activities are considered outside the scope of VAT?
text: Activities associated with the church's religious activities, such as efforts to promote the faith and holding church services for its members, are not 'enterprise' activities. They constitute non-taxable supplies made for no consideration and are outside the scope of VAT.

title: What is the primary purpose of the VAT 404 guide?
text: The VAT 404 is a basic guide where technical and legal terminology has been avoided wherever possible. Although fairly comprehensive, the guide does not deal with all the legal detail associated with VAT and is not intended for legal reference.

title: What VAT rate applies to indirect exports if the supplier has charged VAT at the standard rate?
text: If the supplier has charged VAT at the standard rate on an indirect export, the qualifying purchaser may apply to the VRA for a VAT refund.

title: In which fields of the VAT201 return should VAT on valuable metal supplies under the DRC be recorded?
text: The VAT component paid on supplies of valuable metal acquired that are subject to the domestic reverse charge must be accounted for in Field 12 of the VAT201 return. Any input tax on such acquisitions of valuable metal must be shown in Field 18 of the VAT201 return.

title: Where can information about the VAT rulings process be found?
text: Information about the VAT rulings process, including details on how to apply for a VAT ruling and the acceptable form and content of a VAT ruling application, can be found in the updated VAT Rulings Process Reference Guide (Issue 4).

title: What documentation must be kept for diesel refund claims?
text: All relevant documentation relating to diesel purchases, storage and usage must be kept, including the required storage logbooks and usage logbooks that substantiate the actual volumes of diesel stocks and its application for qualifying or non-qualifying activities during the tax period.

title: What should you do if you have underpaid VAT due to a mistake?
text: You should report it to SARS as soon as possible rather than leaving it for SARS auditors to detect. You can make a request for correction on eFiling if you file your returns electronically, or approach your nearest SARS office for assistance.

title: What types of motor car running costs can be deducted as input tax?
text: VAT incurred on repairs, maintenance and general running costs of a motor car such as insurance, tyres, engine oil and servicing may be deducted as input tax if the vehicle is used exclusively in the course of making taxable supplies. This also includes modification and installation costs incurred after the acquisition of the motor car, such as canopy modification or installation of a built-in toolbox for a bakkie.

title: When does the time of supply occur for goods that are not removed in a connected persons transaction?
text: The time of supply occurs at the time the goods are made available to the recipient.

title: What is included in the definition of a dwelling for VAT purposes?
text: A dwelling is any building, premises, structure, or any other place or part thereof used predominantly as a place of residence or abode of any natural person (or which is intended for this purpose), including any fixtures and fittings belonging thereto and enjoyed therewith.

title: Why was the term 'practice generally prevailing' defined in the TA Act?
text: The term was defined in the TA Act to give certainty to its meaning, as vendors were often unsure of the existence of a practice generally prevailing due to reliance on certain non-SARS publications.

title: How is consideration determined in barter transactions for VAT purposes?
text: To the extent that payment of the consideration is made in money, the consideration for the supply will be the amount of money. To the extent that payment is not in money, the consideration is the OMV of the goods or services received.

title: Which zero-rated supplies are specifically mentioned in Chapter 6?
text: Chapter 6 specifically mentions the following zero-rated supplies: basic foodstuffs, fuel levy goods, going concern, services relating to intellectual property rights, certain payments to welfare organisations made by public authorities and municipalities, foreign donor funded projects, goods temporarily imported for repairs, international transport, land situated in an export country, services physically performed outside the Republic, certain services supplied to non-residents, restraint of trade agreements, municipal property rates, and farming goods.

title: How is output tax calculated on an invoice basis?
text: On an invoice basis, output tax is calculated by adding the consideration in respect of all sales invoices (cash and credit sales, including the VAT) issued in the tax period concerned in respect of taxable supplies, regardless of whether payment has been received or not. This also includes the consideration for supplies not yet invoiced where payment has already been made for the supply.

title: What types of rulings are discussed in Chapter 15?
text: Chapter 15 discusses different types of rulings, with specific focus on VAT rulings. It covers VAT rulings application processes, applications for apportionment rulings, and distinguishes between VAT rulings and decisions under section 72.

title: What VAT rate applies to separately itemised domestic goods and services that are not part of an all-inclusive charge?
text: Separately itemised domestic goods and services that do not form part of an all-inclusive charge attract VAT at the standard rate on the full value.

title: Why was section 54(2C) introduced regarding gold exports?
text: Section 54(2C) was introduced to address the difficulty that arises in the gold refining or smelting process where gold from various depositors is co-mingled and cannot be separately identified. This makes it difficult for depositors to obtain the documentary evidence required under the Export Regulation to substantiate the zero-rating of certain exports.

title: Are game-viewing drives and air/sea transportation between two places within the RSA covered by the passenger transportation VAT exemption?
text: No. A charge or fee levied for a game-viewing drive does not fall within the ambit of the exemption and VAT at the standard rate must be levied. Additionally, transportation of passengers by air and sea between two places with the RSA constitutes a standard-rated supply and does not fall within the exemption.

title: Under what conditions is an enterprise carried on through a South African branch deemed to be carried on by a separate person from the offshore establishment?
text: This rule applies if each enterprise is independent and can be separately identified with reference to their separate accounting records and location of the enterprise.

title: In what situation is an agent required to provide a statement to the vendor regarding imported goods?
text: An agent is required to provide a statement when goods are imported on or after 1 April 2015 where the goods are imported by the agent acting on behalf of the vendor (being the principal), and the bill of entry or other prescribed document is held by the agent.

title: What are the exceptional circumstances that must be present for a vendor to be excused from timely payment obligations under section 218 of the TA Act?
text: The exceptional circumstances prescribed in section 218 of the TA Act that must have made it impossible for the vendor to comply with the obligation to pay on time include: a natural or human-made disaster, a civil disturbance or disruption in services, the vendor suffering a serious illness or involvement in a serious accident, the vendor suffering serious emotional or mental distress, certain acts by SARS such as capturing errors or processing delays, the vendor suffering serious financial hardship, and any other circumstances of analogous seriousness.

title: What is the term used to describe invoicing where the recipient issues tax invoices instead of the supplier?
text: This is referred to as 'recipient-created invoicing' or 'self-invoicing'.

title: In what situations may input tax not be deducted by the company?
text: Input tax may not be deducted by the company where the supply is a taxable supply or a supply of second-hand goods (not being a taxable supply), the goods or services were acquired more than six months before the date of incorporation, or the company does not hold sufficient records.

title: What are the exceptions to VAT payable on imported services?
text: VAT is not payable on imported services where: the supply would be exempt from VAT or zero-rated if made in the RSA; the supply is subject to VAT at the standard rate (15%); the supply is of an educational service by an educational institution established in an export country regulated by an educational authority; the supply is of services of a non-resident employee under an employment contract; or the value of the imported services does not exceed R100 per invoice.

title: Under what conditions can separate VAT registration numbers be issued for welfare organisations?
text: Separate VAT registration numbers may be issued for each of its separately identifiable branches, divisions or enterprises, but only if specifically requested.

title: In Example 36, what is the VAT payable on the imported electronic cookbook?
text: The VAT payable on the imported electronic cookbook is R90, calculated as R600 × 15% = R90.

title: What is the VAT treatment for ABC's technical license fee paid to the non-resident UK company in Example 35?
text: VAT would not be payable by ABC on the services supplied by the non-resident because the services are wholly consumed in the course of making taxable supplies.

title: What topics are covered in sections 10.6 through 10.8?
text: Sections 10.6 through 10.8 cover Managing your payment, Refunds, and Change of bank details.

title: In Example 7, what is the VAT amount that J's Construction will account for in the January 2023 return?
text: J's Construction will account for VAT of R84 782,61 in the January 2023 return, calculated as 15 / 115 × R650 000.

title: In Example 41, what is the VAT amount charged on the tax invoice and how is it calculated?
text: The VAT amount charged is R2 400. It is calculated as follows: VAT = R1 000 (tour guide fee) + R10 000 (accommodation) + R5 000 (paintings) = R16 000 × 15% = R2 400.

title: What documentary proof must vendors maintain to substantiate input tax deductions?
text: Vendors must be in possession of documentary proof prescribed by or which is acceptable to the Commissioner to substantiate any input tax and/or other permissible deductions. They must also keep records of all documentary proof and other records of transactions for at least five years.

title: What output tax must AB account for when using paint from trading stock to paint a private residence?
text: AB must account for output tax on the OMV of the paint used. Based on the example provided, the output tax is calculated as: R28 000 × 15 / 115 = R3 652.

title: When did the wording of section 72 become amended, and what impact did this have on non-resident lessors?
text: Section 72 was amended with effect from 21 July 2019. As a result, any application for a decision to allow a non-resident lessor not to register in respect of any lease concluded in respect of the assets concerned before 1 April 2021 under section 72 cannot be considered.

title: Under what circumstances must a resident recipient account for VAT on imported services?
text: A resident recipient has to account for VAT on imported services when they are: not a registered vendor; a vendor but the services are wholly or partly for making exempt supplies; or a vendor but the services are applied for private purposes.

title: What are the grounds for rejecting a non-compliant VAT Ruling application?
text: A non-compliant application will be rejected if the applicant fails the pre-acceptance and compliance process, the application deals with a matter listed in section 80 of the TA Act including matters listed in Public Notice 748, or the taxpayer is unwilling or unable to provide any additional information requested.

title: What email addresses can vendors use to contact SARS?
text: Tax practitioners can email pcc@sars.gov.za and taxpayers can email contactus@sars.gov.za. When you send an email to these mailboxes, you will receive an automated reply with a case number assigned for reference in related correspondence.

title: What approval is required for VAT refunds exceeding R3,000?
text: The Commissioner needs to approve any refund in excess of R3,000.

title: Under what condition can the full amount of VAT charged on second-hand goods be refunded when exported?
text: The full amount of VAT charged may be refunded (less the VRA's commission) when the second-hand goods were originally acquired from another registered vendor before being exported and a normal input tax deduction supported by a tax invoice was made, because the supplier would not have deducted a notional input tax credit on the acquisition of those second-hand goods.

title: What changes will occur to the zero-rating of farming goods when the Minister determines an effective date?
text: The zero-rating under section 11(1)(g) will no longer apply in respect of the purchase of agricultural, pastoral or other goods described in Part A to Schedule 2, and the exemption from VAT on importation of these goods will also no longer apply.

title: Under what circumstances will SARS request a vendor's bank account details over the phone?
text: Under no circumstances will SARS request a vendor's bank account details over the phone or accept bank account detail changes via telephone or e-mail.

title: What entities can be considered an implementing agency?
text: An implementing agency can include the government of the Republic, or any institution or body established and appointed by a foreign government as contemplated in section 10(1)(bA)(ii) of the Income Tax Act.

title: What are the required particulars that must be included in the statement issued by a recipient vendor to the supplier of valuable metal?
text: The statement issued by a recipient vendor to the supplier must contain the tax invoice number, the value of the DRC supplies, a full and proper description of the valuable metal, and confirmation that the VAT charged by the supplier was accounted for and paid to SARS by reflecting the applicable tax period and payment reference number issued by SARS.

title: What is the general rule regarding VAT deduction on goods or services acquired by a vendor for making taxable supplies?
text: Generally, the full amount of VAT on goods or services acquired or imported by a vendor for the purposes of making taxable supplies may be deducted as input tax.

title: What are the three conditions that SARS must be satisfied about before remitting a percentage-based administrative penalty?
text: SARS may remit a percentage-based administrative penalty if satisfied that all of the following conditions are met: (1) The amount involved is either less than R2 000, or the non-payment is a first incidence; (2) The vendor has reasonable grounds explaining the non-compliance; and (3) The incidence of non-payment has been remedied.

title: What forms and security are required to apply for a deferment facility?
text: Application for the deferment facility can be made by completing forms DA650 (registration particulars of applicant) and DA652 (agreement between the applicant and SARS). Additionally, a bank guarantee or surety must be lodged with SARS to secure any potential tax liability, with the amount of security based on the inherent risks of the business and type of goods to be imported.

title: What is the time limit within which SARS may remit interest payable by a vendor?
text: SARS may not remit interest payable by a vendor after a period of five years from the date that the interest first accrued.

title: Who is considered the applicant when a representative such as a lawyer or accountant files an application on behalf of a third party?
text: The third party on whose behalf the application is filed is considered the applicant, not the representative who files the application.

title: What documentation is required to deduct input tax?
text: You cannot deduct input tax or any part thereof unless you are in possession of documents prescribed by or which is acceptable to the Commissioner.

title: What is recipient-created invoicing also referred to as?
text: Recipient-created invoicing is referred to as 'self-invoicing'.

title: What circumstances apply to non-residents supplying electronic services to be considered as engaging in enterprise?
text: Non-residents supplying electronic services are considered to be engaging in enterprise where at least two out of three of the following circumstances apply: 1. The electronic services are supplied to a South African resident, 2. Payment originates from the RSA, or 3. The recipient has an address (that is business, postal or residential) in the RSA.

title: What are the four circumstances in which an original assessment exists according to the TA Act?
text: An original assessment exists in four circumstances: (1) when a return is submitted as a self-assessment including the vendor's calculations of tax payable or refundable; (2) when no return is required but a tax payment is made, the payment is regarded as the original assessment; (3) when a return is submitted but does not include the vendor's calculation of tax payable or refundable and SARS determines the tax liability by way of an original assessment; and (4) when a return is required and the vendor does not file a return, or no return is required and the vendor does not pay the tax required, SARS will assess the vendor by way of an original assessment based on an estimate.

title: What is a credit push in the context of SARS eFiling payments?
text: A credit push refers to payment transactions that are initiated on eFiling and presented to the vendor's bank as a payment request, pending authorisation. Once a vendor has logged into the banking services and effected the payment, it becomes irrevocable.

title: What is the monthly output tax payable by D's Wholesalers for the motor car fringe benefit?
In Example 31, why was ABC (Pty) Ltd able to apply the zero rate on the sale to XYZ Merchants Ltd?
text: ABC (Pty) Ltd was able to apply the zero rate because the supplier ensured that the goods were delivered to the vessel at the Durban harbour (a designated harbour), the goods had received necessary customs clearance and were ready for export, and the goods would be exported from that location.

title: What is the duty imposed on vendors regarding record-keeping under the TA Act?
text: The TA Act imposes a duty on a person to retain the records, books of accounts or documents needed to comply with a tax Act. Vendors are also required to keep additional specific records under section 55 of the VAT Act.

title: What is the condition for deducting input tax on second-hand fixed property acquired on or before 9 January 2012?
text: The input tax is limited to the transfer duty payable. Input tax may only be deducted once the time of supply for VAT purposes has occurred (the earlier of the date on which any payment is made or the date on which the fixed property is transferred to the recipient vendor by registration in the deeds registry), and only after the transfer duty has actually been paid to SARS.

title: Who can initiate the alternative dispute resolution process?
text: The ADR process is initiated either by you (the vendor) or by SARS as mentioned in section 14.3.1.

title: Under what circumstances is a vendor entitled to deduct the portion of VAT that was originally disallowed?
text: A vendor is entitled to deduct the portion of VAT that was originally disallowed on the acquisition of goods or services when those goods or services were acquired for use partly for making taxable supplies and are thereafter supplied in the course of the enterprise.

title: What are two situations where it would generally not be advantageous for a person to register voluntarily for VAT?
text: It will generally not be advantageous to register voluntarily where there are very few taxable expenses on which input tax can be deducted (for example, when the main enterprise expense is salaries and wages), or where most of the supplies are made to final consumers who are not registered for VAT.

title: Are there cases where a tax invoice is not required to be issued?
text: Yes, in certain cases, a tax invoice is not required to be issued, and there are also some special rules which apply in other cases.

title: What options are available if an indirect export is subject to VAT at the standard rate?
text: If an indirect export is subject to VAT at the standard rate, the qualifying purchaser may claim a refund from the VAT Refund Administrator (Pty) Ltd (the VRA) as set out in the Export Regulations. Alternatively, the vendor may elect to zero-rate the supply of the movable goods, subject to certain requirements set out in the Export Regulations.

title: Why must financial service providers apportion VAT incurred on goods or services acquired?
text: Financial service providers must apportion any VAT incurred (including notional input tax) on goods or services acquired which cannot be directly attributed wholly for the purposes of consumption, use or supply in the course of making taxable supplies, because they make both taxable and exempt supplies.

title: What are the three ways a tax dispute with SARS can be dealt with after an appeal has been noted?
text: A tax dispute with SARS may be dealt with by: (1) ADR, which is intended to be used where you and SARS agree to attempt to resolve a particular dispute outside of court; (2) the Tax Board, which has jurisdiction in respect of those matters where the total amount in dispute does not exceed R1 million; or (3) the Tax Court, even where the total amount in dispute is less than R1 million, for example where there is a legal principle in dispute.

title: What does section 10(29) now provide regarding the consideration for the deemed supply under section 18D(2)?
text: Section 10(29) now provides that the consideration for the deemed supply under section 18D(2) is equal to the adjusted cost to the vendor of the fixed property (including the value of the land) and not merely the adjusted cost of the construction, extension or improvements made.

title: What is the formula used to calculate the adjustment amount when goods or services change from private to taxable use?
text: The adjustment is calculated by applying the tax fraction to the lesser of the adjusted cost (including VAT), or the OMV of the relevant goods or services.

title: What document must a vendor possess to purchase goods at the zero rate?
text: The vendor must be in possession of a Notice of Registration indicating the vendor's entitlement to acquire the goods at the zero rate. With the implementation of the single registration process, the VAT103 was replaced with the new Notice of Registration which is a standard notice of registration across all tax types (except Customs and Excise).

title: Why is the computer software subject to apportionment in this example?
text: The software is subject to apportionment because it is used by the bank partly in the course of making taxable supplies and partly for making exempt supplies.

title: What happens if a dwelling is sold during the period it is temporarily applied for exempt supplies?
text: If the dwelling is sold during the period that it is temporarily applied for exempt supplies, the sale will be a taxable supply under section 7(1)(a).

title: What is the deadline for vendors supplying valuable metal under the domestic reverse charge to re-validate their VAT registration with SARS?
text: Vendors supplying valuable metal under the domestic reverse charge must re-validate their VAT registration with SARS within the earlier of 21 business days of the commencement date of the DRC Regulations (that is, within 21 business days of 1 July 2022) or the date that a supply subject to the domestic reverse charge is made.

title: Which act regulates the electronic transmission and retention of documents?
text: The electronic transmission and retention of documents is regulated by the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act 25 of 2002 (ECT Act).

title: What is the input tax adjustment amount that the vendor would be entitled to deduct in field 18 of the VAT201 return based on the 2022 financial statements?
How does the input tax treatment differ for second-hand fixed property acquired after 9 January 2012?
text: The limitation of the input tax deduction to transfer duty payable does not apply. However, the input tax may only be deducted if the fixed property is transferred to the recipient vendor by registration in the Deeds Registry (where applicable) and to the extent that payment towards the purchase price for the property has been made to the seller.

title: What special consideration applies to the deduction of notional input tax for second-hand goods acquired under a non-taxable supply?
text: Vendors may only deduct the notional input tax to the extent that payment of the consideration for the supply has been made by the vendor, regardless of whether that vendor is registered on the invoice or the payments basis.

title: How is output tax calculated for betting services?
text: Output tax is calculated by applying the tax fraction to the gross amount of the bet received.

title: What are the five different tax period categories for VAT vendors?
text: The five different tax period categories are: Category A – two-monthly (ending at the end of every odd month); Category B – two-monthly (ending at the end of every even month); Category C – monthly (for taxable supplies greater than R30 million in any consecutive period of 12 months); Category D – six-monthly (for certain farmers and micro-businesses with taxable supplies less than R1.5 million in any consecutive period of 12 months); and Category E – annually (only in exceptional circumstances for connected persons with only one transaction per consecutive period of 12 months).

title: What VAT rate applies to services supplied in connection with intellectual property rights used in the Republic?
text: All services supplied in connection with intellectual property rights used in the Republic must be standard rated.

title: Which educational institutions are exempt from VAT for supplying educational services?
text: Educational services supplied by State schools, schools registered under the South African Schools Act, further education and training (FET) institutions registered under the Further Education and Training Act, universities, universities of technologies, colleges and other institutions providing higher education registered under the Higher Education Act, and approved PBOs supplying adult basic education and training (ABET), education and training of religious or social workers, education and training of persons with permanent physical or mental impairment, or bridging courses to indigent persons are exempt from VAT.

title: What VAT adjustment is the vendor entitled to deduct if they used a 72% apportionment percentage during the year instead of the calculated 75%?
text: The vendor is entitled to deduct an input tax adjustment of R1 363.50, calculated as: R45 450 (R303 000 × 15%) × 3% = R1 363.50, representing the shortfall between the 72% used and the 75% calculated percentage.

title: What is the relationship between consideration, value, and VAT according to the formulas provided?
text: According to the formulas provided: VAT = consideration – value, or alternatively, Consideration = value + VAT.

title: When does a vendor end up with a VAT refund instead of a payment due to SARS?
text: A refund situation occurs when a vendor incurs more VAT on expenses than the VAT due on any taxable supplies made during the tax period, or when a vendor has mainly zero-rated supplies, such as an exporter or a business which sells only fresh fruit and vegetables.

title: What types of services are given as examples of concessive supplies?
text: Examples of concessive supplies include office and car rentals and on-going contracts for maintenance, management or cleaning services.

title: How is the sale of shares in a share block company treated for VAT purposes?
text: For VAT purposes, a share in a share block company is considered to be fixed property which falls within the ambit of 'goods' as defined. The sale of shares in a share block company is therefore a supply of goods and a supply of fixed property for VAT purposes.

title: What do the time and value of supply rules determine?
text: These rules determine when a transaction has occurred for VAT purposes and the value of a transaction on which VAT must be calculated and subsequently accounted for in a particular tax period.

title: Under what circumstance will no USP be imposed on a vendor?
text: No USP will be imposed if the vendor can prove that the understatement was as a result of a bona fide inadvertent error.

title: What is required when applying for a class ruling regarding affected class members?
text: A list of the affected class members, including their VAT registration numbers (where applicable), is required.

title: What does the definition of 'residue' specifically relate to under the amended DRC Regulations?
text: The definition of 'residue' is specifically in relation to mining operations carried on by a holder of a mining right or a person contracted to the holder and does not include the general concept of residue.

title: When can a deduction for input tax be made in relation to the tax period?
text: A deduction is only allowed in the tax period that the relevant prescribed or acceptable documentary proof (such as a tax invoice, debit note, credit note, bill of entry or other prescribed or acceptable documentary proof) is obtained.

title: What conditions must be met for a supplier to apply the zero rate on movable goods delivered to a vendor in the RSA for further processing?
text: The supplier may apply the zero rate when movable goods are supplied to a qualifying purchaser but delivered to another vendor in the RSA for further work such as processing, repair, improvement, manufacture, assembly or alteration. The vendor carrying out the further work must ensure the movable goods are delivered to a designated harbour or airport once the work is completed, obtain relevant export documentation, and the supplier must obtain and retain additional documents from the vendor responsible for carrying out the further work as listed in Part Two – Section A of the Export Regulations.

title: What is the prescribed period within which a vendor on the payments basis must notify SARS of ceasing to qualify for that basis?
text: A vendor that is registered on the payments basis is required to notify SARS in writing within a prescribed period of 21 days of ceasing to qualify for the payments basis.

title: What was the purpose of Section 18B and when did it come into effect?
text: Section 18B was introduced with effect from 10 January 2012 to provide temporary relief for property developers when they temporarily change the use of properties held as stock for resale (taxable supplies) by letting them as dwellings (exempt supplies) to tenants. The relief was in the form of a suspension of the liability to declare output tax on the change in use adjustment in certain instances when trading stock in the form of newly developed dwellings were temporarily let to tenants whilst being marketed for sale.

title: What is the time of supply for the adjustment when a developer temporarily applies a dwelling for exempt supplies?
text: The time of supply for the adjustment is the earlier of the date that the agreement for the letting and hiring of the accommodation in a dwelling comes into effect or the date on which the dwelling is occupied.

title: What is the threshold amount mentioned for voluntary registration of taxable supplies?
text: According to section 2.3.6, taxable supplies expected to exceed R50 000 can be voluntarily registered for VAT.

title: When can the zero rate not apply to second-hand goods exports?
text: The zero rate cannot apply where second-hand goods are acquired by the supplier and subsequently exported after notional input tax has been deducted on the acquisition thereof. In such a case, the supplier must levy VAT equal to the notional input tax originally deducted on the acquisition of the goods being exported.

title: In Example 37, what VAT amount is payable by Z for the imported legal services?
text: The VAT payable is R1 350, calculated as R9 000 (the OMV) × 15%.

title: Who qualifies as a 'qualifying purchaser' for indirect exports?
text: A qualifying purchaser includes a non-resident, a tourist, a foreign enterprise, a foreign diplomat, a person who acquires and sells goods on a flash title basis, certain international organisations or organisations which are similar to an association not for gain or a welfare organisation which are registered as such in an export country.

title: Does electing to use a cut-off date under the 10-day rule affect the due date for submitting the return?
text: No, the election by the vendor to use a cut-off date allowed under the 10-day rule does not affect the due date for submitting the return, which remains the 25th day (or the last business day in the case of eFiling users) after the end of the month covered by that tax period.

title: Can a purchaser deduct VAT on modifications made before the supply of a motor car?
text: No, VAT incurred on modifications made before the supply of the motor car cannot be deducted by the purchaser.

title: In the example provided, what was the shortfall amount for a vendor who did not file a return but should have paid VAT?
text: The shortfall was R90 000, which is the difference between the R90 000 that should have been paid and zero, since no return was filed.

title: What is required before SARS can completely deregister a vendor from the VAT register?
text: SARS cannot completely deregister a vendor until all outstanding liabilities or obligations incurred under the VAT Act have been settled or resolved, such as outstanding returns for past tax periods or any VAT payments that are owed.

title: What adjustment must a developer make under Section 18D and when is the time of supply?
text: Under Section 18D, the developer must make an output tax adjustment and declare output tax on the adjusted cost instead of the open market value (OMV) as would normally be the case under section 18(1) before these amendments. The time of supply for the adjustment is the earlier of the date that the agreement for the letting and hiring of the accommodation in a dwelling comes into effect or date on which the dwelling is occupied.

title: What is the time limit for claiming a refund in a VAT return, and are there any other conditions that must be met?
text: A refund must be claimed in a VAT return submitted within five years from the date that the submission of such VAT return became due. Additionally, the vendor must not owe any amounts relating to other taxes administered by the Commissioner.

title: What are the requirements for submitting a request for remission of interest as of 14 October 2016?
text: As of 14 October 2016, a request for remission of interest may only be made via eFiling or at a SARS branch once you have paid any tax which is due in that regard. The remission of interest will only be considered where substantive reasons are given for a late payment, meaning properly motivated mitigating circumstances must be submitted for consideration together with any evidence which supports your case.

title: How can you confirm if your VAT registration has been processed?
text: You can confirm if your registration has been processed by entering your details under 'VAT vendor search' on the SARS website at www.sars.gov.za eFiling by selecting 'VAT Vendor Search' in the drop-down box on the left hand side of the screen.

title: When was the building acquired and what was its purchase price inclusive of VAT?
text: The building was acquired in March 2021 for R1 368 000 (inclusive of VAT).

title: What types of vendors are eligible for Category E (annual tax period)?
text: Category E is for vendors who want their tax periods to align with their 'year of assessment' as defined in section 1(1) of the Income Tax Act. The vendor must be a company or a trust fund and must submit a written application in the prescribed form for registration under this category.

title: Under what circumstances may SARS make a determination of an amount of tax payable under section 31 of the VAT Act?
text: SARS may make a determination under section 31 where, for example, a person who is not registered as a vendor supplies goods or services and represents that tax is charged on the supply, or where a vendor charges an incorrect amount of tax on a supply.

title: What must a vendor do if they subsequently receive payment on a debt that was written off as irrecoverable?
text: The vendor must account for output tax on that payment in the tax period in which it is received.

title: In Example 13, what VAT rate must Airline C levy on the domestic passenger flight from Cape Town to Johannesburg?
text: Airline C must levy VAT at the standard rate on the domestic passenger flight from Cape Town to Johannesburg, as Airline C is not contractually supplying the domestic leg of an international passenger flight.

title: What types of official publications are considered to define a practice generally prevailing under the TA Act?
text: Official publications that define a practice generally prevailing include Binding General Rulings, Interpretation Notes, Practice Notes, and Public notices.

title: Can a business that only makes exempt supplies register as a vendor?
text: No. A business that only makes exempt supplies does not carry on an "enterprise" for VAT purposes and is therefore unable to register as a vendor irrespective of the number or value of the supplies made.

title: Are employees earning a salary or wage from their employer required to register for VAT?
text: No. If you are an employee earning a salary or wage from your employer (excluding an independent contractor), you are not regarded as carrying on an enterprise. However, a non-executive director of a company is considered to be an independent contractor and not an employee.

title: What does Proviso (ix) to the definition of 'enterprise' in section 1(1) provide, and from when?
text: Proviso (ix) to the definition of 'enterprise' in section 1(1) provides the necessary certainty on the 'enterprise' question from 1 April 2021.

title: What types of supplies are made from the building owned by Company J in Example 23?
text: The building is used for mixed purposes with 4 shops on the ground floor (taxable supplies) and 2 large residential apartments on the top floor (exempt supplies).

title: Under what condition is the sale of manufactured chairs by the church considered an exempt supply?
text: The sale of manufactured chairs is exempt if at least 80% of the value of the materials used in making the chairs consists of donated goods and services.

title: What is an audit in the context of VAT?
text: An audit is generally a detailed check on the correctness of VAT returns submitted and payments made by a vendor. It will normally involve an examination of multiple periods, and relevant materials may be obtained from third parties as well as from the vendor.

title: What is the total VAT received by SARS from all vendors in this supply chain?
text: The total VAT received by SARS is R12,00, consisting of R6,00 from the Manufacturer, R4,50 from the Stationery Distributor, and R1,50 from the Supermarket.

title: Are goods temporarily admitted for processing or repair exempt from VAT?
text: Yes, goods temporarily admitted for processing, repair, cleaning, reconditioning or for the manufacture of goods exclusively for export are exempt from VAT according to Schedule 1 to the VAT Act.

title: What conditions must be met for donated goods from non-residents to qualify for VAT exemption?
text: Goods donated by a non-resident to an association not for gain, public authority or municipality are exempt from VAT if they are not for resale and are imported for use exclusively for educational or welfare purposes, educational, medical or scientific research, or for issue to indigent persons at no charge.

title: When did Section 18B relief period end and what replaced it?
text: Section 18B relief period ceased on 1 January 2018. A more permanent relief mechanism in the form of section 18D (together with associated provisions sections 9(13), 10(29) and 16(3)(o)) has now been introduced to deal with temporary letting.

title: When was the Domestic Reverse Charge Regulations relating to gold and valuable metals published?
text: The Domestic Reverse Charge Regulations relating to certain transactions involving gold and other valuable metals was published as Notice 2140 in GG 46512 dated 8 June 2022.

title: What is an example of a deemed supply on which a vendor must account for output tax at the standard rate?
text: Examples of deemed supplies on which a vendor has to account for output tax at the standard rate include trading stock taken out of the business for private use, certain fringe benefits provided to staff, assets retained upon ceasing to carry on an enterprise, short-term insurance claims that have been paid in connection with the enterprise, receipt of payments from government by designated entities for taxable supplies, betting and other gambling transactions, and change in use adjustments.

title: Which authority is competent to provide advice on whether Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) systems meet the technical specifications of the ECT Act?
text: The National Department of Communications is the competent authority to approach for advice on whether EDI systems or other electronic communications meet the technical specifications of the ECT Act, as it regulates the ECT Act. SARS is not in a position to issue rulings or provide advice on this matter.

title: What amendment was made to the definition of 'derivative' in section 2(2)(iiiA), and what is its effective date?
text: A textual amendment was made to update the definition of the term 'derivative' in section 2(2)(iiiA) so that it now aligns to International Financial Reporting Standard 9 (IFRS 9) issued by the International Accounting Standards Board. This amendment has retrospective effect, applicable from 1 January 2024.

title: What is the input tax amount that can be deducted on the rugby kit acquired by ABC Rugby Club?
text: The input tax on the rugby kit acquired is R1 304, calculated as 15/115 × R10 000. However, ABC Rugby Club can only deduct input tax on the OMV (Open Market Value) of the rugby kit acquired, as money is not regarded as either goods or services for VAT purposes.

title: What is the deemed supply in Example 10 and when was the indemnity payment received?
text: The deemed supply is an insurance indemnity payment of R57,000 received on 1 February 2023. The payment was made by P's insurer to compensate for damages to P's delivery van that was involved in an accident on 5 January 2023.

title: Are there any exceptions to the invoice or payments basis for VAT accounting?
text: Yes, fixed property transactions are accounted for to the extent of payment made towards the purchase price irrespective of whether a vendor is on the invoice or payments basis.

title: What must a vendor do when they receive payment on a debt that was previously written off as irrecoverable?
text: The vendor must account for output tax on that payment in the tax period in which it is received.

title: What must vendors do if they want to apply self-invoicing procedures but cannot comply with BGR 15 requirements?
text: Vendors that want to apply self-invoicing procedures but are unable to comply with the requirements stipulated in BGR 15 will have to obtain written authorisation from SARS before they will be allowed to apply this method of invoicing or creating credit and debit notes.

title: What form must a vendor complete and submit to request registration on the payments basis under section 15(2)?
text: A vendor must complete and submit Form VAT117 to request registration on the payments basis under section 15(2).

title: Who is authorized to issue official publications under the TA Act?
text: Official publications are issued by either a senior SARS official who is specifically designated as such by the Commissioner or by the Commissioner him or herself.

title: What is the definition of 'enterprise' for VAT registration purposes in South Africa?
text: Enterprise is defined in section 1(1) to include any activity carried on continuously or regularly by any person in South Africa or partly in South Africa, resulting in income being earned from the supply of goods or services (that is supplied for a 'consideration') to another person, whether for profit or not.

title: What types of hospitality expenses are generally not deductible for input tax purposes?
text: Input tax relating to entertainment expenses is generally not deductible. This includes catering services, furniture, equipment and utensils used in kitchens, canteens and dining rooms; beverages, meals, entertainment shows, amusements or other hospitality supplied to customers and clients at product launches and promotional events; and capital goods such as hospitality boxes, holiday houses, yachts and private aircraft.

title: In Example 24, what input tax amount did H deduct when purchasing the computer in February 2016?
text: H deducted input tax of R700 in the February 2016 VAT return.

title: When is the supply of goods under an Instalment Credit Agreement (ICA) deemed to take place?
text: The supply of goods under an ICA is deemed to take place at the earlier of the time the goods are delivered, or any payment of the consideration is received by the supplier in respect of the supply.

title: What right does a vendor have when the Commissioner authorises a jeopardy assessment?
text: When the Commissioner authorises a jeopardy assessment, a vendor has the right to approach a High Court for review on the basis that the assessment is excessive or that there is no justification for the jeopardy assessment. In such a review application, SARS bears the burden of proving that the making of the assessment was reasonable in the circumstances.

title: In what currency must a tax invoice be issued, and are there any exceptions?
text: A tax invoice must be in South African currency, except for a zero-rated supply (for example, goods exported) or in the case of certain supplies of electronic services by non-resident suppliers.

title: In which field on the VAT return is the VAT on the difference of R35 000 × 15/115 = R4 565,22 declared?
text: Field 12 on the VAT return (output tax).

title: Who is responsible for accounting for VAT on supplies of valuable metal under the DRC?
text: Unlike conventional VAT rules, the DRC Regulations shifts the responsibility of accounting for VAT on the supply of valuable metal to the recipient instead of the supplier, subject to certain requirements.

title: How should expenses be categorized in an apportionment application?
text: Expenses must be categorized into three groups: those directly attributed to making taxable supplies, those directly attributed to making exempt supplies or for other non-taxable purposes, and mixed expenses that are subject to the requested apportionment method.

title: What are examples of domestic goods or services included in the VAT Act definition for commercial accommodation establishments?
text: According to the VAT Act, domestic goods or services include cleaning and maintenance, electricity, gas, air conditioning or heating, a telephone, television set, radio or other similar article, furniture and other fittings, meals, laundry, nursing services, and water.

title: What is the definition of an assessment under the TA Act?
text: An assessment is defined in the TA Act as the determination of the amount of a tax liability or refund either through a vendor's self-assessment or an assessment by SARS (administrative assessment).

title: Within what timeframe must a vendor issue a tax invoice to the recipient?
text: A vendor must issue a tax invoice to the recipient within 21 days of the supply having been made for which the consideration for the supply exceeds R50.

title: Which vehicles are excluded from the definition of 'motor car' for VAT purposes?
text: The term 'motor car' excludes: goods transportation trucks, single cab light and heavy delivery vehicles, motor-cycles, caravans, ambulances, game viewing vehicles and hearses, vehicles capable of accommodating more than 16 persons (such as buses), vehicles with an unladen mass of 3,500 kg or more, special purpose vehicles constructed for purposes other than carrying passengers, and equipment such as bulldozers, graders, hysters, harvesters and tractors.

title: What is the key characteristic of exempt and out of scope supplies in terms of VAT and input tax?
text: Exempt and out of scope supplies are supplies of goods or services where no VAT is levied and input tax may not be deducted on the VAT incurred to make these supplies.

title: What is GN 316 in GG 37580 of 2 May 2014 referred to as?
text: The Export Regulations

title: How many VAT returns must be submitted under the branch or division arrangement?
text: Two VAT returns must be submitted: the main vendor must submit a VAT return to account for its own VAT, and a separate VAT return must be submitted for the branch or division to account for the VAT of all the other entities.

title: What is the deadline for vendors to submit their VAT201 return and payment to SARS?
text: Vendors must submit their VAT201 return and payment to SARS by no later than the 25th day of the first calendar month after the end of the tax period concerned. However, vendors registered to submit their returns electronically have until the last business day of that calendar month to submit their VAT201 together with the payment.

title: What is the fee amount in rands for which VAT is not payable?
text: The fee amount is R200 000.
