title: If only 70% of chair materials are donated wood and 30% consists of purchased taxable inputs, what is the VAT treatment and what input tax can be deducted?
text: The supply would be taxable at the standard rate. Input tax could be deducted on the goods or services from vendors which were acquired to manufacture and sell the chairs, but not on the wood which was received as a donation.

title: What constitutes a donation according to the provided definition?
text: A donation is a gratuitous payment voluntarily made to any association not for gain for the carrying on or carrying out of the purposes of that association, in respect of which no identifiable direct valuable benefit arises or may arise in the form of a supply of goods or services to the person making that payment. It includes not only cash payments, but also the value of goods or services donated.

title: What is the turnover threshold that requires a vendor to notify SARS for Category C tax period registration?
text: If turnover exceeds R30 million in any consecutive period of 12 months subsequent to registration for VAT, the vendor is required to notify SARS to amend their registration to a Category C tax period within 21 days of becoming liable to register.

title: Under what circumstances does the standard rate apply to services supplied to non-residents in connection with fixed property?
text: The standard rate applies to services supplied to non-residents in connection with fixed property (land and improvements) situated in the Republic when the services are supplied directly.

title: What is the taxable value of an imported service according to VAT 404?
text: The taxable value of the supply is the greater of the consideration or the OMV (Open Market Value).

title: When can a vendor deduct the full amount of VAT on goods or services acquired for making taxable supplies?
text: A vendor can deduct the full amount of VAT on goods or services acquired or imported for the purposes of making taxable supplies when the goods or services are acquired exclusively for taxable supplies.

title: What does the term 'Person' refer to in VAT legislation?
text: Person refers to the entity which is liable for VAT registration and includes sole proprietors (natural persons), companies/CCs, partnerships/joint ventures, deceased/insolvent estates, trusts, incorporated bodies of persons (such as entities established under their own enabling Act of Parliament), unincorporated bodies of persons (such as clubs, societies or associations with their own constitution), municipalities, and public authorities.

title: What is the email address for VAT refund inquiries from Namibia?
text: namibia@taxrefunds.co.za

title: How is the time of supply determined for successive supplies under a rental agreement?
text: The time of supply for each successive supply is deemed to take place on the earlier of the date when payment is due or is received.

title: What is the minimum threshold for voluntary VAT registration for welfare organisations?
text: The minimum threshold of R50 000 for voluntary registration is not applicable to the welfare activities of a welfare organisation. This means a welfare organisation is entitled to register for VAT purposes and deduct VAT incurred on goods or services acquired in carrying out its welfare activities, even if it does not charge any consideration.

title: What does VAT stand for and what type of tax is it?
text: VAT stands for value-added tax. It is an indirect tax based on consumption in the economy.

title: When may input tax be deducted for supplies of valuable metal under the DRC?
text: Input tax may only be deducted in respect of the supply of valuable metal if the recipient has accounted for and paid the VAT in respect of such supply on a VAT201 return. If the documents are in order, the recipient may deduct the input tax in the same tax period in which the output tax is declared.

title: What is the consequence if an agent exporting gold on behalf of a principal is not in possession of the required documentation?
text: If the agent is not in possession of the required documentation, the agent will be liable to account for output tax at the standard rate on the supply of the gold.

title: What types of plant products are eligible for the VAT concession?
text: Plants eligible for the VAT concession include trees, bulbs, roots, cuttings or similar plant products used for cultivation.

title: What format change was made to the VAT201 return on eFiling from December 2020?
text: From December 2020, the VAT201 return on eFiling has been converted from Adobe Flex to HTML5 format for better user experience and compatibility.

title: How is the private company administering the VAT refund mechanism appointed?
text: The private company is appointed by SARS on a tender basis

title: What is the date of assessment when a VAT return is submitted to SARS?
text: The date of assessment is the date when the VAT return is submitted to SARS. This is considered an original self-assessment under the TA Act.

title: What happens to assets when a vendor deregisters?
text: When a vendor deregisters as a vendor, certain goods or rights forming part of the enterprise's assets are deemed to be supplied in the course of the vendor's enterprise, immediately before the person ceased to be a vendor, irrespective of when those assets may have been acquired, subject to certain exceptions.

title: What is a cartage contractor and what are the requirements for them?
text: A cartage contractor is a person whose activities include the transportation of goods and includes couriers and freight forwarders. The transportation of goods does not have to be the main activity of the cartage contractor and the cartage contractor is also not required to be a registered vendor. The cartage contractor must invoice the supplier, being the person who is liable for payment of the full costs of the delivery services rendered.

title: What is the prescribed period within which a vendor must lodge an objection to an assessment?
text: An objection must be lodged within 80 business days after the date of the assessment or decision or reasons for the assessment were furnished by the Commissioner. A vendor may apply for an extension of this period.

title: What is the total amount including VAT?
When do the general time of supply rules apply for connected persons?
text: The general time of supply rules apply when an invoice is issued or payment is received on or before the date that a return was submitted (covering the tax period in which the goods or services are deemed to be supplied) or the last day for submitting a return for that tax period, or when the consideration cannot be determined at the time the supply is deemed to be made to a connected person who is entitled to deduct input tax in full.

title: What types of entities are included in the definition of 'person' for VAT purposes?
text: The term 'person' includes individuals, partnerships and bodies of persons, private and public companies, share block companies and close corporations (CCs), public authorities and municipalities, associations not for gain such as clubs and welfare organisations, insolvent and deceased estates, and trust funds.

title: What is the general rule for services supplied to non-residents before the standard rate exceptions are applied?
text: The supply of certain services to a non-resident may qualify for the zero rate under section 11(2)(l) provided certain conditions are met.

title: What change was made to the definition of 'enterprise' regarding FDFP activities?
text: The definition of 'enterprise' was amended to include reference to the activities of an implementing agency in respect of the FDFP activities rather than to regard the FDFP as a separate person conducting the activities, as was the case before the amendment. This amendment came into effect from 1 April 2020.

title: Under what conditions can input tax be deducted on the acquisition of a hearse or game viewing vehicle?
text: Input tax on the acquisition of hearses and game viewing vehicles may be deducted where the vehicle is used exclusively for making taxable supplies. Additionally, a vendor is entitled to an input tax deduction on the acquisition of a motor car if it is acquired so that it can be permanently converted into a game viewing vehicle or hearse if that type of vehicle is required for use in the enterprise.

title: How long does BB hold ownership of the goods supplied by BF?
text: The ownership of the goods supplied by BF to BB vests in BB for only a moment before the same goods are subsequently sold by BB to its various customers.

title: What are the eligibility criteria for Category D (six-monthly tax period)?
text: Category D is for vendors who are: (1) micro businesses under the Income Tax Act and have made a written application to be placed under Category D; or (2) who solely carry on a farming, pastoral or agricultural enterprise and whose total turnover has not exceeded R1,5 million per consecutive period of 12 months and is not likely to exceed that amount in the next consecutive 12 months; or (3) who solely carry on a farming, pastoral or agricultural enterprise as a separate enterprise, branch or division of a vendor or an association not for gain, whose total turnover has not exceeded R1,5 million in the past 12 months and is not likely to exceed that amount in the next 12 months.

title: What are the two types of administrative non-compliance penalties mentioned in the VAT 404 Guide?
text: The two types of administrative non-compliance penalties are fixed-amount penalties and percentage-based penalties. Fixed-amount penalties are imposed when a vendor does not comply with an obligation contained in a public notice issued by the Commissioner, while percentage-based penalties are imposed in line with the procedure contained in section 213 of the TA Act.

title: How is VAT calculated upon goods being cleared for home consumption?
text: VAT is calculated on the Assessed Total Value (ATV), which includes the purchase price/customs value, customs duty (at 25% of CV), and a 10% upliftment (at 10% of CV). VAT is then applied at 15% to this ATV.

title: Under what circumstances will approval for self-invoicing procedures be granted?
text: Approval for self-invoicing procedures will only be granted in the case of those industries and transactions where an effective self-invoicing system has traditionally been followed in the past. Approval will not be granted if the purpose is merely to facilitate the obtaining of a tax invoice, credit or debit note by the recipient.

title: What is the compulsory VAT registration threshold for intermediaries facilitating electronic services supplies?
text: An intermediary will be required to register and account for VAT if the value of their taxable supplies (including any deemed supplies) exceeds the compulsory threshold of R1 million.

title: In Example 32, why is the non-resident M from Botswana not entitled to a refund of the R1 500 VAT charged on the painting?
text: M is not entitled to a refund of the R1 500 VAT charged because the VAT levied at the standard rate is equal to the amount of notional input tax deducted by DEF on the acquisition of the second-hand painting.

title: What functions does the eFiling system provide to help vendors manage their VAT payments?
text: The eFiling system provides a VAT payment allocation function and a VAT Statement of Account (VATSA). The VAT payment allocation function enables vendors to allocate payments, to reallocate payments and to locate missing payments, which can be performed by vendors without requiring any intervention from SARS.

title: What will apply to DRC supplies after the regulations change?
text: The DRC Regulations will no longer apply to these supplies and the normal VAT rules will apply.

title: What was the outcome regarding VAT registration liability in the Wenco case?
text: The non-resident person (a Canadian company) was found to be liable for VAT registration in regard to the supplies that it made in the Republic, rather than any separate 'branch'.

title: Where can you find the guides, interpretation notes, rulings, forms, returns and tables referenced in the VAT guide?
text: All guides, interpretation notes, rulings, forms, returns and tables are available on the SARS website or via e-Filing at www.sarsefiling.co.za (guides only), and they are the latest versions unless indicated otherwise.

title: In Example 18, why can ABC Bank deduct the full amount of VAT charged on the building purchase?
text: ABC Bank can deduct the full amount of VAT charged because the building was acquired exclusively for making taxable supplies (letting of property as offices). Although the bank makes both taxable and exempt supplies, the expense is wholly attributable to making taxable supplies, so the full VAT of R435 000 can be deducted as input tax.

title: What is a 'qualifying purchaser' (QP1) under the flash title sales amendment?
text: A qualifying purchaser (QP1) is a non-resident non-registered vendor that acquires goods from a vendor and merely takes ownership of the goods in South Africa for a moment in time, before supplying those goods to another qualifying purchaser under a 'flash title' as defined in the Export Regulation.

title: What is the minimum adjusted cost threshold below which no adjustment is applicable?
text: No adjustment is applicable where the adjusted cost is less than R40 000 (excluding VAT).

title: What are the two methods a supplier can use to apply the zero rate for direct exports?
text: A supplier can either physically deliver the goods to the recipient, the recipient's duly appointed agent or the recipient's customer at an address in an export country ('deliver'), or obtain the services of a cartage contractor who is contractually obliged to deliver the goods on behalf of the supplier to the recipient, the recipient's duly appointed agent or the recipient's customer at an address in an export country ('consign').

title: What are the conditions for passenger transport by road or rail to be exempt from VAT?
text: The supply of passenger transport by road or rail is exempt from VAT if the transportation is between two places within the RSA, of fare-paying passengers and their baggage or belongings.

title: What refund method is used for qualifying purchasers from Botswana, Namibia and Swaziland?
text: Qualifying purchasers from Botswana, Namibia and Swaziland (eSwatini) are not issued with a pre-paid debit card. Instead, the refund will be transferred via EFT to the qualifying purchaser's designated bank account.

title: What are the main benefits of alternative dispute resolution?
text: ADR is a less formal, more cost-effective and speedier method of dispute resolution.

title: What types of items are typically included in 'c' in the turnover-based apportionment formula?
text: Items typically included in 'c' are dividends and statutory fines (if any), which represent other amounts of income not included in 'a' or 'b' that were received or accrued during the period.

title: When was GN 1594 published in the Government Gazette?
text: GN 1594 was published in GG 45324 dated 10 December 2021.

title: What is required on the Notice of Registration for vendors conducting farming enterprises?
text: It is a requirement that a statement must appear on the Notice of Registration indicating that certain farming inputs may be purchased at the zero rate, subject to certain conditions.

title: What contact details must be provided in a VAT ruling application?
text: The applicant must provide contact details such as phone or e-mail, and if applicable, the contact details of the applicant's representative.

title: What standard must be applied when deciding if the turnover-based method is appropriate for apportionment?
text: A common-sense approach which would be applied by a reasonable person must be applied. The method must achieve a 'fair and reasonable' result which is a proper reflection of the manner in which the vendor's resources (business inputs) are applied for making taxable and non-taxable supplies respectively.

title: What does Section 25(5) of the TA Act allow SARS to request from a person?
text: Section 25(5) of the TA Act allows SARS to request a person to submit an amended return for the correction of an undisputed error made in a return, before SARS issuing an original assessment.

title: In Example 44, what is the vendor seeking to confirm regarding the supply of marketing services to a non-resident?
text: The vendor wants to confirm whether VAT may be levied at the zero rate under section 11(2)(l).

title: What information must be included in a signed VAT264 declaration for second-hand goods?
text: A signed VAT264 declaration must include the following information: name, address and ID no. of the supplier (ID no. of the representative person if it is a company or CC); quantity or volume of goods; full description of the goods; consideration for the supply; proof of payment (including the date of payment); and declaration by the supplier stating that the supply is not a taxable supply.

title: What was the main purpose of introducing VAT rulings in 1991?
text: The main purpose of introducing VAT rulings was to provide certainty to vendors regarding the VAT implications of transactions effected in the course of conducting an enterprise, and to provide an assurance to the applicant that the ruling could be relied upon until withdrawn by the Commissioner, provided certain conditions were met.

title: What is the compulsory VAT registration threshold based on contractual obligations?
text: If you have a contractual obligation in writing to make taxable supplies in excess of R1 million within a 12-month period, you will be liable to register at the commencement of the first month in which the said 12-month period begins.

title: How much input tax did C deduct in respect of the computer system in the April 2022 tax period?
text: C deducted input tax of R5 305 (14 / 114 × R54 000 × 80%) in respect of the computer system acquired in April 2022.

title: What is the purpose of the new provision regarding pooling arrangements in VAT treatment?
text: The new provision is intended to deal with the VAT treatment of pooling arrangements established as a manner of complying with the provisions of legislation, regulations or rules of a professional body. It allows entities like medical pools to be deemed separate from their members and registered as vendors, enabling the pool to account for the VAT of members rather than registering each individual practitioner separately.

title: Can a vendor deduct VAT on goods or services acquired for private purposes?
text: No. No VAT may be deducted when goods or services are acquired for private purposes, exempt supplies or other non-taxable purposes.

title: What is the Payment Reference Number (PRN) and how is it used?
text: A unique 19 digit Payment Reference Number (PRN) is pre-populated on the VAT201 by SARS. You must use this PRN when making your VAT payment to SARS. Each VAT201 return requested on eFiling from SARS will have its own unique PRN which will be used to track individual payments and queries for that tax period only.

title: What does Chapter 4 of the TA Act set out regarding records?
text: Chapter 4 of the TA Act sets out the details of the person who must keep records, the general records that must be kept, the form in which these records must be kept (including electronic form) and the period for which they should be retained.

title: What is the purpose of using the USP table in VAT assessment?
text: The USP table is used to identify the highest applicable percentage to each shortfall in relation to each understatement in a return that best describes the facts of the case and the vendor's behaviour in respect of each understatement of that return.

title: To whom do the administrative duties mentioned in the guide apply?
text: The administrative duties apply to vendors, persons who have registered voluntarily for VAT, as well as persons who should have registered for VAT but who have not done so.

title: How can a vendor on the payments basis apply to change to the invoice basis?
text: A vendor that is registered on the payments basis may, at any time, submit a written application to SARS to use the invoice basis. In certain instances, a vendor may also elect to change the accounting basis by accessing the RAV form on efiling.

title: When must an apportionment of input tax be made?
text: An apportionment of input tax must be made if goods or services are acquired only partly for making taxable supplies.

title: When did the Tax Administration Act commence and what did it replace?
text: The Tax Administration Act (TA Act) was promulgated into law on 4 July 2012 and commenced on 1 October 2012. With its implementation, certain administrative provisions previously contained in the VAT Act were replaced by similar provisions contained in the TA Act.

title: What is a condition for zero-rating services supplied to non-residents under section 11(2)(l)?
text: A condition of the zero-rating under section 11(2)(l) is that the non-resident person (or any other person consuming the service) is not present in the RSA at the time the services are rendered.

title: How must non-vendors pay VAT on imported services and what documentation is required?
text: Non-vendors must complete and retain form VAT215 and ensure that payment is made via eFiling within 30 days of importing the services. Once the payment is successfully processed, eFiling will generate a receipt number which must be inserted in the relevant field on the VAT215 record.

title: What is the relationship between the Tax Administration Act and specific tax Acts like the VAT Act?
text: The Tax Administration Act regulates the administration of all tax Acts, and a specific tax Act must be read together with the TA Act. If there is an administrative provision in a tax Act and the TA Act is silent, the specific administrative provision in the tax Act applies. If the tax Act is silent about an administrative process, a right that a taxpayer has, or the power or authority of SARS, then the provision in the TA Act must be used. If there is any inconsistency between a tax Act and the TA Act, the tax Act will prevail.

title: How must transfers of taxable goods or services between separately registered enterprises, divisions or branches be treated for VAT purposes?
text: Any transfers of taxable goods or services between the separately registered enterprises, divisions or branches must be charged with VAT and accounted for on the relevant VAT201 return covering that period.

title: What are the two bases on which input tax can be calculated for VAT purposes?
text: The two bases are the invoice basis and the payments basis. On the invoice basis, you add VAT incurred on goods or services acquired for taxable purposes from all tax invoices received in the tax period, regardless of whether payment has been made. On the payments basis, you add VAT incurred on goods or services acquired for taxable purposes to the extent of actual payment made in the tax period to suppliers for current or past taxable supplies received.

title: What is the time limit for acquiring goods or services before the date of incorporation for input tax deduction purposes?
text: The goods or services must not have been acquired more than six months before the date of incorporation.

title: When must a vendor account for output tax if the purchase price for goods or services has not been paid?
text: A vendor must account for output tax to the extent that the purchase price for the goods or services has not yet been paid after 12 months from the end of the tax period in which such deduction was made. However, if the purchase price or a part thereof is only payable later under an agreement in writing, then the 12-month period will start running from the end of the month in which payment should have been made under that agreement.

title: What was the case reference for the Wenco International Mining Systems Ltd decision?
text: Wenco International Mining Systems Ltd and Another v CSARS (59922-2019) [2021] ZAGPPHC 70 (19 January 2021)

title: What dairy products are included in this list?
text: The dairy products included are milk, cultured milk, milk powder, and dairy powder blend.

title: What is the contact number for the SARS Service Centre and what are their operating hours?
text: You can contact the SARS Service Centre on 0800 00 7277 between 08h00 – 17h00 on weekdays.

title: What are your options if the ADR process is unsuccessful?
text: In the event of the ADR process being unsuccessful, the unresolved dispute may be referred to the Tax Board or the Tax Court by the appellant within 20 days of termination of the ADR proceedings, depending on the amount in dispute.

title: What does BGR 65 direct regarding credit notes from suppliers?
text: BGR 65 directs that the supplier is not required to issue a credit note, subject to certain conditions. Instead, to reduce over-declaration of output tax on standard-rated supplies, the supplier may make an adjustment to the output tax attributable for that tax period or deduct input tax, and must retain relevant records to substantiate the adjustment.

title: In Example 14(a), why were the calibration services supplied by CI subject to VAT at the zero rate?
text: The calibration services supplied by CI were subject to VAT at the zero rate because the machine would subsequently be exported to KM, and the services (including any incidental goods incorporated into the machine or consumed in the calibration process) were made directly in connection with movable goods that were being exported.

title: What are some specific activities that are included in the definition of 'enterprise' despite not meeting the general criteria?
text: The following activities are specifically included in the definition of 'enterprise': anything done in connection with the commencement or termination of an enterprise; the supply of goods or services by a public authority which the Minister is satisfied could be supplied by another person in carrying on a continuous business activity; the activities of a 'welfare organisation' for VAT purposes; the activities of a share block company where such share block company has been registered on a voluntary basis; the activities of an implementing agency carried on in the course of implementing, operating, administering or managing a foreign donor funded project (FDFP); and the supply of certain electronic services by non-resident suppliers.

title: Is the Tax Ombud's recommendation binding on SARS or the taxpayer?
text: No. The Tax Ombud can only make a recommendation on the resolution of the complaint, which is not binding on SARS or the taxpayer. Both the taxpayer and the relevant SARS department must be duly informed of the recommendation.

title: What penalties and interest are charged for late VAT payments?
text: Late payments of VAT attract a penalty of 10% of the outstanding tax. Interest is also charged at the prescribed rate on any late payments made after the month in which the payment for the tax period concerned was due, as well as any balance of taxes outstanding for past tax periods.

title: What is the time of supply for services supplied between connected persons?
text: The time of supply occurs at the time the services are performed.

title: When must output tax on fringe benefits be declared and paid for the first tax period covering March and April 2023?
text: Output tax on the fringe benefit for the first tax period covering March 2023 and April 2023 must be declared in Field 12 of the VAT201 return and paid on 25 May 2023.

title: What is the requirement for documents submitted with a VAT registration application?
text: It is very important that you submit the correct documents with your application to register. SARS will not accept any faxed or photocopied applications for registration. The requirement for a "recent" document refers to a document that is not more than three months old from the date of application for registration. A comprehensive list of documents that must be submitted can be found in the VAT-REG-02-G01 – Guide for Completion of VAT Registration Application Forms – External Guide and VAT-REG-02-G02 – VAT Registration Guide for Foreign Suppliers of Electronic Services.

title: What are Official Development Assistance Agreements (ODAAs) and what binds them to the Republic?
text: Official Development Assistance Agreements (ODAAs) are international agreements that are binding on the Republic under section 231(3) of the Constitution of the Republic of South Africa. These agreements normally provide that donated funds should only be used for specific, mutually agreed upon programmes and activities, and cannot be used to pay for any taxes imposed under South African Law.

title: What information must a creditor obtain in order to deduct notional input tax under section 20(8A)?
text: The creditor must obtain: the date on which the goods were repossessed or surrendered, the name of the supplier, a description of the goods, the consideration for the supply, and further particulars in the form and manner as the Commissioner may prescribe.

title: How is the VAT calculated on the indemnity payment, and what is the amount?
text: The VAT is calculated as the tax fraction of the amount received using the formula: R57,000 × 15/115 = R7,435. This amount must be accounted for as output tax in the February 2023 return.

title: What is the extended deadline for submitting VAT returns if using eFiling?
text: If you use eFiling to submit your return and make payment by electronic means (payment submitted on eFiling or effected by electronic funds transfer through internet banking), you have until the last business day of the month after the end of your tax period.

title: What is the consequence for BB of not being able to register for VAT?
text: Since BB may not register for VAT, the VAT incurred on expenses (R7,435 per month) cannot be deducted as input tax. Consequently, this VAT amount forms part of the business costs of running the crèche and after-school facility.

title: What is a foreign donor funded project (FDFP)?
text: A foreign donor funded project is a project established as a result of an international donor funding agreement to supply goods or services to beneficiaries, to which the South African government is a party. These international agreements are referred to as Official Development Assistance Agreements (ODAAs) and are binding on the Republic under section 231(3) of the Constitution of the Republic of South Africa.

title: What are the conditions under which a vendor's separately registered branch can be cancelled?
text: A vendor's separately registered enterprises/divisions/branches may be cancelled if the vendor applies in writing, the main registration is cancelled (in which case all branch registrations will be cancelled), or it appears to the Commissioner that the duties under the VAT Act or the TA Act have not been carried out properly.

title: What conditions must be met for SARS to impose an understatement penalty on a vendor?
text: An understatement penalty may only be imposed if the fiscus is prejudiced by the vendor's conduct in reporting its VAT liability. The fiscus will be prejudiced if there is a shortfall, which is the difference between the correct amount of tax that should have been paid and the amount that was paid by the vendor in respect of a tax period. The prejudice must have been because a vendor defaulted in rendering a return, filed a return but omitted an item, filed a return with an incorrect statement, failed to pay the correct amount of tax where a return is not required, or entered into an impermissible avoidance arrangement.

title: What is the time period during which a vendor acquiring an enterprise may issue tax invoices reflecting the details of the supplying vendor?
text: A vendor acquiring an enterprise may issue tax invoices reflecting the details of the supplying vendor for a period of six months from the date of acquiring the enterprise, as per section 20(5A).

title: What turnover threshold requires a vendor to inform SARS about a category change?
text: A vendor that is not registered under Category C is required to inform SARS when taxable supplies exceed or are likely to exceed R30 million in any consecutive period of 12 months. Similarly, a vendor registered under Category D must inform SARS when the total value of taxable supplies for the past 12 months has exceeded R1.5 million or is likely to exceed R1.5 million in the next 12 months.

title: Does SARS accept cheque payments for VAT?
text: No, SARS no longer accepts cheque payments. South African banks ceased accepting cheque payments with effect from 1 January 2021, and SARS also ceased to accept cheque payments from that date. Cheques received by SARS will be returned to the vendor.

title: When did the amendments to the DRC Regulations come into effect?
text: The amendments to the DRC Regulations came into effect from 1 April 2024 unless otherwise stated.

title: What documentation must a vendor possess to apply zero-rating for repair services on temporarily imported goods?
text: The vendor must be in possession of a copy of the SARS Customs Declaration evidencing the temporary import as well as the corresponding release notification, among other documentary requirements.

title: What must match for a request to change bank details to be considered?
text: The vendor or the authorised representative vendor's details must always match the existing details on the SARS system for a request to change bank details to be considered.

title: Under what condition are goods manufactured by an association not for gain exempt?
text: Goods made or manufactured by an association not for gain are exempt if at least 80% of the value of the materials used in making or manufacturing such goods consists of donated goods.

title: What documents and agreements must be included in a written application to apply self-invoicing?
text: The written application must contain a description of the nature of the businesses carried on by the supplier and the recipient, a full description of the transactions requiring self-invoicing, details of existing invoicing procedures being followed, and an undertaking by the recipient to comply with administrative requirements. The applicant must also obtain and retain the written agreement of each affected supplier and their written confirmation that they will comply with the administrative requirements.

title: What is the threshold for taxable supplies that allows natural persons to account on the payments basis?
text: Natural persons (or partnerships consisting only of natural persons) whose total taxable supplies at the end of a tax period have not exceeded R2,5 million in the previous 12 months, and are not likely to exceed R2,5 million in the next 12 months, are allowed to account on the payments basis.

title: What must farmers remember to show on their VAT return to avoid unnecessary audits?
text: Farmers must remember to show the total consideration in field 2 on their VAT return – failure to do so will result in unnecessary audits.

title: On what basis can vendors be selected for audits?
text: Vendors may be selected for an inspection or verification based on any consideration relevant for the proper administration of a tax Act, including a random or risk assessment basis.

title: What forms must accompany a VAT ruling application?
text: The application must be accompanied by the VAT301 form and the Power of Attorney Form (where applicable).

title: Does filing an objection automatically suspend the obligation to pay tax?
text: No. Under the 'pay now, argue later' rule, the obligation to pay tax arising upon the issue of an assessment is not automatically suspended by an objection or appeal. A separate request for suspension of payment must be made to SARS.

title: What types of entities are eligible to apply for Category E registration?
text: The vendor must be a company or a trust fund.

title: Under which legislative framework are VAT class rulings and VAT rulings currently issued?
text: VAT class rulings and VAT rulings are issued under section 41B, read with Chapter 7 of the TA Act.

title: What must SARS prove according to the TA Act regarding assessments based on estimates?
text: According to section 102 of the TA Act, SARS must prove that an assessment based on an estimate is reasonable.

title: Which types of vehicles do NOT qualify as a 'motor car' under VAT regulations?
text: Vehicles that do not qualify as a 'motor car' include: vehicles capable of accommodating more than 16 persons (such as a bus), specialised vehicles such as hysters, graders, tractors, mobile cranes, and earthmoving vehicles, ambulances and caravans, vehicles with an unladen mass of 3500 kg or more, single cab bakkies/trucks/lorries/delivery vehicles, and hearses and game viewing vehicles.

title: In what circumstances can a vendor deduct input tax on motor cars?
text: Input tax deduction on motor cars is only allowed for suppliers of motor cars, such as motor car dealers or car rental enterprises, as it is the nature of these enterprises to supply motor cars on a continuous or regular basis.

title: What can a telecommunications supplier do when a customer's unused credit is used to pay a third party on behalf of the customer?
text: The telecommunications supplier may issue a credit note under section 21 of the VAT Act as specified in BGR 72 and claim the corresponding input tax. This allows the supplier to recover the inequity that arises from paying output tax on the portion of the telecommunications service that will never be supplied.

title: How is interest calculated on late VAT payments according to current rules?
text: Interest at the prescribed rate is charged per month or part thereof on late payments of VAT. The prescribed rate is linked to the rate determined under section 80 of the PFMA. Interest is calculated from the first day of the month after the month in which payment was due, until the outstanding amount is paid.

title: What is the monthly interest amount calculated in this example?
What should a company do when it undergoes a name change regarding tax invoices issued in the old name?
text: When a company undergoes a name change, tax invoices in the old name are not invalidated. The recipient vendor should not apply for a ruling under section 16(2)(g), but instead maintain documentary proof of the name change from the Companies and Intellectual Property Commission, inform SARS immediately by way of a change in registered particulars, and inform all suppliers to correct their records.

title: What telecommunication services are subject to VAT?
text: Telephone, internet, computer and other telecommunication services are subject to VAT.

title: When did the single registration process for all tax types come into effect?
text: The single registration process for all tax types came into effect on 12 May 2014.

title: What requirement do the Export Regulations impose regarding the tax invoice in Example 33?
text: The Export Regulations require that the tax invoice complies with the requirements in section 20(4).

title: What is the VAT treatment of fees and commissions related to financial services?
text: All fees, commissions, merchant's discount and similar fee-based charges relating to certain financial services are subject to VAT. For example, in a credit agreement where a borrower pays both interest and fees, only the fee will attract VAT, whereas the interest will be regarded as consideration for the supply of an exempt financial service.

title: What is required to secure the amount of VAT payable on temporarily imported goods or goods in transit through the RSA?
text: A provisional payment or bond will normally be required to secure the amount of VAT payable on the importation of goods that are temporarily imported into the RSA or removed in transit through the RSA. When the goods leave the country, the provisional payment may be refunded, or the bond released.

title: What is the definition of a 'first incidence' for the purposes of penalty remission?
text: A 'first incidence' means that no other penalty (fixed or percentage-based) must have been imposed within a period of 36 months preceding the one in respect of which the remission is concerned.

title: What is the general rule regarding the deductibility of entertainment expenses as input tax?
text: As a general rule, expenses relating to entertainment may not be deducted as input tax. However, there are some exceptions to this rule.

title: What are the different payment methods mentioned in section 10.4?
text: The different payment methods mentioned in section 10.4 are: Payment by EFT, Payment by using eFiling, and Payments at various banks.

title: Who can benefit from the zero rate for services physically performed outside the Republic?
text: Both residents and non-residents can benefit from the zero rate for services physically rendered or performed outside the RSA, or to a customs controlled area enterprise or a special economic zone operator in a customs controlled area.

title: In what situations would it generally not be advantageous for a person to register voluntarily for VAT?
text: It will generally not be advantageous for a person to register voluntarily where there are very few taxable expenses on which input tax can be deducted (for example, when the main enterprise expense is salaries and wages), or where most of the supplies are made to final consumers who are not registered for VAT.

title: What is the VAT rate applied to services supplied by an implementing agency for a foreign donor funded project?
text: The supply of services by an implementing agency in respect of implementing, operating, administering or managing a foreign donor funded project is taxable at the rate of 0%.

title: What is the de minimis rule for valuable metal under the amended DRC Regulations?
text: The de minimis rule excludes the supply of valuable metal containing less than 1% of gold in gross weight and a supply of gold-plated jewellery where the gold is present as a minor constituent only. Therefore, the DRC Regulations will no longer apply to these supplies and the normal VAT rules will apply.

title: Under what circumstances must a vendor approach SARS to obtain approval to use an alternative apportionment method?
text: A vendor must approach SARS to obtain approval to use an alternative method when the turnover-based method is inappropriate because it produces an absurd result, proves impossible to use, or does not yield a fair approximation of the extent of taxable application of the enterprise's VAT-inclusive expenses.

title: What document must be submitted when an agent files a VAT ruling application on behalf of a client?
text: A Power of Attorney or equivalent written statement must be submitted, in terms of which the applicant (client) authorises the agent to file the application and act as the applicant's representative throughout the application and ruling process.

title: What are the two main methods of accounting for VAT in the South African VAT system?
text: The two main methods are the 'invoice basis' (or 'accrual basis') and the 'payments basis' (or 'cash basis') of accounting. The invoice basis is the general requirement, while certain vendors may qualify to use the payments basis.

title: When can VAT paid on importation of goods by a vendor be deducted as input tax?
text: VAT payable on importation of goods by a vendor may only be deducted during the tax period when the goods are released under the Customs and Excise Act.

title: What is 'relevant material' in the context of SARS information gathering powers?
text: Relevant material is information, documents, or things that are foreseeably relevant for tax risk assessment, assessing and collecting tax, or for determining compliance with a tax obligation. The information gathering powers of SARS may only be used to obtain relevant material.

title: What are the main categories of expenses for which input tax is denied under the VAT Act?
text: Input tax is denied on: goods or services acquired for purposes of entertainment (subject to certain exceptions); membership fees or subscriptions of clubs, associations or societies of a sporting, social or recreational nature; the acquisition of a motor car by a vendor (subject to exceptions for motor car dealers and car rental enterprises); and goods or services acquired by medical schemes or benefit funds for the purposes of health insurance or benefit cover.

title: Can ADR proceedings be electronically recorded?
text: No, ADR proceedings may not be electronically recorded.

title: Within what timeframe must you advise SARS of changes to your registered particulars?
text: You are required to advise SARS within 21 days of any changes in your registered particulars, including changes in your representative, business address, banking details, trading name, or if you cease trading.

title: What must services to non-residents be tested against in order to qualify for zero-rating?
text: The supply of services to non-residents must be tested against all the exclusions in sub-paragraphs (i) to (iii) of section 11(2)(l) in order to qualify for the zero-rating.

title: What is the time limit for requesting reasons for an assessment if they are not adequate or not included?
text: You are entitled to request reasons for an assessment in writing within 30 business days from the date of the assessment if the statement of grounds are not adequate or not included at all.

title: What is the timeframe for completing the ADR process?
text: The ADR process must be concluded within 90 business days, or such further period that you and SARS may agree upon.

title: What should be included when submitting a ruling application to SARS for VAT matters?
text: A ruling application should be submitted to SARS headed either 'Application for a VAT Class Ruling' or 'Application for a VAT Ruling' together with the VAT301 form by e-mail to VATRulings@sars.gov.za.

title: What is the proper procedure if a taxpayer wants to challenge an interpretation given in a VAT Ruling?
text: If an interpretation given in a VAT Ruling is to be challenged, then the ruling must be taken on review in the High Court. However, if SARS raises an assessment as a result of the interpretation of the law provided in the ruling, the assessment itself may be the subject of an objection or appeal rather than the ruling itself.

title: What documents must accompany a VAT Ruling application?
text: The VAT ruling application must be accompanied by a VAT301 form and a signed Power of Attorney form where applicable. Both the POA and the VAT301 must be completed and signed (physical or electronic signature) by the relevant authorised persons and must also be signed by a witness.

title: What is the time of supply for concessive supplies such as office rentals and maintenance services?
text: The time of supply for concessive supplies is deemed to take place on the earlier of the date when payment is due or is received. Examples include office and car rentals and on-going contracts for maintenance, management or cleaning services.

title: What are the main topics covered in Chapter 1 of the VAT 404 Guide for Vendors?
text: Chapter 1 covers the following topics: What is VAT, How does VAT work, Who charges VAT, Payment, recovery and refund of VAT, Accounting for VAT, Documentation, and Tax Administration Act.

title: What obligation arises for property developers when they let residential properties out as dwellings while continuing to market them for sale?
text: Property developers will be making exempt supplies and will be required to account for output tax on the OMV (Open Market Value) of the properties concerned at the time when the change of use occurs.

title: What is GN 1594 related to?
text: GN 1594 prescribes the requirements for tax invoices in respect of electronic services supplied by non-residents.

title: What are the main factors that affect the amount of input tax deductions a vendor is entitled to?
text: The main factors that affect the amount of input tax and/or other specified deductions are: the accounting basis on which you are registered (whether you are on the invoice or payments basis) and any special rules applicable to the particular supply; whether the specific inputs are disallowed or are limited in any way; and the extent to which the goods or services acquired will be used for taxable purposes.

title: What are the methods by which vendors can request a VAT201 return from SARS?
text: Vendors can request the VAT201 return from the nearest SARS office in person, in writing, or telephonically via the SARS Service Centre on 0800 00 7277.

title: When were all previous editions of VAT 404 – Guide for Vendors withdrawn?
text: All previous editions of VAT 404 – Guide for Vendors were withdrawn with effect from 17 December 2024.

title: What must be done with the receipt number generated by eFiling after successful payment?
text: The receipt number generated by eFiling must be inserted in the relevant field on the VAT215 record.

title: What is the current standard VAT rate in South Africa?
text: VAT is currently levied at the standard rate of 15% on most supplies and importations.

title: What is the SACU and which countries are members of it?
text: The SACU is the Southern African Customs Union, which is formed by the BLNS countries (Botswana, Lesotho, Namibia and Swaziland/eSwatini) together with the RSA (Republic of South Africa).

title: What method of apportionment did C elect to use and what was the split between taxable and exempt supplies?
text: C elected to use the standard turnover-based method of apportionment and determined that 80% of the building's income is derived from taxable supplies and 20% from exempt supplies.

title: What remedy is available to a vendor to dispute interest on late payment of VAT?
text: A request for remission is the only remedy available to a vendor to dispute interest on late payment of VAT. A vendor is not allowed to lodge an objection to dispute interest on late payment or a decision by SARS to disallow a request for remission.

title: What types of goods imported by tourists are exempt from VAT according to Schedule 1?
text: Goods imported by tourists for personal use are exempt from VAT, including visitor's personal effects, sporting and recreational equipment which will be used during their stay in the RSA, and wine, cigarettes, perfume and certain other new or used goods being gifts or items for personal use (within certain limits) imported as part of a passenger's baggage.

title: What is the VAT treatment when a vendor sells a motor car for which input tax was previously denied?
text: The subsequent sale by the vendor of a motor car in respect of which an input tax was denied is not seen as a supply in the course or furtherance of an enterprise, and therefore the vendor is not required to account for output tax. This compensates for the disallowance of the input tax deduction on the acquisition of the motor car.

title: What interpretation note addresses supplies made without consideration?
text: Interpretation Note 70 addresses supplies made for no consideration.

title: What is the general rule for determining the time of supply for VAT purposes?
text: The time of supply is the earlier of the time an invoice is issued by the supplier (or the recipient in certain instances), or the time any payment of consideration is received in respect of that supply.

title: What is the general VAT treatment of public authorities' activities?
text: The activities of public authorities are generally out-of-scope for VAT purposes and they will not be able to register for VAT, unless they have been notified by the Commissioner that their supplies constitute 'enterprise' activities.

title: Who is required to keep records under the VAT Act, beyond registered vendors who have filed returns?
text: The duty to retain records is extended to include those who ought to, but have not filed a return, and those who would have been obliged to file a return if not for an exemption or threshold.

title: What documentation must a third-party vendor supplier issue under section 8(8A)?
text: If the third-party supplier is a vendor, then a tax invoice must be issued to both the insurer and the insured parties, to the extent that each party has paid, or is liable to pay a consideration for the goods or services to be reinstated.

title: What adjustments are covered in Chapter 9?
text: Chapter 9 covers adjustments including irrecoverable debts, debit and credit notes, prompt settlement discounts, rounding differences, and changes in use or application of goods or services between taxable and non-taxable purposes.

title: Which of the following items are listed as zero-rated basic foodstuffs?
text: Zero-rated basic foodstuffs include brown bread, dried mealies and mealie rice, brown bread flour (excluding wheaten bran), samp, hens eggs, fresh vegetables and fruit, dried beans, lentils, maize meal, rice, pilchards in tins or cans, vegetable cooking oil (excluding olive oil), milk, cultured milk, milk powder and dairy powder blend, and edible legumes and pulses of leguminous plants such as peas, beans, and peanuts.

title: How do you submit a VAT Ruling application to SARS?
text: You may submit a VAT Ruling application on a VAT301 to SARS by e-mail to VATRulings@sars.gov.za. All applications must comply with section 79 of the TA Act (excluding section 79(4)(f), (k) and (6)).

title: Under what condition is the local leg of transport between two places in the RSA zero-rated when it is part of an international transportation service?
text: The local leg of transport between two places in the RSA is zero-rated to the extent that the same supplier is contractually liable to the same recipient for both the local and international portions of the transportation service. The supplier may subcontract the work to another vendor but must remain contractually liable to the recipient for the relevant part of the transportation.

title: What information must be included on a full tax invoice according to Section 20(4) of the VAT Act?
text: A full tax invoice must include: the words 'tax invoice' or 'invoice' or 'VAT invoice'; name, address and VAT registration number of the supplier; name, address and VAT registration number of recipient; serial number and date of issue; full and proper description of the goods and/or services; quantity or volume of goods or services supplied; and price and VAT according to one of the 3 approved methods.

title: What does section 5.2 of the guide address?
text: Section 5.2 addresses the time of supply, covering the general rule, connected persons, progressive/successive/periodic supplies, instalment credit agreements, fixed property, fringe benefits, lay-by agreements, machines/meters and other devices, betting transactions, and supplies made by a branch.

title: What is the input tax adjustment amount that C is entitled to deduct in the February 2023 VAT return?
text: C is entitled to deduct an additional R35 687 in the February 2023 VAT return under field 18. This is calculated using the formula: 15/115 × R1 368 000 × (80% – 60%) = R35 687.

title: What documentation is required for deducting input tax on goods repossessed or surrendered under an ICA?
text: Records must be maintained by the vendor deducting the input tax as per section 20(8A) and the further particulars prescribed in BGR 63.

title: What is the VAT treatment for J's Transport regarding the VAT paid on the importation of a new bus in Example 19?
text: J's Transport cannot deduct any VAT paid on the importation of the bus as input tax. Since the supply of public passenger transport to fare-paying passengers is exempt, the VAT paid is wholly attributable to making exempt supplies.

title: What documents must a vendor possess when submitting a VAT return containing a deduction for imported goods?
text: The vendor must be in possession of a statement containing the following information at the time the VAT return containing the deduction is submitted to SARS: the quantity or volume of the goods, the value of the goods, and the amount of tax paid along with the receipt relating to the payment of such tax (the receipt number issued on eFiling for such payment).

title: Has ABC Municipality arranged its contracts and accounting methods to allocate costs by division?
text: No, ABC Municipality has not arranged its contracts or implemented accounting methods to specifically allocate costs incurred by each division.

title: What is the minimum percentage decrease in the extent of taxable use of capital goods or services that triggers an adjustment requirement?
text: An adjustment is required where there is a decrease of more than 10% in the extent to which capital goods or services are used or applied in the course of making taxable supplies.

title: What should you do after making a VAT payment at a bank?
text: You must still send your VAT201 return to SARS for capturing even if you have made payment at the bank or if you are submitting a nil return.

title: What is the difference between direct export and indirect export under VAT 404?
text: A direct export is where the supplying vendor consigns or delivers movable goods to a recipient at an address in an export country, with the supplier in control of the export. An indirect export refers to a situation where goods are exported by the recipient (being a qualifying purchaser) and that person removes or arranges for the removal and transport of movable goods to an address in an export country.

title: What is the rule for calculating deductible input tax on second-hand goods?
text: The amount of input tax that is deductible is the tax fraction of the payment made towards the purchase price.

title: From which tax period can input tax on imported goods be deducted?
text: With effect from 1 April 2015, input tax deduction on imported goods may only be made from the tax period in which the custom release notification is issued, which can be confirmed by the date on such notification. The goods must have been released by Customs before the deduction can be made.

title: Within what timeframe must a deduction be made after a tax invoice for a standard rated supply should have been issued?
text: A deduction must be made in a tax period falling within five years after the end of the tax period during which a tax invoice for a standard rated supply should have been issued (that is, within 21 days from the time of supply).

title: When will SARS issue an additional assessment?
text: SARS will issue an additional assessment if any assessment does not reflect the correct application of a tax Act to the prejudice of SARS or the fiscus. An additional assessment replaces the original or latest assessment and will always be issued subsequent to that assessment.

title: What effect did the introduction of the Advance Tax Ruling (ATR) legislation in 2006 have on previously issued rulings?
text: The introduction of the Advance Tax Ruling (ATR) legislation in 2006 had the effect of withdrawing all rulings previously issued by the Commissioner.

title: What is the consequence if all conditions for the exclusion are met regarding the foreign lessor's VAT obligations?
text: If all of the above conditions are met, the foreign lessor will not be regarded as conducting an enterprise in the Republic and will not be liable to register for VAT.

title: What are the key requirements that an objection to SARS must meet?
text: An objection must: be in the DISP01 form with completed information; specify in detail the grounds upon which the objection is made; specify an address where notice and delivery of SARS's decision will be accepted; and be signed by the taxpayer or their appointed representative.

title: What can new enterprises with no past financial statements use to estimate their apportionment percentage?
text: New enterprises with no past financial statements can use an estimate based on expected taxable turnover according to the enterprise's business plan or sales/marketing forecasts for each and every tax period.

title: Can welfare organisations deduct input tax on entertainment expenses?
text: Yes, a welfare organisation may deduct input tax on entertainment expenses which are associated with its welfare activities. Entertainment expenses are usually disallowed for other vendors.

title: How is VAT applied when a municipality charges a flat rate that includes municipal rates plus other services?
text: Where a municipality charges a flat rate which includes a charge for municipal rates plus other charges for water, electricity, refuse removal or other standard-rated goods or services supplied, the entire charge is subject to the standard rate.

title: What is the role of the Tax Ombud?
text: The Tax Ombud reviews and addresses complaints by a taxpayer regarding a service matter or a procedural or administrative matter arising from the application of a Tax Act by SARS. It is an independent, informal and simple mechanism affording taxpayers recourse.

title: Which legislation does the VAT 404 guide primarily reference?
text: All references to sections in the guide are to sections of the Value-Added Tax Act 89 of 1991 (VAT Act), unless the context indicates otherwise.

title: What is a vendor entitled to if they pay disputed tax and their objection or appeal is upheld?
text: A vendor is entitled to interest from the date of payment of the disputed amount until the date on which such amount is refunded.

title: What is the VAT treatment when a person stays in commercial accommodation for more than 28 days?
text: When a person stays in an establishment which provides commercial accommodation for an unbroken period of more than 28 days, only 60% of the all-inclusive charge for the accommodation and the domestic goods and services is subject to VAT at the standard rate.

title: What is the difference between a Binding Private Ruling (BPR) and a Binding Class Ruling (BCR) in terms of who they apply to?
text: A Binding Private Ruling (BPR) applies to one or more parties to a 'proposed transaction' and is binding on SARS and the applicant, while a Binding Class Ruling (BCR) applies to a specific 'class' of persons in respect of a 'proposed transaction' and is binding on SARS and the specific 'class' of persons.

title: Are there any goods that are exempt from VAT on importation?
text: Yes, no VAT is payable on the importation of certain goods. These exemptions are listed in Schedule 1 to the VAT Act.

title: What is the de minimis rule in the context of the turnover-based apportionment method?
text: Where the formula yields an apportionment ratio/percentage of 95% or more, the full amount of VAT incurred on mixed expenses may be deducted.

title: What are examples of activities that qualify for the Diesel Refund Scheme?
text: Activities that qualify for the Diesel Refund Scheme include own primary production activities in farming, forestry, mining or fishing, as well as certain other activities such as coastal shipping.

title: What are the two types of penalties mentioned in the context?
text: The two types of penalties are fixed-amount penalties and percentage-based penalties. Fixed-amount penalties are charged when an administrative obligation is not complied with, and percentage-based penalties are generally imposed when certain amounts of tax are not paid.

title: Within what timeframe must a vendor issue a tax invoice to the recipient for supplies exceeding R50?
text: A vendor must issue a tax invoice to the recipient within 21 days of the supply having been made for which the consideration for the supply exceeds R50, whether or not the recipient has requested it.

title: What must a vendor do if goods acquired for making taxable supplies are subsequently applied wholly for private purposes?
text: An adjustment to output tax will be required where goods or services acquired for making taxable supplies are subsequently applied wholly for private, exempt or other non-taxable purposes.

title: What is the 10-day rule for vendors?
text: A vendor that has an accounting date within 10 days before or after the end of the month in which the tax period ends may use that date as the last day for the tax period, provided the vendor selects a fixed day or date approved by the Commissioner.

title: What are the two types of instalment credit agreements?
text: The two types of instalment credit agreements (ICAs) are an instalment sale agreement and an instalment lease agreement. These agreements are characterised by a suspensive condition as to the passing of ownership of the goods or services supplied.

title: What method does the context describe for calculating the estimated percentage for input tax deduction?
text: Vendors are allowed to calculate the estimated percentage using the turnover figures from the previous year's financial statements, and to apply that percentage for deducting input tax in each and every individual tax period for the current year.

title: Who is required to create and furnish a tax invoice when goods supplied under an instalment credit agreement are repossessed from or surrendered by a vendor?
text: When goods supplied under an instalment credit agreement are repossessed from or surrendered by a vendor, the person exercising the right of repossession (normally a bank or other financier who is also a vendor) is required to create and furnish a tax invoice to the debtor.

title: What happens if a vendor owes amounts on other taxes when claiming a VAT refund?
text: If you owe amounts on other taxes administered by the Commissioner, debt equalisation will be applied to offset the amounts owing in respect of other taxes, and the balance (if any) will be refunded.

title: What is the total amount of mixed expenses for Company J for the year ending 28 February 2023?
text: The total mixed expenses for Company J is R303 000, which comprises: Painting (R120 000) + Electricity (R48 000) + Water (R63 000) + Telephone (R6 000) + Insurance (R66 000).

title: What is required to apply for voluntary registration for a future enterprise supply as a going concern?
text: To apply for voluntary registration if you intend to carry on an enterprise from a future date, the enterprise or part thereof which is capable of separate operation must be supplied to you as a going concern.

title: What does a negative value in field 34 indicate?
text: If field 34 is less than zero, a refund is due to you.

title: What types of VAT201 returns will SARS not accept?
text: SARS will not accept old format VAT201 returns, copies of VAT201 declarations printed from eFiling and used for manual submission, manually submitted VAT201 returns by vendors falling within Category C tax period, and photocopied returns.

title: In the example provided, what was C's extent of taxable use before and after the business change?
text: C's extent of taxable use was 80% before the business change and 60% after the business change at the end of February 2023.

title: What is the basis for the principle underlying interest calculation on tax payments?
text: Interest is compensation for the loss of the use of money.

title: Who charges output tax and on what?
text: A vendor charges output tax (VAT) on a taxable supply of goods or services.

title: What is the deferment facility and which taxes does it cover?
text: The deferment facility is a credit facility with SARS that allows regular importers or their clearing agents to defer payment for a specified amount for customs duty and VAT payable on the importation of goods into the RSA. However, the facility may not be used for the payment of excise duty or fuel levy.

title: On what page does the section about 'The 10-day rule' begin?
text: The 10-day rule section begins on page 24.

title: What is the total price M pays for the painting in Example 33, including VAT?
text: M pays R16 500 for the painting, which includes R2 152,17 VAT.

title: What documents must a vendor obtain and retain for all supplies?
text: For all supplies, the vendor must obtain and retain a copy of the supplier's identity document/identity card in the case of a natural person. In the case of a company or CC, a business letterhead or similar document is also required which shows the name and registration number allocated by the Registrar of Companies.

title: What is the USP penalty rate for a substantial understatement in a standard case?
text: The USP penalty rate for a substantial understatement in a standard case is 10%.

title: What should a vendor do if their preferred cut-off date does not fall within the approved categories listed in BGR 19?
text: If the preferred cut-off dates do not fall within one of the categories listed in BGR 19, the vendor concerned may apply for a VAT ruling or VAT class ruling to determine another appropriate date on which the tax period(s) should end.

title: When can input tax be deducted under the payments basis of accounting?
text: Under the payments basis, the vendor may only deduct input tax to the extent of actual payment made during the tax period towards the purchase price of the goods or services acquired.

title: How many years of apportionment ratio calculations must be submitted in a VAT ruling application?
text: Detailed calculations of the apportionment ratio for three consecutive years from the latest information available must be submitted for the standard turnover-based method and the two alternative apportionment methods that have been considered.

title: What is the requirement regarding tax invoices for Category E vendors?
text: The vendor must agree with the recipients that tax invoices are issued only once a year at the end of the year of assessment of the vendor making the supplies.

title: What is the 'pay now, argue later' principle in VAT taxation?
text: The 'pay now, argue later' principle is a long-standing principle that requires a vendor to pay taxes that are the subject of a dispute with SARS. This principle has been affirmed by the highest court in the country and is now governed by the TA Act. The obligation to pay tax is not automatically suspended by an objection or appeal but only upon specific request by the vendor.

title: What interpretation notes are referenced regarding zero-rating provisions?
text: Interpretation Note 85 'The Master Currency Case' confirms the Commissioner's interpretation of the zero-rating provisions in section 11(2)(l), and Interpretation Note 81 'The Supply of Goods and Services by Professional Hunters and Taxidermists to Non-residents' provides additional information.

title: Who is required to charge VAT on supplies of goods or services in South Africa?
text: Generally, vendors are required to charge VAT on all supplies of goods or services in South Africa, subject to certain exemptions and exceptions. A vendor is a person who is registered or is required to be registered for VAT.

title: What does manual submission refer to in the context of VAT returns?
text: Manual submission refers to a situation where a vendor is not registered on eFiling and will have to call at a SARS branch office or service centre for assistance to complete the return online.

title: At what rate must a farmer pay VAT on income received from a harvest through crop sharing?
text: A farmer who receives income from a harvest (crop sharing) must pay VAT at the standard rate on that portion of the proceeds unless the supplies are zero-rated under any of the items in Schedule 2 to the VAT Act.

title: What is the minimum VAT-inclusive total of movable goods that must be purchased to qualify for a VAT refund?
text: R250 per qualifying purchaser

title: What is a share block company for VAT purposes?
text: A share block company is a company whose activities comprise of or include the operation of any scheme in terms of which a share in that scheme grants a right to or an interest in the use of immovable property. The shareholders acquire rights to occupy specified parts of the immovable property allocated to their shares in the company.

title: What documentation is required for input tax deduction on standard-rated supplies?
text: For standard-rated supplies, a valid tax invoice, debit note or credit note or other prescribed documentation must be held by the vendor. Should such tax invoice, debit note or credit note be issued in the name of the vendor's agent, the vendor must be in possession of a statement received from such agent containing certain particulars.

title: Who can receive goods at a designated harbour or airport for the zero rate to apply under 'Other supplies'?
text: Under 'Other supplies', the zero rate may apply when goods are delivered to the port authority, master of the ship, a container operator, the pilot of an aircraft, or are brought within the control area of the airport authority, provided the goods are situated at the designated harbour or airport and are destined to be exported from the RSA.

title: What is the OMV of advertising and publicity that ABC Rugby Club must account for in this transaction?
text: The OMV of advertising and publicity is R15 000, which represents the total consideration received by C Hardware Stores in exchange for the rugby kits and cash.

title: Under what circumstances may the supply of financial services to a non-resident be subject to VAT at the zero rate instead of being exempt?
text: The supply of certain financial services to a non-resident, who is not in the Republic at the time the service is rendered, may be subject to VAT at the zero rate. For example, interest received in respect of the provision of credit to a non-resident is subject to tax at the rate of 0% provided the non-resident is not in the Republic at the time that the financial service is rendered to the non-resident.

title: On what date did P submit the return and payment?
What are the three circumstances that determine if electronic services supplied by non-residents are included in the definition of 'enterprise'?
text: The three circumstances are: (1) The recipient of those electronic services is a South African resident, (2) Payment for such electronic services originates from a South African bank account, and (3) The recipient of such electronic services has an address (for example, residential, business or postal) in South Africa. At least any two of these three circumstances must apply.

title: What are the requirements that the Commissioner must be satisfied about before approving a voluntary registration application?
text: The Commissioner must be satisfied that the person applying for voluntary registration: has a fixed place of residence or business; keeps proper accounting records; has opened a banking account in the RSA; and has not previously been registered as a vendor under VAT or General Sales Tax (GST) and failed to perform the duties of a vendor.

title: What are two important uses of the date of assessment?
text: The date of assessment is used to work out: (1) the period for record retention; and (2) the date after which SARS may not issue another assessment (subject to certain exceptions).

title: Under what conditions can a company deduct VAT on pre-incorporation expenses?
text: A company can deduct VAT on pre-incorporation expenses if: (1) the person was reimbursed by the company for the whole amount paid, and (2) the goods or services were acquired for the purpose of an enterprise to be carried on by the company and have not been used for any other purpose.

title: What is the VAT treatment of motor oil and oil lubricants?
text: Motor oil and oil lubricants are subject to the standard rate of VAT.

title: What is the purpose of the TA Act regarding registration requirements?
text: The TA Act seeks to provide a clear and comprehensive description of registration requirements and to make the procedural requirements for registration as easy as possible.

title: What forms must be submitted with a VAT Ruling application?
text: The application must be accompanied by the VAT301 form and the Power of Attorney Form (where applicable), along with all relevant information as specified in the requirements.

title: Are hobbies and private recreational pursuits subject to VAT?
text: No, hobbies or any private recreational pursuits not conducted in the form of a business are not subject to VAT.

title: What does the amendment to 'enterprise' focus on instead of regarding the FDFP as a 'person'?
text: The amendment focuses on including reference to the activities of an implementing agency in respect of the FDFP activities.

title: What is a non-binding private opinion in the context of VAT rulings?
text: A non-binding private opinion provides informal guidance on the VAT treatment of a particular set of facts and circumstances or transaction. It does not have 'binding effect' upon SARS and is issued under the ATR rules, and may therefore be rejected under certain circumstances.

title: How does separate VAT registration benefit an association not for gain?
text: An association not for gain can separately apply the compulsory and voluntary registration thresholds to each of its branches, divisions or enterprises in order to determine which of them must, or could be registered. This allows the association to register and account for VAT only in respect of certain specific taxable activities, instead of all activities.

title: What are some key duties that a registered VAT vendor must perform?
text: A registered VAT vendor must charge VAT, submit returns, make VAT payments on time, and keep proper records for at least five years.

title: What documentation is required when applying for voluntary registration for certain activities?
text: When applying for voluntary registration for the listed activities, you must be in possession of the relevant permit, licence or similar document required to conduct those activities from the relevant regulatory authority (if required). Alternatively, you must have proof that you have applied to the relevant authorities that are responsible for issuing the permit, licence or similar document.

title: What are the two values that B represents in the input tax adjustment formula, and which one is used in C's calculation?
text: B represents the lesser of: (i) the adjusted cost of the building namely, R1 368 000; or (ii) the OMV of the building namely, R2 280 000. In C's calculation, the adjusted cost of R1 368 000 is used as it is the lesser value.

title: What is the consideration for betting services?
text: The consideration for the betting services is the total amount received in respect of the bet.

title: What are the two main restrictions on decisions that can be given under section 72?
text: No decision can be given under section 72 which has the effect of reducing or increasing the liability for tax levied under the VAT Act, or is contrary to the construct and policy intent of the VAT Act as a whole or any specific provision thereof.

title: What VAT rate change occurred on 1 April 2018?
text: On 1 April 2018, the standard rate of VAT increased from 14% to 15%.

title: What documents are important for the VAT system and what purpose do they serve?
