text: Tax invoices for supplies made, bills of entry for goods imported, and proper accounting records and documents are important aspects of the VAT system. These documents form an audit trail which SARS uses to verify that the vendor has complied with the law.

title: What must a vendor account for if a lay-by sale is cancelled?
text: If a lay-by sale is cancelled for any reason, any deposit payment retained by the vendor or any amount of the consideration paid (or which is recoverable) is regarded as consideration for a taxable supply of services. The vendor must account for output tax on the total amount retained in the tax period during which the sale was cancelled.

title: In Example 42, why was a credit note issued by Vendor (Pty) Limited to ABC (Pty) Limited?
text: A credit note was issued because the vendor charged for 400 units of 30mm Widgets instead of 300 units, resulting in an overcharge of R100 in net amount and R13.04 in VAT.

title: What are the accepted channels for changing bank account details with SARS?
text: The accepted channels for changing bank account details are: in person at any SARS branch (the preferred channel), through the SARS eFiling channel if registered as an eFiler, and via email only in exceptional circumstances.

title: What are second-hand goods and what do they exclude?
text: Second-hand goods are goods (including fixed property) that have been previously owned and used. The term excludes certain things such as animals, gold, gold coins, and 'old order' mining rights. Vendors trading in goods containing gold which are sold in substantially the same state as when those goods were acquired may be entitled to a deduction of notional input tax in certain circumstances.

title: In the barter transaction between ABC Rugby Club and C Hardware Stores, what is the total consideration received by ABC Rugby Club?
text: The total consideration received by ABC Rugby Club is R15 000, which comprises the OMV of rugby kit received (R10 000) and cash received as payment (R5 000).

title: What is the final output tax payable by the vendor on the VAT return after accounting for the sale of the computer system?
text: The output tax payable is R2 400, calculated as the output tax on sale of R6 000 less the input tax credit of R3 600.

title: In which situation does the 10% upliftment not apply to imports?
text: The 10% upliftment does not apply in the case of imports from BLNS countries.

title: What is the compulsory VAT registration threshold in South Africa?
text: The compulsory VAT registration threshold is R1 million in any consecutive period of 12 months, or if you will exceed that amount in terms of a written contractual obligation. This threshold applies to the total value of taxable supplies (turnover) and not the net income (profit).

title: What was the threshold amount for a matter to be referred to the Tax Board after 1 January 2016?
text: The threshold amount for a matter to be referred to the Tax Board was increased to R1 million with effect from 1 January 2016.

title: What are the two conditions that must be met for input tax deduction to be allowed?
text: The person must have been reimbursed by the company for the whole amount paid, and the goods or services must have been acquired for the purpose of an enterprise to be carried on by the company with no other use of the goods or services.

title: What are the key requirements for foreign entities to use the single branch VAT registration under Section 23(2A)?
text: Under Section 23(2A), foreign entities forming part of the same group of companies must have one entity in the group that is already conducting an enterprise in South Africa and registered as a vendor (main registered vendor). The main registered vendor may then register a separate branch or division for all the other foreign entities in the group.

title: What are the circumstances under which the Commissioner may remit interest according to the TA Act?
text: The Commissioner's discretion to remit interest is limited to whether or not the payment of tax was made within time as a result of circumstances beyond the vendor's control. An example is when a vendor's payment instruction could not be carried out by the vendor's bank in time because of a failure in the banking system.

title: Under what condition are no additional assessments permitted?
text: No additional assessments may be made if the previous assessment was made in accordance with a practice generally prevailing at the time.

title: What does an extreme apportionment result of either 0% or 100% indicate?
text: An extreme result of either 0% or 100% may be an indication that either the formula is inappropriate, or it is not being applied correctly.

title: What is the VAT treatment for services supplied in connection with intellectual property rights used outside the Republic versus inside the Republic?
text: Services supplied in connection with intellectual property rights for use outside the Republic are taxable supplies which are subject to VAT at the zero rate. Any ancillary services supplied together with these intellectual property rights may also be zero-rated. However, should the intellectual property rights be used in the Republic, all services supplied in connection with such intellectual property rights must be standard rated.

title: How much input tax can the supermarket deduct on the purchase of boxes of paper sheets?
text: The supermarket may deduct R21,00 as input tax on the purchase of the boxes of paper sheets.

title: How can vendors calculate the apportionment percentage if it is difficult to determine accurately for each tax period?
text: Vendors are allowed to calculate the estimated percentage using the turnover figures from the previous year's financial statements and apply that percentage for deducting input tax in each individual tax period for the current year.

title: Is the supply of commercial accommodation considered a dwelling under VAT rules?
text: No, the definition of a dwelling excludes the supply of 'commercial accommodation'.

title: How is the cash value determined for a dealer in instalment credit agreements?
text: The cash value is the price at which the goods are normally sold by the dealer and other related costs (such as installation, assembly, erection) that form part of the capital amount financed under the ICA.

title: Under what circumstances will zero-rating not apply to agricultural products?
text: Zero-rating will not apply where: other goods or services not listed in the regulations are supplied to the agricultural industry (such as consultation fees from a vet or goods to install an irrigation system); the sale of the goods is prohibited under section 7bis of the Fertilisers, Farm Feed Agricultural Remedies Act 36 of 1947 (such as banned substances like DDT); or such products are purchased for purposes of resale.

title: What is the invoice-based credit method of VAT and how does it work?
text: The invoice-based credit method is a subtractive or credit input method that allows the vendor to deduct the tax incurred on enterprise inputs (input tax) and other permissible deductions from the tax collected on the supplies made by the vendor (output tax). This is known as the consumption-type VAT.

title: How does the R100 000 threshold affect vendors registered on the payments basis?
text: In the case of a supply for a consideration of R100 000 or more, vendors registered on the payments basis (other than public authorities, municipalities and certain municipal entities) must account for the full amount of output tax in the period in which the supply occurs, meaning the supply is treated as if on the invoice basis.

title: Under what circumstances may SARS withdraw an assessment?
text: SARS may withdraw an assessment if it was issued to the incorrect taxpayer, issued in respect of the incorrect tax period, or issued as a result of an incorrect payment allocation.

title: What is the effective date of the DRC Regulations changes?
text: The effective date of the Regulations is 1 January 2024.

title: What information must be included on a full tax invoice when the consideration is R5 000 or more?
text: A full tax invoice must include: the words 'tax invoice', 'VAT invoice' or 'invoice'; name, address and VAT registration number of the supplier; name, address and VAT registration number of the recipient; serial number and date of issue; full and proper description of goods and/or services supplied; quantity or volume of goods or services supplied; and price and VAT.

title: When can a person who previously accounted for VAT on the invoice basis switch to the payments basis?
text: A person who previously accounted for VAT on the invoice basis will only be allowed to account on the payments basis from a future tax period as specified by the Commissioner.

title: What is the physical address of SARS Head Office?
text: The SARS Head Office physical address is: South African Revenue Service, Lehae La SARS, 299 Bronkhorst Street, Nieuw Muckleneuk, 0181, Pretoria.

title: What types of entities are included in the definition of 'public authority' for VAT purposes?
text: Public authorities include all national and provincial government departments listed in Schedules 1, 2 or 3 of the Public Service Act; entities (including subsidiaries and controlled entities) listed in Parts A and C of Schedule 3 to the Public Finance Management Act (PFMA); or certain public entities which should be regarded as public authorities for VAT purposes.

title: What are the three types of advance rulings issued under Chapter 7 of the TA Act?
text: The three types of advance rulings issued under Chapter 7 of the TA Act are: Binding general ruling (BGR), Binding private ruling (BPR), and Binding class ruling (BCR).

title: What does 'practice generally prevailing' mean in relation to SARS?
text: If SARS has a practice which is generally prevailing, a vendor has an expectation that SARS will follow that practice in similar circumstances for every vendor. The meaning of this term has been dealt with in numerous cases, sometimes with conflicting outcomes, which has often left vendors unsure of the existence of a practice generally prevailing.

title: What does the cash value of consideration in instalment credit agreements include and exclude?
text: The cash value excludes any interest, finance charges and related admin fees but includes VAT and the sales amount for which the goods were sold.

title: What automatic suspension applies when a vendor requests remittance of an administrative non-compliance penalty?
text: There is an automatic suspension of the duty to pay and SARS's right to collect, which will run from the day the application is submitted until 21 business days after a decision is taken not to remit the administrative penalty.

title: What does the term 'connected person' include?
text: The term 'connected person' includes but is not limited to: family relatives, partnerships, trust beneficiaries, branches of the same legal entity, companies with substantially the same shareholders, natural persons who are related by blood or marriage, a company and any subsidiaries of that company, any CC and its members, and a natural person and a company where that natural person owns more than 10% of the shares or voting rights in that company.

title: What are the two main requirements for an organisation to qualify as a welfare organisation for VAT purposes?
text: An organisation must be an approved public benefit organisation (PBO) for income tax purposes and must carry on one or more of the welfare activities as determined by the Minister for purposes of the VAT Act.

title: Why must P account for output tax on the insurance indemnity payment?
text: P must account for output tax because P is a vendor registered under Category B tax period, and the delivery van was used in P's enterprise. Therefore, the indemnity payment is deemed to be received in the course of P's enterprise.

title: What is the time of supply rule for vending machines and similar devices that accept coins, tokens, or paper money?
text: The time of supply rule for the vendor making the supply is the time that the coins, tokens, paper money or cards are removed from the machine, meter or device.

title: Where can the further particulars prescribed by the Commissioner under section 20(8A)(c) be found?
text: The further particulars prescribed by the Commissioner under section 20(8A)(c) are set out in BGR 63.

title: What documentation must a vendor possess to deduct VAT paid on the importation of goods?
text: A vendor must possess the following documentation: an EDI Customs Status 1 Release Message, a valid bill of entry or other document prescribed by the Customs and Excise Act (such as form SAD 500 and/or any additional SAD document that might be required), and the receipt number for the payment of such tax issued on eFiling.

title: What is the standard due date for VAT payments by EFT, and how does it differ for eFilers?
text: The standard due date for VAT payments by EFT is the 25th of the month. However, for eFilers who submit via eFiling, the due date is extended to the last business day of the month.

title: From what date does the supply of the right of use of ships, aircraft and rolling stock by a non-resident no longer constitute an 'enterprise' in the Republic?
text: From 1 April 2021, the supply by a non-resident of the right of use of ships, aircraft and rolling stock in the specified circumstances will no longer be regarded as an 'enterprise' conducted in the Republic by that person.

title: What VAT tax fraction should be used to calculate the adjustment when H upgrades the office computer in February 2019?
text: The tax fraction 15/115 must be used because the time of supply for the adjustment is after the VAT rate increase from 14% to 15% on 1 April 2018.

title: In Example 6, when would Farmer A have to account for the supply if no payment was received and no invoice was issued by 25 March 2023?
text: If no payment was received and no invoice was issued by 25 March 2023, the time of supply will be at the time that the goods were removed on 10 January 2023. Farmer A will have to account for the supply in the February 2023 return.

title: What types of foreign-owned assets are covered under the cross border leasing provisions in the VAT Act amendment?
text: The cross border leasing provisions cover foreign-owned ships, aircraft, rolling stock and parts directly in connection thereto that are leased for use in the Republic.

title: What are the conditions under which a person can apply for voluntary VAT registration if they supply commercial accommodation?
text: A person can apply for voluntary registration if they supply commercial accommodation, provided the minimum threshold of R120 000 is met.

title: What is the rule when the OMV is less than the consideration paid for second-hand goods?
text: Where the OMV is less than the consideration paid, the OMV will be used to calculate the notional input tax deduction.

title: Where can information about payment limits be found?
text: Information about payment limits for EFT, eFiling, and bank payments can be found in section 10.5.

title: Which of the following expenses can a vendor deduct input tax on: salaries and wages of employees or fees charged by VAT registered consultants?
text: A vendor can deduct input tax on fees charged by VAT registered consultants and other independent contractors, but not on salaries and wages of employees.

title: What is considered a betting transaction?
text: A betting transaction occurs when one person places a sum of money at risk with another person who accepts the money as a bet, based upon the outcome of a race, competition or other event or occurrence where the outcome is uncertain.

title: Where can information about VAT treatment for table games of chance in casinos be found?
text: Information about the calculation of VAT for table games of chance can be found in BGR 59 'Calculation of VAT for table games of chance'.

title: Within what timeframe must the annual adjustment be made according to Note 5?
text: Within six months of the financial year-end

title: What is the maximum purchase amount for which vendors are not obliged to obtain tax invoices for petty cash payments?
text: Vendors are not obliged to obtain tax invoices for purchases not exceeding R50. However, they must keep the till slip, cash slip or sales docket with details of the purchase in a petty cash book or similar record in order to deduct the input tax.

title: What happens to a suspended tax payment if no objection is lodged within the prescribed time period?
text: The suspension is revoked with immediate effect from the date of the expiry of the relevant prescribed time period or any extension of the relevant time period under the TA Act.

title: What supplies can a Category E vendor make and to whom?
text: The supplies by the vendor applying for Category E must be made to a connected person in relation to that vendor and consist solely of the letting of fixed properties, the renting of movable goods, or the administration or management of such companies.

title: What is the physical address of the VRA's Head Office?
text: Plot 206/1 High Road, Pomona, Kempton Park, 1619

title: What is the threshold amount for VAT registration liability based on turnover?
text: R1 million. If the running total of turnover exceeds R1 million in any particular month, a person becomes liable to register for VAT from the first day of the next month.

title: What determines which acts of non-compliance can result in fixed-amount penalties?
text: Fixed-amount penalties may only be imposed in respect of the non-compliance listed in a public notice issued by the Commissioner and not every act of non-compliance under a tax Act.

title: What is the minimum threshold for adjusted cost below which no adjustment is applicable for an increase in the extent of taxable use of capital goods?
text: No adjustment is applicable where the adjusted cost is less than R40 000 (excluding VAT).

title: What is the amount of VAT refundable to M in the export example provided?
text: The VAT refundable to M is R652.17, which is the total VAT charged (R2,152.17) less the notional input tax deducted (R1,500.00), and further reduced by the VRA's commission.

title: When were the amendments to section 72 of the VAT Act introduced and what was their main purpose?
text: The amendments to section 72 were introduced by Act 34 of 2019 with effect from 21 July 2019. The main purpose was to limit the extent of the Commissioner's discretion in making a decision under this section by clarifying the circumstances under which the provision can be invoked and aligning the wording with the construct and policy intent of the VAT Act as a whole.

title: What is the prescribed application fee for an application for a decision under section 72?
text: The prescribed application fee for any application for a decision under section 72 is R2 500, in accordance with section 81 of the TA Act read with VAT Notice 299.

title: What is the compulsory registration threshold for VAT, and when can a vendor apply for cancellation based on this threshold?
text: The compulsory registration threshold is R1 million. A vendor may apply for cancellation of registration if the value of taxable supplies is less than R1 million in any consecutive period of 12 months.

title: What is the VAT treatment of clothes sold by a VAT-registered church at its annual bazaar?
text: The sale of clothes by the church is an exempt supply. Therefore, no input tax may be deducted in making these supplies.

title: What adjustment must a vendor receiving a credit note make?
text: The vendor receiving a credit note must make an adjustment to output tax.

title: What is the standard tax rate at which a supply must be taxable for the DRC to apply?
text: The supply must be taxable at the standard rate, which is currently 15%. Any supply that is zero-rated, such as the export of a valuable metal or the supply of a Krugerrand coin, does not fall within the DRC.

title: How much input tax credit can be deducted for the previously disallowed exempt use portion when the computer system is sold?
text: The input tax credit that can be deducted is R3 600, calculated using the formula: 15/115 × R46 000 × 60%, where 15/115 is the tax fraction, R46 000 is the lesser of the adjusted cost (R54 000) or OMV (R46 000), and 60% represents the extent of exempt use.

title: What are the exceptions to the standard rate application for services related to movable property situated in the RSA?
text: The standard rate does not apply if: the movable property is subsequently exported; the non-resident is not in the RSA when the service is rendered; or the services supplied by the RSA vendor form part of a supply by the non-resident to the recipient vendor in the RSA and the services are acquired wholly for taxable purposes by that recipient.

title: How should a ruling application be submitted to SARS for VAT matters?
text: A ruling application should be submitted to SARS by e-mail to VATRulings@sars.gov.za, headed either 'Application for a VAT Class Ruling' or 'Application for a VAT Ruling' together with the VAT301 form.

title: In Example 20, what are the two divisions housed in ABC Municipality's rented building and what supplies does each make?
text: The two divisions are the public passenger transport division (occupying the ground floor) and the municipal rates division (occupying the first floor). The public passenger transport division makes only exempt supplies and the municipal rates division makes only taxable supplies.

title: How does M obtain and export the painting in Example 33?
text: M collects the painting in the RSA and exports it in person.

title: When are Category D tax returns typically due?
text: Category D returns are usually due every six months, ending on the last day of February and August. However, vendors may apply to the local SARS office to alter the end of the period to another month.

title: Can input tax be deducted on VAT incurred to make out-of-scope supplies?
text: No, input tax may not be deducted on the VAT incurred to make out-of-scope supplies. Supplies of goods or services not specifically exempted under section 12 of the Act are regarded as being out-of-scope supplies.

title: How is VAT calculated when consideration relates only partly to a taxable supply?
text: When consideration relates only partly to a taxable supply of goods or services, VAT is calculated only on that portion of the consideration which is properly attributable to the taxable supply.

title: What is the time limit for claiming a VAT refund from SARS?
text: A VAT refund must be claimed in a VAT return that is submitted within a period of five years from the date that the submission of such VAT return became due.

title: What is the effective date from which the employee of D's Wholesalers is granted the right to use the motor car?
text: 1 March 2023

title: What is the deadline for submitting a VAT201 return and payment if your tax period ends on 31 March?
text: If your tax period ends on 31 March, you have until 25 April to submit the return and payment, or until 30 April if using eFiling to submit your return and make payment electronically.

title: How should sales be calculated when a person operates multiple businesses or business divisions?
text: The sales of all separate components of the enterprise must be added together to determine the total turnover, regardless of whether permission has been granted to have separate VAT registration numbers for each component.

title: Who is responsible for paying VAT on the importation of goods into South Africa?
text: The VAT on importation is payable by the importer, whether that person is a vendor or not.

title: In Example 4, what is the VAT payable or refundable on the payments basis for the January to February 2023 tax period?
text: On the payments basis for January to February 2023, the VAT refundable is R1 500. This is calculated by declaring output tax of R1 500 (on cash sales received of R11 500) and deducting input tax of R3 000 (on full cash payments made for purchases of R23 000), resulting in a refundable amount of R1 500.

title: What is the condition for a local leg of a passenger flight to be zero-rated as part of an international journey?
text: The local leg of a passenger flight that forms part of an international journey by air is zero-rated to the extent that it constitutes 'international carriage' as defined in Article 1 of the Convention set out in the Schedule to the Carriage by Air Act 17 of 1946.

title: What are the four main tax period categories available for VAT purposes?
text: The four tax period categories are: Category A or B (Two-monthly tax period), Category C (Monthly tax period), Category D (Six-monthly tax period), and Category E (Annual tax period).

title: What should vendors do if the 25th day falls on a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday?
text: If the 25th day falls on a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday, the submission must be made on the last working day before such 25th day.

title: What are the three main parts of the Export Regulation?
text: The three main parts are: Part One, which deals with procedures for qualifying purchasers when VAT has been charged at the standard rate on movable goods to be exported; Part Two, which deals with procedures for suppliers electing to supply movable goods at the zero rate (split into Section A for goods delivered to designated harbours/airports or supplied via pipeline/electrical transmission, and Section B for goods exported by road or rail through a designated commercial port); and Part Three, which deals with time periods for exporting goods, the responsible party, and time periods for obtaining documentary proof.

title: What topic is discussed in section 3.2.2?
text: Section 3.2.2 discusses change of circumstances after registration.

title: What is the deadline for submitting a VAT return and payment when using eFiling?
text: The deadline for submitting a VAT return and payment when using eFiling of return and payment via either SARS eFiling or EFT (internet banking) is the last business day of the month.

title: What is the standard VAT rating for the management of a superannuation scheme?
text: The management of a superannuation scheme is standard rated.

title: Under what conditions does the output tax adjustment not apply?
text: The output tax adjustment does not apply if the goods or services were acquired from another vendor that is a wholly-owned member of the same 'group of companies' for income tax purposes as the acquiring vendor, for as long as both the vendors are wholly-owned members of the same 'group of companies', and such other vendor has not made an input tax deduction for an irrecoverable debt in respect of the outstanding amount.

title: What documentation is required for the importation of goods under VAT?
text: A bill of entry or other prescribed customs documentation is required, including the receipt for the payment of the VAT to Customs (the receipt number on eFiling). If the bill of entry or documentation is held by the vendor's agent, the vendor must be in possession of a statement from the agent containing the receipt number for the payment of the VAT on importation issued on eFiling.

title: What additional information must be clearly shown on a tax invoice for supplies of valuable metal subject to the DRC?
text: A tax invoice for supplies of valuable metal subject to the DRC must clearly show that: the supply is subject to the DRC; the VAT charged on the supply must not be included in the amount shown as due by the recipient (no VAT charge must be shown on the tax invoice); and a statement that the amount of VAT charged must be accounted for and paid by the recipient on behalf of the supplier.

title: Can persons making only exempt supplies register for VAT and recover input tax?
text: No. Persons making only exempt supplies may not register for VAT and may not recover input tax on purchases to make exempt supplies.

title: What does the term 'flash title' refer to in the context of export regulations?
text: The term 'flash title' refers to a situation in which movable goods are supplied to a qualifying purchaser and that person immediately supplies the same goods to another qualifying purchaser. Ownership of the goods therefore vests in the first qualifying purchaser only for a moment before being sold to another qualifying purchaser.

title: What are the three conditions SARS must be satisfied with before issuing a VAT ruling for alternative documentary proof?
text: SARS will only issue the ruling if satisfied that: (1) the vendor has taken all reasonable steps to obtain the prescribed documentary evidence and is applying for the ruling as a last resort; (2) the inability to obtain and maintain the prescribed documentation was due to circumstances beyond the vendor's control; and (3) no other provision of the VAT Act allows for a deduction based on the particular document in the vendor's possession.

title: What is required before a vendor can apply the payments basis of accounting?
text: A vendor must apply in writing to SARS before being allowed to apply the payments basis.

title: What change was made to Section 20(8) regarding repossessions and surrender of goods?
text: Section 20(8) has been amended to apply only to the supply of second-hand goods. Section 20(8A) has been introduced to specifically deal with the documentary requirements for repossessions or surrender of goods under section 8(10).

title: What is the first step before attempting to apportion an expense for VAT purposes?
text: The first step is to determine if the expense can be directly attributed. Direct attribution means that you will be required to attribute the VAT expense according to the intended purpose for which the goods or services acquired will be used.

title: What adjustment must be made if the temporary letting period exceeds 12 months or the property is permanently applied for a non-taxable purpose?
text: An adjustment must be made under section 18(1) and not under section 18D(2).

title: What documentation must a vendor retain when acquiring second-hand goods?
text: The vendor must retain a proper record of the details of the transaction on VAT264 Form.

title: How do facilitated supplies by intermediaries affect VAT registration thresholds for non-resident electronic service suppliers?
text: If a non-resident supplier of electronic services facilitates supplies of any other non-resident supplier of electronic services as an intermediary on a platform, the value of those facilitated supplies counts towards the calculation of the R1 million compulsory VAT registration threshold, but only when those other suppliers are not registered for VAT in South Africa at the time of supply.

title: What documentary requirement was removed under section 20(8A) for repossession and surrender of goods under an ICA?
text: The creditor is no longer required to obtain a signed VAT264 declaration.

title: How is the taxable supplies percentage calculated using the turnover-based method for Company J?
text: The taxable supplies percentage is calculated as: Shop rental (R576 000) divided by total income (R771 000) × 100 = 75%.

title: When is a vendor registered under the monthly tax period (Category C)?
text: A vendor is registered under Category C when their turnover exceeds or is likely to exceed R30 million in any consecutive period of 12 months. If a person operates more than one business or a business with different divisions, branches or separate enterprise activities, the sales of all separate components must be added together to determine total turnover.

title: What must happen if an agreement is reached during the ADR process?
text: If an agreement or settlement is reached, the facilitator must record the terms of the agreement. It must then be recorded and signed by you and a SARS representative. SARS will issue a revised assessment where necessary to give effect to the agreement reached.

title: Which institutions can provide exempt lodging or boarding services?
text: Institutions such as schools, universities, colleges or universities of technology can provide exempt lodging or boarding services.

title: What is the standard timeframe within which SARS must process a refund after receiving a correctly completed VAT return?
text: SARS should make the refund within 21 business days of receiving a correctly completed VAT return. If the refund is not paid within this period, interest will be paid to the vendor at the prescribed rate.

title: What form must be used when lodging an objection with SARS?
text: The objection must be in the form prescribed by the Commissioner, which is the DISP01 – Notice of Objection form, with all requested information completed.

title: What conditions must be met for VAT incurred on the acquisition of fixed property to be deductible?
text: VAT incurred on the acquisition of fixed property is deductible to the extent that payment towards the purchase price has been made during the tax period and the time of supply has been triggered.

title: What amendment was made to section 50(1) regarding Foreign Donor Funded Projects?
text: Section 50(1) was amended to require the project to be registered as a separate entity from the main enterprise activities of the implementing agency.

title: What are the main topics covered in Chapter 14 of the VAT 404 Guide?
text: Chapter 14 covers Assessments, objections and appeals. It includes sections on the purpose and types of assessments, dispute resolution procedures with the Tax Administration, objection and appeal processes, the office of the Tax Ombud, and alternative dispute resolution methods.

title: What must a vendor do if they were registered for VAT voluntarily under section 23(3)(b)(ii) and reach the R50 000 threshold?
text: A vendor that was allowed to register for VAT voluntarily under section 23(3)(b)(ii) without making taxable supplies of R50 000 must migrate to the invoice basis once the R50 000 threshold is reached. However, the vendor may remain on the payments basis provided that it falls within one of the entities who qualify to be on the payments basis even after reaching the threshold.

title: When is a credit note not required due to a prompt payment discount?
text: A credit note is not required when a prompt payment (settlement) discount is the reason for the reduction in the consideration, provided the terms of that discount are clearly shown on the tax invoice.

title: What was the applicable interest rate per annum used in this example?
text: 10,25% per annum

title: Can input tax be deducted on VAT paid for membership fees to sporting and recreational clubs?
text: No, input tax may not be deducted on VAT paid in respect of any membership fees to sporting, recreational and private clubs, such as country clubs, soccer supporters clubs, amateur boxing clubs, holiday clubs, tea clubs, and stokvel savings clubs.

title: What is the first step when determining VAT deductibility for a vendor that conducts both taxable and non-taxable activities?
text: The first step is to determine whether the expense is incurred wholly for taxable, exempt or other non-taxable purposes.

title: Are services rendered by an employee to an employer considered enterprise activities for VAT purposes?
text: No, services rendered by an employee to an employer, such as salary, wage, or remuneration earnings, are not considered enterprise activities and will therefore not attract VAT. However, this must be distinguished from a private independent contractor who is not excluded.

title: What does the term 'goods' include according to the VAT 404 Guide?
text: The term 'goods' includes corporeal (tangible) movable things, fixed property and land and buildings (including any real right in the property), sectional title units (including timeshare), shares in a share block company, postage stamps, electricity, and second-hand goods.

title: What is the domestic reverse charge (DRC) and which vendors are affected by it?
text: The DRC Regulations applies to all registered vendors that buy and sell gold and goods containing gold in the specified forms as provided in the definition of 'valuable metal'. Examples of affected vendors are mines, dealers in gold, wholesale and retail jewellers, and coin shops.

title: What is the date as of which the operational information in the VAT 404 Guide for Vendors is current?
text: The operational information contained in the guide is up to date as at 17 December 2024.

title: What does the shortfall represent in VAT purposes?
text: The shortfall is an expression of the prejudice to the fiscus. It is calculated as the difference between the VAT that should have been paid and the VAT that was actually paid.

title: What is the threshold amount that determines whether a full tax invoice or abridged tax invoice should be issued?
text: A full tax invoice is required for consideration of R5 000 or more, while an abridged tax invoice is used for consideration less than R5 000.

title: Under what condition can supplies made by public authorities be treated as 'enterprise' activities for VAT purposes?
text: For supplies made by public authorities to be treated as 'enterprise' activities, the public authority concerned has to be notified in that regard by the Commissioner, acting on instruction from the Minister. The Minister must be satisfied that the supplies (or certain of them) are of the same kind or similar to taxable supplies already being made, or which might be made by vendors in the private sector.

title: What types of amounts should be excluded when calculating debtors and creditors balances during an accounting basis change?
text: Amounts that should be excluded are: any amounts included in debtors and creditors which do not include VAT at the standard rate (for example, debtors and creditors for exempt or zero-rated supplies) and amounts payable to creditors which relate to non-permissible expenses.

title: What is excluded from the zero-rate treatment for welfare organisations receiving subsidies?
text: The zero-rate treatment excludes any payment for actual supplies made to the public authority or municipality, or payment for a specific supply made to a third person on behalf of that public authority or municipality.

title: What are the circumstances under which the Commissioner will be satisfied that a person can reasonably be expected to make taxable supplies in excess of R50 000?
text: The Commissioner will be satisfied where one or more of the following circumstances exist: if you have made taxable supplies for more than two months with an average exceeding R4 200 per month; if you made taxable supplies for only one month with a value exceeding R4 200; if you have a written contractual obligation to make taxable supplies in excess of R50 000 in the 12-month period following registration; or if you have acquired finance from specified credit providers where total repayments in the 12 months following the date of application will exceed R50 000.

title: Who generally bears the burden of proof in disputes under the new rules and why?
text: The burden of proof generally lies with the vendor because the assessment is essentially based on facts within the particular knowledge of the vendor.

title: What is the minimum value of taxable supplies that must have been made in the preceding 12-month period for a going concern to qualify for VAT registration?
text: The value of the taxable supplies made from carrying on that enterprise (or part thereof) by the supplier of the going concern must have exceeded R50 000 in the preceding 12-month period.

title: What is the timeframe within which SARS must pay refunds?
text: SARS must pay refunds within 21 business days of receiving the correctly completed refund return.

title: Name two activities listed in GN 446 for which a person may apply to register voluntarily if taxable supplies can only be made after a certain period of time.
text: Agriculture, farming, forestry and fisheries, and mining of minerals, metal, oil, gas or natural gas resources (including both exploration and extraction) are two activities listed in GN 446.

title: When is a deposit considered a payment for VAT purposes?
text: A deposit is not considered to be 'any payment' until the seller is able to apply that payment as consideration for the supply.

title: When must a vendor make an adjustment to output or input tax according to section 9.6.1?
text: A vendor must make an adjustment to output or input tax if the extent to which capital goods or services used to make taxable supplies increases or decreases, or if goods or services are applied wholly for a different purpose to the intended purpose when acquired, manufactured, assembled, constructed or produced. This includes stock items or capital assets taken from the business for own use, or for exempt or other non-taxable purposes.

title: In Example 35, why are the technical license fees paid by ABC to the United Kingdom company not considered 'imported services'?
text: The technical license fees are not considered 'imported services' because the services are wholly consumed in the course of making taxable supplies (manufacturing and selling ball valves).

title: When do the amendments to section 8(8) and 8(8A) come into effect?
text: The amendments come into effect from 1 January 2024.

title: What conditions must be met for a vendor to deduct input tax?
text: Input tax may only be deducted by the recipient vendor if the goods or services are acquired for making taxable supplies and if the vendor is in possession of the relevant documentary proof under section 16(2)(a) to (e). Additionally, an apportionment of input tax must be made if goods or services are acquired only partly for making taxable supplies.

title: What are the three types of commercial accommodation?
text: The three types of commercial accommodation are: (1) Lodging or board and lodging together with domestic goods and services in any house, flat, apartment, room, hotel, motel, inn, guesthouse residential establishment, holiday accommodation unit, chalet, tent, caravan, campsite, houseboat or similar establishment supplied regularly and systematically, excluding a 'dwelling' supplied for letting or hiring; (2) Lodging or board and lodging in a home for the aged, children, physically or mentally handicapped persons; and (3) Lodging or board and lodging in a hospice.

title: Which of the following are examples of standard-rated supplies: professional services, exempt supplies, or zero-rated supplies?
text: Professional services are examples of standard-rated supplies. These include construction/building, estate agents, consultants, architects, engineers, project managers, doctors, private hospital services, lawyers, plumbers, electricians and accountants.

title: On what basis is an adjustment made to a vendor's output tax when there is a decrease in the extent to which capital goods or services are used in making taxable supplies?
text: The adjustment is made on an annual basis.

title: What information about the recipient must appear on a tax invoice?
text: The name, address and VAT registration number of the recipient must appear on a tax invoice.

title: How much output tax must H account for in the February 2019 VAT return for the computer upgrade?
text: H must account for output tax of R300, calculated as R2 300 × 15/115, which must be entered in field 12 of the February 2019 VAT return.

title: Does a payment held in trust by an estate agent or attorney constitute payment made to the seller?
text: No, a payment that is held in trust by an estate agent or attorney does not constitute payment made, as the seller cannot apply the amount against the outstanding debt at that time.

title: Which system is used to facilitate the administrative process of receiving applications for a decision under section 72?
text: The ATR eFiling system is used to facilitate the administrative process of receiving applications for a decision under section 72, including the levying and receipt of the required application fee.

title: What is a jeopardy assessment and when may it be issued?
text: A jeopardy assessment, also known as a protective assessment, may be issued in advance of the date on which a return is normally due in order to secure the collection of tax that would otherwise be in jeopardy, or where there is danger of tax being lost by delay. Examples include when a vendor tries to place assets beyond the reach of SARS or when a tax debtor is about to leave the RSA and will leave behind a tax debt.

title: Under what circumstances can a VAT Ruling regarding apportionment methods be appealed?
text: A VAT Ruling can be subject to appeal if it concerns the refusal by the Commissioner to approve a special method of apportionment proposed by a vendor for mixed purposes, when the standard method prescribed in BGR 16 is not fair and reasonable in the circumstances.

title: What is a 'substantial understatement' for purposes of USP imposition?
text: A substantial understatement is when USP could be imposed if the prejudice to SARS is the greater of 5% of the tax properly chargeable or R1 million, in cases where none of the specific behaviours can be identified.

title: What is the VATSA and what does it help vendors do?
text: The VATSA contains information which will empower vendors to manage their VAT accounts by giving them insight into their transactions per tax period.

title: What is the rule for determining consideration in a supply made to connected persons for no consideration or below OMV?
text: The consideration for the supply is equal to the OMV if the recipient would not have been entitled to a full input tax deduction on the goods or services acquired, had the OMV been charged in respect of that supply. This rule does not apply to fringe benefits to employees who are not connected persons, as other special rules apply in those situations.

title: What methods can a vendor use to lodge an objection to an assessment as of 14 October 2016?
text: With effect from 14 October 2016, a vendor can lodge an objection only on eFiling or by visiting a SARS branch.

title: Under the payments basis, when is VAT accounted for?
text: Under the payments basis, VAT is only accounted for on actual payments made and actual payments received in respect of taxable supplies during the period. The date to account for VAT is delayed until payment has been made.

title: What are the business hours for contacting SARS Practitioners?
text: SARS Practitioners can be contacted on weekdays from 8:00 – 16:00 (except Wednesdays) and on Wednesdays from 9:00 – 16:00.

title: Why does SARS deny VAT registration applications from persons who have not yet been awarded a tender?
text: SARS denies these applications because the relevant provisions of the VAT Act would not yet have been met. Additionally, registering persons in anticipation of being awarded a tender places an unnecessary administrative burden on SARS and the person concerned.

title: What are the two requirements that must be met for an association not for gain to register a branch, division or enterprise separately for VAT?
text: The separate branch, division or enterprise must maintain an independent accounting system and can be separately identified by the nature of its activities or its geographic location.

title: When is the time of supply for goods or services supplied by a South African branch to an offshore establishment?
text: The time of supply is when the goods are consigned or physically delivered to the offshore establishment or when the services are rendered to the offshore establishment.

title: What is the timeframe for the Commissioner to request additional substantiating documents from a vendor regarding an objection?
text: Within 30 days from the date of delivery of the objection, the Commissioner may request that additional substantiating documents be provided.

title: What automatic suspension applies when a vendor requests remittance of an administrative non-compliance penalty?
text: There is an automatic suspension of the duty to pay and SARS's right to collect, which will run from the day the application is submitted until 21 business days after a decision is taken not to remit the administrative penalty.

title: What is the current percentage of the penalty imposed when a vendor fails to pay VAT within the allowed payment period?
text: A 10% penalty is imposed when a vendor fails to pay VAT within the period allowed for payment. This percentage is prescribed in section 39 of the VAT Act.

title: Can input tax be deducted on expenses attributable to making exempt supplies?
text: No, no input tax may be deducted on any expenses attributable to making exempt supplies.

title: What are the two channels through which a vendor can submit a request for remission of the non-compliance penalty as of 14 October 2016?
text: As of 14 October 2016, a request for remission of the non-compliance penalty can only be made via two channels: eFiling or by visiting a SARS branch.

title: How long should you allow for your VAT registration application to be processed?
text: You should allow at least 21 business days for your application to be processed.

title: What rights does a vendor have after an audit concludes?
text: At the conclusion of an audit, a notice is required to be issued explaining any proposed material adjustments to be made and the vendor has the right to respond to the notice within 21 business days.

title: What types of accommodation and hospitality services are subject to VAT?
text: Restaurant meals, hotel accommodation, liquor sales, arcade amusements, casino slot machines and gambling services are subject to VAT. For more information, see VAT 411 – Guide for Entertainment Accommodation and Catering.

title: Who is eligible to account for VAT on the payments basis?
text: The payments basis is only available to: vendors who are natural persons, certain vendors that have been allowed to register voluntarily in accordance with section 23(3)(b)(ii) (until the R50 000 threshold is met), and the South African Broadcasting Corporation as contemplated in section 8A of the Broadcasting Act 4 of 1999. Juristic persons (such as companies) and trust funds do not qualify for the payments basis unless they are one of the types of entities listed above.

title: What are the four main requirements that must all be met for the DRC to apply?
text: The four requirements are: (1) The supplier is a registered vendor, (2) The recipient is a registered vendor, (3) The goods and ancillary goods or services supplied must fall within the meaning of 'valuable metal' as defined in the DRC Regulations, and (4) The supply is taxable at the standard rate, which is currently 15%.

title: What types of supplies does the public passenger transport division make?
text: The public passenger transport division makes only exempt supplies.

title: When must adjustments to VAT be accounted for following the issuance of a credit note?
text: Adjustments must be accounted for in the VAT return for the tax period in which the decrease in consideration occurs, that is, in the tax period in which the credit note is issued.

title: When is a resident recipient required to account for VAT on imported services?
text: A resident recipient has to account for VAT on imported services when the recipient is not a registered vendor, is a vendor but the services are wholly or partly for making exempt supplies, or is a vendor but the services are applied for private purposes. Additionally, if the value of any particular supply exceeds R100 and the services are acquired wholly or partly for non-taxable purposes, VAT on importation must be paid to SARS.

title: Why cannot you apply the tax fractions 14/114 or 15/115 to the full VAT-inclusive balances?
text: Because it will not correspond to the actual VAT that you have charged to customers or that you have been charged by suppliers.

title: Why is there no input tax deducted by customers on the R1,50 VAT charged by the supermarket?
text: Because the supermarket's customers are final consumers and are not registered for VAT.

title: When did the updated Electronic Services Regulation come into effect and what was expanded?
text: The updated Electronic Services Regulation came into effect on 1 April 2019. The previous scope of 'electronic services' was expanded to include any supplies of electronic services, subject to a few exceptions such as telecommunications services, certain group company transactions and educational services.

title: When is a suspension of disputed tax payment revoked?
text: The suspension of disputed tax payment is revoked with immediate effect from the date of the expiry of the relevant prescribed time period or any extension of the relevant time period under the TA Act if: no objection is lodged; an objection is disallowed and no appeal is lodged; or an appeal to the tax board or court is unsuccessful and no further appeal is noted.

title: What are the three conditions that must be met for the Export Regulations to apply to progressive supplies spanning 2 May 2014?
text: The three conditions are: (1) The goods are subject to processes contemplated in section 9(3)(b), (2) The invoice is issued and/or the payment is received before and after 2 May 2014, and (3) Delivery of the goods takes place after 2 May 2014.

title: What is the threshold amount for total repayments within 12 months following the date of application for acquired finance from specified credit providers?
text: The total repayments in the 12 months following the date of application must exceed R50 000.

title: What is the current standard VAT rate applied to taxable supplies?
text: The current standard VAT rate is 15%.

title: Under what conditions can input tax credit be claimed for personal subsistence expenses?
text: Input tax credit can be claimed for personal subsistence only if the entertainment is acquired by the vendor and relates to a meal, refreshment or accommodation consumed or enjoyed by the vendor or the vendor's employee in carrying out the vendor's business, in respect of any night that the vendor or employee must be away on business from his or her normal place of work and residence. However, no input tax credit is allowed in respect of an allowance paid to the employee to cover such expenses.

title: What is the deadline for the Commissioner to make a decision on an objection after receiving it?
text: The Commissioner must, within 60 days from the receipt of the objection, or within 45 days from the date that the requested further information has been delivered, take a decision regarding the grounds of the objection.

title: What threshold applies to voluntary registration for commercial accommodation in hospices or homes for the aged?
text: The normal R50 000 threshold for voluntary registration applies to commercial accommodation in hospices or homes for the aged, rather than the R120 000 threshold.

title: What is Chapter 14 about?
text: Chapter 14 covers Assessments, objections and appeals.

title: What are the contact options available if you need further information or clarification on VAT matters?
text: You can visit the SARS website, contact the SARS National Service Centre (0800 00 7277 for local calls or +27 11 602 2093 from abroad, between 8am and 4.30pm South African time except Wednesdays when available from 9am to 4.30pm), have a virtual consultation with a SARS consultant by appointment via the SARS website, contact your own tax advisors, submit legal interpretative queries to TAAInfo@sars.gov.za, or submit a ruling application to VATRulings@sars.gov.za.

title: What is the timeframe within which a recipient vendor must issue a statement to the supplier of valuable metal?
text: The recipient vendor must issue a statement containing the prescribed details in writing to the vendor that supplied the valuable metal within 21 days from the end of the calendar month during which the VAT on the supply of valuable metal has been accounted and paid for by the recipient vendor.

title: Where can information about the specific welfare activities that qualify for VAT purposes be found?
text: The welfare activities are determined by the Minister for purposes of the VAT Act, and the regulations issued under GN 112 in GG 27235 of 11 February 2005 contain the welfare activities regulations.

title: What are the consequences if SARS pays an undue VAT refund to a vendor?
text: The payment of an undue refund by SARS to a vendor is regarded as an outstanding tax debt due by the vendor from the date on which the refund amount was paid. Interest accrues and is payable by the vendor on the amount not properly payable to the vendor, from the date of payment of such amount by SARS.

title: What is a taxable supply according to the VAT 404 Guide?
text: A taxable supply is a supply (including a zero-rated supply) which is chargeable with tax under the VAT Act. A taxable supply does not include any exempt supply listed in section 12, even if supplied by a registered vendor. There are two types of taxable supplies: those which attract the zero rate (listed in section 11) and those on which the standard rate must be charged.

title: Can a vendor deduct VAT incurred on subscriptions to magazines and trade journals?
text: Yes, VAT incurred on subscriptions to magazines and trade journals which are related in a direct manner to the nature of the enterprise carried on by the vendor may be deducted as input tax.

title: Under an Instalment Credit Agreement, when must suppliers account for the full amount of output tax?
text: Suppliers of taxable goods under an Instalment Credit Agreement must account for the full amount of output tax in the tax period in which the goods are delivered or any payment of the consideration is received, whichever is the earlier, irrespective of the accounting basis on which they are registered.

title: Under what conditions does BGR 15 allow recipients to issue tax invoices and credit/debit notes?
text: BGR 15 allows recipients to issue tax invoices, credit and debit notes when the recipient determines the consideration for the supply of goods or services and is in control of determining the quantity or quality of the supply, or is responsible for measuring or testing the goods sold by the supplier. This is on condition that the parties to the transaction comply with the requirements set out in BGR 15.

title: What is the maximum duration for a temporarily applied letting period?
text: The temporarily applied letting period must not exceed a period of 12 months, whether as a fixed or continuous period, or in aggregate, per dwelling unit.

title: What special consideration applies to imported goods when claiming input tax?
text: Certain business inputs are subject to special rules and limitations, or may be specifically denied. Separate lists must be made for VAT paid in respect of capital and other goods imported, as these amounts must be reflected separately on the return.

title: How much input tax can ABC Bank deduct on the computer software purchase?
text: ABC Bank can deduct R36 000 as input tax, calculated as [(R460 000 × 15 / 115) × 60%] = R60 000 × 60% = R36 000.

title: Under what circumstances can SARS withhold a refund without paying interest?
text: SARS may withhold a refund in several exceptional circumstances: when there is a defect in or incompleteness of a VAT return that affects the refund amount (until rectified), when bank account details have not been provided, when a vendor has outstanding VAT returns to file, when a refund will be refunded by the vendor to another party where that party bears the output tax, or when a verification, inspection or audit of the refund has not been finalised (unless acceptable security is provided).

title: Under what circumstances can VAT refunds be paid into a third party bank account?
text: Only non-resident companies and resident companies that are part of the same group structure may request that VAT refunds be paid into a bank account of a nominated person. In the case of a subsidiary company or holding company as contemplated in the Companies Act, VAT refunds may be paid into the bank account of either entity, on condition that SARS is indemnified against any loss. The use of third party bank accounts will not be permitted in any other cases.

title: What is an example of an out-of-scope supply involving the sale of property?
text: When a property developer sells his private residence, the sale does not form part of his enterprise and no VAT must be charged on the sale of the property. Instead, the purchaser will have to pay transfer duty on acquisition of the property, subject to any exemptions or exceptions which may be applicable.

title: When does a person become liable to register for VAT after their turnover exceeds the threshold?
text: A person becomes liable to register from the first day of the next month after the running total of turnover exceeds R1 million in any particular month.

title: What is required for a vendor to obtain relief under the voluntary disclosure programme?
text: For relief to be granted under the voluntary disclosure programme (VDP), the vendor must voluntarily disclose the default by submitting a valid VDP application in the prescribed form. Relief is only granted if the outcome of the VDP application is positive, and successful applicants will be required to enter into a VDP agreement with SARS.

title: How will the Notice of Registration be issued to you?
text: The Notice of Registration is issued to you on eFiling if you are a registered eFiler, or it will be sent to your email address, or posted to the postal address given on your registration application.

title: Can a vendor backdate a request for a change of category?
text: No, a request for a change of category can only be implemented with effect from a future date and cannot be backdated, except in two instances: if the wrong category was captured in the registration process (with proof that SARS made an error), or if the vendor's circumstances have changed such that registration in another category is required.

title: What are the four BLNS countries mentioned in the context of VAT imports?
text: The BLNS countries are Botswana, Lesotho, Namibia, and Swaziland (eSwatini).

title: When was the revenue threshold for commercial accommodation enterprises increased to R120 000?
text: The threshold was increased from R60 000 to R120 000 with effect from 1 April 2017.

title: What is alternative dispute resolution (ADR) and what is required for it to apply?
text: ADR is a form of dispute resolution that is quicker and more informal than litigation or adjudication through the courts. ADR is a voluntary process and both parties have to agree to the process before it can apply to the resolution of a dispute. If you wish to use ADR, you must indicate this in your Notice of Appeal (NOA).

title: What VAT amount must C declare in the VAT201 return for the tax period ending February 2019?
text: C must declare the VAT amount of R36 939 in field 12 of the VAT201 return for the tax period ending February 2019.

title: What must a vendor do when changing their accounting basis according to VAT 404?
text: The vendor must make the necessary changes to the registered particulars on eFiling and include the adjustment on the return for the period concerned. Vendors need to keep the calculation and a list of debtors and creditors as part of their records and submit same on eFiling (if requested) or to the SARS office where that vendor is registered.

title: What are the main duties and responsibilities that vendors must perform under VAT 404?
text: Vendors must ensure that VAT is collected on taxable transactions, returns are submitted and payments made on time, tax invoices are issued where required, and that all prices advertised or quoted include VAT.

title: Which land border post is listed for Zimbabwe?
text: Beit Bridge is the land border post listed for Zimbabwe.

title: What should vendors ensure before implementing an electronic system to replace their paper-based documentary systems?
text: Vendors wishing to implement an electronic system must ensure that they do not replace their existing paper-based documentary systems before ensuring that they meet all the requirements.

title: Under what conditions may the Commissioner grant permission for tax invoices not to be issued or for information on tax invoices to vary from standard requirements?
text: The Commissioner may grant permission for tax invoices not to be issued or for information to vary from standard requirements if the Commissioner is satisfied that there will be sufficient records and that it will be impractical for a tax invoice to be issued.

title: What is the general time period within which movable goods must be exported from the Republic?
text: Generally, goods must be exported within 90 days from the earlier of the time an invoice is issued or the time any payment of consideration is received by the supplier.

title: How is the VAT calculated for ABC Rugby Club in this barter transaction example?
text: The VAT is calculated using the formula 15/115 × R15 000, which equals R1 957. This is calculated on the total consideration received of R15 000.

title: What additional information must a debit- or credit note issued by a supplier of valuable metal under the DRC contain?
text: A debit- or credit note issued by a supplier of valuable metal under the DRC must clearly show that: the supply is subject to the DRC; a statement that the increase in the VAT amount in the case of a debit note that was previously accounted for and paid by the recipient must be accounted for and paid by such recipient; and a statement that the decrease in the VAT amount in the case of a credit note that was previously accounted for and paid by the recipient must be accounted for and deducted by such recipient in its VAT return.

title: Under what conditions may ABC (Pty) Ltd levy VAT at the zero rate on the sale of movable goods to XYZ Merchants Ltd?
text: ABC (Pty) Ltd may elect to levy VAT at the zero rate on the sale of movable goods to XYZ Merchants Ltd on the basis that the supplier ensured that the goods were delivered to the vessel concerned which was situated at the Durban harbour (being a designated harbour) and from where the goods will be exported.

title: What is the definition of 'municipality' for VAT purposes?
text: The definition of 'municipality' in the VAT Act refers to the definition contained in section 1 of the Income Tax Act, which makes reference to section 12(1) of the Local Government: Municipal Structures Act. In effect, the term 'municipality' means the category of municipalities as contemplated in section 155 of the Constitution, which deals with the establishment of municipalities.

title: What happens to the title, costs, and risks of granite once it is loaded onto the vessel in the BF to BB transaction?
text: Once the granite has been loaded onto the vessel (delivered under FOB), the title, costs and risk passes from BF to BB and immediately from BB to its various customers.

title: What must be done before submitting a VAT refund claim for exported movable goods?
text: The goods must first be declared to a customs official at the exit point before submitting the claim for a refund of the VAT paid to the VRA.

title: What is the threshold for adjusted cost below which no adjustment is applicable for a decrease in extent of taxable use of capital goods or services?
text: No adjustment is applicable where the adjusted cost is less than R40 000 (excluding VAT).

title: How is the adjustment for irrecoverable debts calculated?
text: The adjustment is calculated by applying the tax fraction which applied at the time of supply to the amount actually written off.

title: How much input tax can the stationery distributor deduct in total?
text: The stationery distributor may deduct input tax of R16,50, which consists of R12,00 VAT incurred on the purchase of the paper rolls and R4,50 VAT incurred on the purchase of packaging boxes.

title: What must happen if a person has been incorrectly registered for VAT in anticipation of being awarded a tender that is not awarded to them?
text: The person would have to de-register from VAT if the tender is not awarded to them.

title: Who is supplying the educational services in this situation?
text: The University of Wales, a non-resident, is supplying the educational services.

title: What are the conditions under which the five-year deduction period applies?
text: The five-year deduction period applies when: a tax invoice for a standard rated supply should have been issued; imported goods were entered for home consumption under the Customs and Excise Act; second-hand goods were acquired or goods were repossessed or surrendered in terms of an ICA; a vendor was provided with a statement by its agent in respect of purchases or importation on behalf of that vendor; or a vendor first became entitled to such deduction in any other case.

title: What is the prescribed period for lodging an appeal after receiving notice of SARS's decision on an objection?
text: The appeal must be lodged within the prescribed period of 30 business days of receiving notice of SARS's decision in respect of the objection.

title: What is the minimum annual revenue threshold for commercial accommodation establishments to be considered an enterprise?
text: The total annual receipts from the activity of supplying commercial accommodation must exceed R120 000 or be reasonably expected to exceed that amount in a period of 12 months, for the activity to be an enterprise.

title: What types of passenger transportation are exempt from VAT?
text: Passenger transportation supplied in the course of a transport business (taxi operators/bus and rail companies) in a vehicle operated by the supplier of the transportation service or a person acting as the supplier's agent is exempt from VAT. The exemption includes various types of commuter transport, such as suburban and mainline trains, mainline and luxury bus travelling services, and taxis.

title: When must a vendor accepting bets account for output tax on betting transactions?
text: A vendor accepting bets in the course or furtherance of an enterprise must account for output tax on the consideration received in respect of all betting transactions for which payment has actually been received in the tax period concerned, whether the vendor accounts for VAT on the invoice or payments basis.

title: What is the current standard VAT rate that must be included in all prices charged by vendors?
text: The current standard VAT rate is 15%. All prices charged, advertised or quoted by a vendor must include VAT at this applicable rate for standard-rated supplies.

title: What is the separate process for applications under section 72, and where can information about it be found?
text: Applications for a decision under section 72 is a separate process. Information regarding this process can be found in Binding General Ruling (BGR) 56 'Application for a Decision under Section 72' and the VAT Section 72 Decision Process Reference Guide on the SARS website.

title: What is the special rule for input tax deduction on fixed property supplies?
text: The recipient may only deduct input tax to the extent that payment of the consideration has been made, provided the time of supply has been triggered. These supplies are treated as if on the payments basis.

title: How should terms defined in a tax Act be interpreted when there is inconsistency with the TA Act?
text: If a tax Act uses a term that is defined in that tax Act, that meaning must be applied. This rule also applies when there is an inconsistency between a term used in a tax Act and the TA Act. However, this rule will not apply if the context in which the term is used in the TA Act indicates that the TA Act meaning must be applied.

title: When does a developer account for output tax on the sale of a dwelling if a written sale agreement was concluded during the temporary letting period but the transfer occurs after the 12-month period expires?
text: The developer accounts for output tax on the sale of the dwelling at the time contemplated in section 9(3)(d), which is on the date of registration of transfer of the property in the Deeds Registry or the date on which any payment in respect of the consideration for the supply is made, whichever is earlier.

title: What are the four main requirements that must all be met for the DRC to apply?
text: The four main requirements are: (1) The supplier is a registered vendor, (2) The recipient is a registered vendor, (3) The goods and ancillary goods or services supplied must fall within the meaning of 'valuable metal' as defined in the DRC Regulations, and (4) The supply is taxable at the standard rate, which is currently 15%.

title: Where can information on documentation to be retained by the agent be found?
text: Information on the documentation to be retained by the agent can be found in BGR 69.

title: What must a debit note or credit note explain regarding the amount altered?
text: A brief explanation of the circumstances giving rise to the debit or credit note must be provided, along with the amount by which the value of the supply and the VAT charged has been altered, or where applicable, the amount by which the consideration has been reduced and either the difference in VAT or a statement that the adjustment includes an amount of tax and the rate of the tax included.

title: Which of the following financial services are typically exempt from VAT: the provision of credit, currency exchange fees, and life insurance policies?
text: The provision of credit and life insurance policies are exempt financial services. However, currency exchange is exempt only for the exchange itself (such as the rand value given for dollars), while any fee charged for the exchange service is taxable at the standard rate.

title: What information must be included on a debit note or credit note regarding the date?
text: The date on which the debit note or credit note is issued must be included.

title: Which tax fraction must be used to calculate VAT on the adjustment and why?
text: The tax fraction 15/115 must be used to calculate VAT on the adjustment because the time of supply for the adjustment is after the VAT rate increase from 14% to 15% on 1 April 2018.

title: What are examples of domestic goods and services supplied with commercial accommodation?
text: Examples of domestic goods and services supplied with commercial accommodation include: cleaning and maintenance, electricity, gas, air conditioning or heating, use of a telephone, television set, radio or other similar article, furniture and other fittings, meals, laundry, nursing services, and water. This list is not exhaustive.

title: What exception exists for genuine client promotions regarding entertainment expense deductions?
text: For genuine client promotions, the entertainment does not have to be supplied at a charge that covers costs or equates to OMV, provided the entertainment is of the same sort as that normally provided for a charge (for example, two milkshakes for the price of one).

title: What method is used to calculate the proportion of taxable use?
text: The apportionment ratio is calculated to determine the proportion of taxable use, which can be determined using the turnover-based method or other specifically approved methods.

title: What factor is applied to calculate consideration in money when input tax on acquisition of a vehicle was allowed?
text: When input tax on acquisition of the vehicle was allowed (for example, a bakkie), the consideration in money is calculated by applying a factor of 0.006 (0.6%) instead of 0.003 (0.3%).

title: In Example 17, what is the total input tax deduction that can be claimed by the second-hand goods dealer?
text: The total input tax deduction is R78.26, consisting of R52.17 deducted in tax period 1 and R26.09 deducted in tax period 2.

title: What is the VAT treatment of marked illuminating kerosene (paraffin) intended for use as fuel or for heating?
text: The sale of marked illuminating kerosene (paraffin) intended for use as fuel or for heating is subject to VAT at the zero rate.

title: What VAT tax fraction should be used for adjustments after the VAT rate increase on 1 April 2018?
text: The tax fraction 15/115 must be used to calculate VAT on the adjustment, as the time of supply for the adjustment is after the VAT rate increase from 14% to 15% on 1 April 2018.

title: What information about the applicant must be included in a VAT Ruling application?
text: The applicant's name, VAT number (if applicable), postal address, contact details (such as phone or e-mail), and if applicable, the contact details of the applicant's representative must be included.

title: What specific acts by SARS are considered exceptional circumstances under section 218 of the TA Act?
text: Certain acts by SARS that are considered exceptional circumstances include capturing errors and processing delays.

title: From what date can non-resident electronic services suppliers opt to be registered on the invoice basis?
text: From 21 August 2020, non-resident electronic services suppliers can opt to be registered on the invoice basis.

title: What options are available to a vendor if they disagree with an understatement penalty imposed?
text: A vendor may object to the understatement penalty (USP) being imposed and request a re-classification of the behaviour or for circumstances not previously considered to be taken into account by SARS. If the objection is allowed, this may result in a reduction of the USP imposed, or a decision that no USP should be imposed in a case where the understatement results from a bona fide inadvertent error.

title: What are the two key restrictions on decisions made under section 72 following the amendments?
text: Following the amendments, no decision can be given under section 72 which has the effect of reducing or increasing the liability for tax levied under the VAT Act, or is contrary to the construct and policy intent of the VAT Act as a whole or any specific provision thereof.

title: What applies to supplies made between entities in the same group?
text: The normal VAT rules apply to supplies made between entities in the same group, and if those supplies form part of their enterprise activities in the Republic, the VAT on such supplies must be accounted for in the VAT return submitted for the branch or division.

title: Which types of organizations are allowed to account on the payments basis regardless of the value of taxable supplies?
text: Public authorities, water boards, certain municipal entities, municipalities, associations not for gain and welfare organisations are allowed to account on the payments basis regardless of the value of taxable supplies.

title: What is the effective date of the latest amendments included in this guide?
text: The information in this guide is based on the VAT Act and the TA Act as at the time of publishing and includes the latest amendments contained in the Taxation Laws Amendment Act, 17 of 2023, the Tax Administration Laws Amendment Act, 18 of 2023 and the Rates and Monetary Amounts and Amendment of Revenue Laws Act, 19 of 2023. These Acts were all promulgated on 22 December 2023.
