title: What documentation is required to justify why the standard turnover-based method in BGR 16 is not appropriate for apportionment?
text: A detailed explanation as to why the standard turnover-based method in BGR 16 is considered not to be fair and reasonable in the circumstances, or why it is inappropriate, must be provided as part of the application.

title: Is VAT levied on the supply of a dwelling under a lease agreement?
text: No. VAT is not levied on the supply of a dwelling under a lease agreement. This rule also applies to employee housing supplied by an employer.

title: Who performs the installation work at B's premises and under what arrangement?
text: LBW, an RSA vendor, performs the necessary installation services on behalf of K at B's premises. K sub-contracts the installation work to LBW.

title: In Example 22, what was ABC Properties' apportionment ratio and how much VAT could be deducted?
text: ABC Properties' apportionment ratio was 96%. Since this meets the de minimis rule threshold of 95% or more, the full amount of R60 000 VAT incurred on the acquisition of the computer software could be deducted as input tax.

title: What is consideration in VAT terms?
text: Consideration is generally the total amount of money (including VAT) received for a supply. For barter transactions where consideration is not in money, it is the OMV of goods or services (including VAT) received for making the taxable supply. Any act of forbearance, whether voluntary or not, for the inducement of a supply of goods or services constitutes consideration, but it excludes donations made as unconditional gifts to associations not for gain and deposits lodged to secure a future supply of goods and held in trust.

title: What alternative documentation can a vendor provide if they do not possess the standard importation documents?
text: If the vendor is not in possession of the aforementioned documentation, it must in terms of section 54(3)(b) be in possession of the statement issued by the agent when deducting input tax under section 16(3)(a)(iii) or (b)(ii).

title: Where can information about supplies not made for a consideration be found?
text: Information about supplies not made for a consideration can be found in IN 70 (Interpretation Note 70 'Supplies made for No Consideration').

title: When must an adjustment be made if goods or services are applied wholly for non-taxable purposes?
text: The adjustment must be made at the time that the goods or services are applied wholly for non-taxable purposes.

title: What is the VAT rate that must be stated on a tax invoice according to the examples provided?
text: The VAT rate that must be stated on a tax invoice is 15%.

title: What is the minimum threshold of taxable supplies required for voluntary registration for a person carrying on an enterprise?
text: A person carrying on an enterprise can apply for voluntary registration if the value of taxable supplies made has exceeded the minimum threshold of R50 000 in the past 12 months, or if the value has not exceeded R50 000 but can reasonably be expected to exceed that threshold within 12 months from the date of registration.

title: Does a deposit received trigger the time of supply if it does not form part of the payment due for a supply?
text: No. A deposit received that does not form part of the payment due (that is consideration payable) for a supply will not trigger the time of supply, unless and until it is applied as payment against the consideration.

title: How is a shortfall defined in the context of understatement penalties?
text: A shortfall is the difference between the correct amount of tax that should have been paid and the amount that was paid by the vendor in respect of a tax period.

title: What are the two bases of accounting that vendors can use for VAT?
text: Vendors must account for VAT on the invoice (or accrual) basis unless application has been made and permission has been received from the Commissioner to use the payments basis of accounting.

title: What details should be provided to identify the transaction that a debit or credit note refers to?
text: Sufficient information to identify the transaction should be provided, for example, a reference to the original tax invoice number and the date on which it was issued.

title: What must non-resident suppliers of electronic services do to register for VAT?
text: Non-resident suppliers of electronic services must complete form VAT 101 and send it by email to eCommerceRegistration@sars.gov.za together with the supporting documents as outlined in VAT-REG-02-G02 – VAT Registration Guide for Foreign Suppliers of Electronic Services. Once successfully registered, they must register as an eFiler, which will enable them to file returns electronically and make VAT payments from outside South Africa.

title: What is the relationship between K's supply to B and LBW's supply to K in this scenario?
text: The supply of the service by LBW forms part of the supply by K to B. LBW's supply to K (who is not an RSA resident nor a vendor) is therefore subject to VAT at the zero rate.

title: What are the circumstances under which the Commissioner will deregister a vendor?
text: The Commissioner will deregister a vendor if: the enterprise closes down and will not commence again within the next 12 months; the enterprise never actually commenced or will not commence within the next 12 months; or the vendor no longer complies with the requirements for voluntary registration.

title: What is the maximum USP penalty rate that can be applied for intentional tax evasion?
text: The maximum USP penalty rate for intentional tax evasion is 200%, which applies when the case is obstructive or is a repeat case.

title: Within what timeframe must you apply for VAT registration after becoming liable?
text: You must apply for registration within 21 business days of becoming liable to register.

title: What VAT rate applies to unmarked illuminating paraffin that is blended or mixed with other products?
text: Any unmarked illuminating paraffin or other forms of paraffin which are blended or mixed with any other products are subject to the standard rate of VAT.

title: What are the conditions required for a notional input tax deduction on second-hand goods?
text: The conditions for a notional input tax deduction on second-hand goods are: the goods must be 'second-hand goods' as defined in section 1(1); the supply may not be a taxable supply; the supplier must be a South African resident and the goods must be situated in RSA; the purchaser must have made payment or at least part payment; the goods must be acquired wholly or partly for consumption, use or supply in the course of making taxable supplies; the vendor must be in possession of prescribed records per section 20(8); and for gold and goods containing gold, the goods must be sold in the same or substantially the same state as when acquired.

title: What goods listed in Part A of Schedule 2 may be purchased locally at the zero rate or imported exempt from VAT?
text: Goods listed in Part A of Schedule 2 that may be purchased locally at the zero rate or imported exempt from VAT include stock licks, fertiliser, seed, pesticide, remedies or medicines (but not other items such as syringes or vet's fees), animal, poultry, fish or game feed (including vitamins, bone products or maize products), and plants (including trees, bulbs, roots, cuttings or similar plant products used for cultivation).

title: On what types of supplies or acquisitions may input tax not be deducted?
text: As a general rule, input tax may not be deducted on supplies of 'entertainment', motor cars, and club subscriptions. Input tax may also not be deducted where goods or services are acquired for the purpose of making exempt supplies, for private use, or for other non-taxable activities.

title: How do vendors on the payments basis account for debit or credit notes differently from those on the invoice basis?
text: Vendors on the payments basis only make necessary adjustments when payment in respect of the debit or credit note is made or received, whereas vendors on the invoice basis account for the debit or credit note upon the issue or receipt of that document.

title: What documentation is required for petty cash purchases to allow VAT deduction?
text: For petty cash purchases, the receipt must indicate the amount of VAT charged, or alternatively, a statement that the amount charged includes VAT at the standard rate. The till slip, cash slip or sales docket must be kept in a petty cash book or similar record; otherwise any deduction will be disallowed.

title: What are the submission requirements for vendors in Category C tax period?
text: All vendors falling within Category C tax period must submit their returns in electronic format and make payments electronically on eFiling.

title: What does 'facilitates' mean in the context of an intermediary's activities under the new electronic services rules?
text: In the context of an intermediary's activities, 'facilitates' means that the electronic services are advertised, sold or otherwise made available via an online marketplace or 'platform' operated by the intermediary.

title: What must a supplier do if they cannot obtain required documentary proof within the prescribed period?
text: If the supplier is unable to obtain the required documentary proof within the prescribed period, the supplier must account for output tax in respect of the supply in field 12 of the VAT return in the tax period in which the prescribed period ends.

title: What are the consequences of late VAT payments according to SARS?
text: SARS has emphasised that payments received late would lead to penalties and interest being imposed.

title: According to the general time of supply rule, when does a supply occur?
text: According to the general time of supply rule, a supply occurs at the earlier of the following events: at the time that an invoice is issued, or at the time any payment is received by the supplier.

title: How can a vendor lodge a notice of appeal against a disallowed objection as of October 14, 2016?
text: With effect from 14 October 2016, a vendor can lodge a notice of appeal only on eFiling or by visiting a SARS branch.

title: Why is VAT not payable by the recipient DFG on the educational services fees?
text: VAT is not payable because the VAT Act makes provision for an exemption in this situation, as the same educational services, if provided by any university in the RSA, would have been exempt from VAT under section 12(h).

title: What is a vendor entitled to if they pay disputed tax and their objection or appeal is upheld?
text: A vendor is entitled to interest from the date of payment of the disputed amount until the date on which such amount is refunded.

title: When is input tax deducted as per the apportionment ratio?
text: Input tax is deducted as per the apportionment ratio (the extent of taxable supplies) per the turnover-based method or other specifically approved method when the apportionment ratio is less than 95% of the total use or consumption.

title: Does a request to change from invoice basis to payments basis accounting qualify as an application for a VAT ruling or decision?
text: Yes, the application qualifies as a request for a decision. The Commissioner is required to exercise discretion in directing that the vendor may be registered on the payments basis in accordance with the discretionary power provided in section 15(2).

title: What documents must be retained by the recipient of imported services?
text: The VAT215 record and the invoices issued by the foreign supplier must be retained as relevant material by the recipient.

title: What amendments were made to address difficulties with section 18D?
text: Two amendments were made: the first amendment is to section 10(29), which now includes the value of the land in the consideration calculation, and the second amendment was the deletion of section 18D(5)(c).

title: What is the facilitator bound by to ensure fair alternative dispute resolution?
text: The facilitator is bound by a Code of Conduct and must seek a fair, equitable, and legal resolution of the dispute. The process is also governed by a set of terms and conditions to which you agree.

title: What are the channels through which vendors can request the VATSA?
text: Vendors can request the VATSA through the following channels: electronically via eFiling or by calling the SARS Service Centre on 0800 00 SARS (7277).

title: Are spare parts used in the repair of a foreign-going aircraft eligible for zero-rating?
text: Yes, spare parts (such as welding rods, gas, welding plates, etc.) used in the repairs of a foreign-going aircraft may be zero-rated, provided the vendor obtains and retains the prescribed documentation including the particulars of the aircraft.

title: What circumstances trigger an immediate output tax adjustment for a vendor?
text: In the case of a vendor that has been sequestrated, declared insolvent, entered into an arrangement under section 155 of the Companies Act or cease to be a vendor within 12 months after the expiry of the tax period in which such deduction was made, the output tax adjustment must be made at the time of the occurrence of such sequestration, declaration of insolvency or arrangement or immediately before ceasing to be a vendor.

title: What type of tax invoice can be issued for supplies less than R5,000?
text: An abridged tax invoice may be issued for supplies with consideration less than R5,000 (including tax), except when that supply is a zero-rated supply.

title: What are examples of cases that are NOT regarded as the disposal of an income-earning activity?
text: Examples of cases which are not regarded as the disposal of an income-earning activity include: the sale of a bakery business without the ovens; the sale and leaseback of a commercial building; the disposal of a business yet to commence; and the disposal of a dormant business.

title: What change was made regarding the registration requirements for foreign electronic service providers, and when did it come into effect?
text: A new proviso was inserted into section 23(1A) with effect from 5 January 2023 to provide that a foreign electronic services provider is not required to register as a vendor if the total value of taxable supplies of R1 million in any consecutive 12-month period has been exceeded solely as a consequence of abnormal circumstances of a temporary nature. This amendment was aimed at achieving parity with the concession granted to local vendors.

title: What is included in the definition of a 'dwelling' for VAT purposes?
text: A 'dwelling' is basically defined as a building or a part of a building which is used, or intended to be used, as the residence of a natural person, and includes any fixtures and furnishings enjoyed with the supply of the dwelling.

title: What was the previous compulsory registration threshold for non-resident suppliers of electronic services, and what is it now?
text: The previous compulsory registration threshold for non-resident suppliers of electronic services was R50,000. It has been increased to R1 million under the updated ESR which came into effect on 1 April 2019.

title: What does the consideration for a supply normally equal in VAT purposes?
text: The consideration for a supply will normally be equal to the amount of money which is payable as the price charged for the supply. The consideration for a supply is represented by the value plus the VAT charged.

title: When must a full tax invoice be issued according to the VAT Act?
text: A full tax invoice must be issued when the consideration for the supply is more than R5,000 (including tax).

title: From what date did section 18B cease to apply?
text: Section 18B ceased to apply from 1 January 2018.

title: Where can information about the qualifications for the voluntary disclosure programme be found?
text: More information on the qualifications for the voluntary disclosure programme can be found in section 226 of the TA Act.

title: What statement must applicants provide regarding their tax compliance history?
text: Applicants must provide a statement confirming that all tax returns required to be rendered under a tax Act have been rendered and tax has been paid, or arrangements acceptable to SARS have been made for the submission of any outstanding returns or for the payment of any outstanding tax.

title: Do vendors need prior approval from the Commissioner to implement e-invoicing?
text: No, vendors do not need prior approval from the Commissioner to implement e-invoicing.

title: What types of cross-border transport qualify for the zero-rate in the RSA?
text: Cross-border transport of goods or passengers from a place outside the RSA into the RSA or vice versa qualifies for the zero-rate. Additionally, the transportation of goods or passengers between two places outside the RSA also qualifies for the zero-rate.

title: What are the three approved methods for reflecting consideration and VAT on a tax invoice?
text: Method 1: All individual amounts reflected (Price excl. VAT, VAT charged, and Total including VAT). Method 2: Total consideration only and the VAT rate charged. Method 3: Total consideration and the VAT charged.

title: When is the time of supply determined for goods and services supplied in connection with construction work where payment is made in instalments?
text: The time of supply is the earliest of the date when payment is due or is received, or any invoice relating to the payment is issued.

title: What statement must applicants provide regarding tax compliance in their ruling application?
text: Applicants must provide a statement confirming that all tax returns required to be rendered under a tax Act have been rendered and tax has been paid, or arrangements acceptable to SARS have been made for the submission of any outstanding returns or for the payment of any outstanding tax.

title: Are fringe benefits subject to VAT, and must employers report them on their VAT return?
text: Yes, the VAT on fringe benefits must be calculated by the employer and must be reflected in the employer's VAT return, even if the employer does not recover the VAT from the employee enjoying the fringe benefit. However, benefits arising from exempt or zero-rated supplies, or granted in the course of making exempt supplies, or a supply of entertainment are not subject to tax.

title: What are some examples of activities that qualify for voluntary VAT registration?
text: Examples of activities that qualify for voluntary VAT registration include: the building of ships, yachts, other floating vessels and aircrafts; the manufacture or assembly of plant, machinery, motor vehicles and locomotives; the construction of residential or commercial buildings for the taxable supply thereof; infrastructure development for purposes of carrying on an enterprise whereby the value of such development in terms of a contractual obligation exceeds R1 million and such development will take more than 12 months to complete; and the treatment of mining products to improve the properties thereof (beneficiation).

title: When did the amendments discussed in this guide come into effect?
text: The amendments discussed came into effect from 1 April 2023 unless otherwise stated.

title: What is a proposed transaction according to the ATR rules?
text: A proposed transaction is a transaction that an applicant proposes to undertake, but has not agreed to undertake, other than by way of an agreement that is subject to a suspensive condition or is otherwise not binding.

title: When does the time of supply occur for temporary letting of dwellings by developers under section 9(13)?
text: The time of supply under section 9(13) is triggered in the tax period in which the agreement for letting and hiring of the dwelling comes into effect or when the dwelling is occupied, whichever occurs first.

title: What happens if SARS determines that an objection does not comply with the requirements?
text: SARS may inform you by notice within 30 days that the objection is not accepted as a valid objection. However, you may within 20 days of delivery of that notice submit an amended objection, which SARS will accept if it complies with the requirements for a valid objection.

title: What is the time of supply for fringe benefits under VAT?
text: The time of supply for VAT purposes is the end of the month in which the cash equivalent of the fringe benefit is required to be included in the remuneration of the employee. In cases where the cash equivalent is not required to be included monthly or weekly, the time of supply is the last day of assessment of the employee under the Income Tax Act.

title: Is the list of requirements for VAT class ruling applications exhaustive?
text: No, the list of requirements is not exhaustive.

title: What are some of the key duties of a vendor that are impacted by the TA Act?
text: Some of the duties of a vendor that are impacted by the TA Act are registration, record-keeping, payments made to SARS and the obligation to inform SARS of changes in registered particulars to ensure that SARS has the most current information.

title: What VAT rate applies when a South African vendor builds a house situated in Botswana for a South African resident?
text: The zero rate applies when a South African vendor builds a house situated in Botswana for a South African resident, as any service supplied directly in connection with land situated outside the RSA is zero-rated.

title: Within what timeframe must an adjustment be made for any shortfall or overestimation in the percentage used for input tax calculation?
text: The adjustment should be done within a period of six months after the financial year-end. If the audited financial statements have not been completed within that time, an adjustment should be made using the year-end trial balance figures, followed by a final adjustment when the audited financial statements are eventually finalised.

title: What does the variable 'a' represent in the turnover-based method of apportionment formula?
text: The variable 'a' represents the value of all taxable supplies (including deemed taxable supplies) made during the period.

title: What is the exemption rule for associations not for gain that sell manufactured goods?
text: The exemption applies in a case where the organisation sells goods which it has manufactured if at least 80% of the value of the supply consists of donated goods or services.

title: What must a taxpayer do before approaching the Tax Ombud with a complaint against SARS?
text: A taxpayer must first exhaust all internal administrative complaints resolution processes which commence at the SARS branch level. If still dissatisfied, the complaint can be escalated to the SARS Complaints Management Office (CMO), and only once all these remedies are exhausted can the taxpayer approach the office of the Tax Ombud.

title: What financial documentation must be submitted as part of an application to use an alternative method of apportionment?
text: Annual Financial Statements for the past three years must be submitted as part of an application to use an alternative method of apportionment.

title: Which sections of the TA Act outline the objection and appeal procedures for certain Commissioner decisions?
text: Certain decisions are subject to objection and appeal as set out in section 32 and section 104 of the TA Act.

title: Who should provide the statement in a VAT class ruling application?
text: The class representative should provide the statement on behalf of the class.

title: What are the consequences for vendors who fail to comply with the VAT re-validation requirement?
text: Vendors that fail to comply with the VAT re-validation requirement either wilfully or through neglect will be guilty of an offence.

title: When a non-resident purchases a second-hand motor vehicle from a motor car dealer in the RSA, what is the extent to which they can claim a VAT refund?
text: A non-resident can only claim a refund to the extent that the VAT charged exceeds the amount of notional input tax deducted by the motor car dealer.

title: Why is it important to ensure SARS has correct banking details?
text: It is important to ensure SARS has correct banking details so that any refunds due can be paid safely and conveniently into the account without any unnecessary delays.

title: What VAT treatment applies to services supplied by a welfare organisation that receives a subsidy from a public authority?
text: A welfare organisation that receives a subsidy or grant payment from a public authority or municipality to enable it to carry on its welfare activities is deemed to supply services at the zero rate to the public authority or municipality.

title: What new provision was inserted regarding credit notes in the telecommunications industry?
text: Section 21(1)(f) was inserted to allow a VAT-registered and licensed telecommunications service provider to issue a credit note in certain circumstances where the nature of the supply has been fundamentally varied or altered subsequent to the issuing of a prepaid voucher under section 10(19) for future services to be rendered.

title: What is the VAT201 return and when must it be submitted?
text: The VAT201 return is a declaration that must be completed and submitted to SARS at the end of every tax period if you are a vendor. It reflects the VAT that you have charged on supplies and the amounts you believe you are entitled to deduct as input tax. Vendors must submit their returns by the due date, even if there is no payment required for the tax period concerned.

title: What is covered in section 3.2.1 of the document?
text: Section 3.2.1 covers new registrations.

title: What payment methods must Category C vendors use for VAT payments?
text: Category C vendors must file VAT returns on eFiling and make VAT payments electronically on eFiling or by EFT.

title: When does the time of supply occur for goods that are to be removed in a transaction between connected persons?
text: The time of supply occurs at the time the goods are removed.

title: Who may export movable goods under Part One of the Export Regulation?
text: Under Part One, a qualifying purchaser, its duly appointed agent, or cartage contractor may export the movable goods.

title: What were the three dates by which existing section 72 decisions ceased to apply?
text: Existing section 72 decisions remained valid until the earlier of: the stated expiry date of that decision, 31 December 2021, or the effective date of any amendment in the legislation that affects the previous arrangement or decision. All decisions under section 72 that did not have a stated expiry date before 31 December 2021 ceased to apply from that date.

title: Why is it important for vendors to be mindful of time of supply rules when there has been a change in the VAT rate?
text: Vendors need to know the time of supply rules to establish whether they have charged the correct rate of VAT to their customers or been charged the correct rate by their suppliers, which in turn affects the completion of the VAT201 return.

title: What are the three requirements that must be met for VAT to qualify as input tax?
text: To qualify as input tax, three requirements must be met: (a) The goods or services supplied must be acquired by the vendor wholly or partly for consumption, use or supply in the course of making taxable supplies. (b) VAT at the standard rate must have been charged on the taxable supply (except in the case of second-hand goods, or goods repossessed or surrendered under an ICA which have been acquired under a non-taxable supply). (c) The appropriate documentation must be held by the vendor, including a valid tax invoice, debit note or credit note or other prescribed documentation for standard-rated supplies.

title: What conditions must be met for the supply of an enterprise as a going concern to qualify for the zero rate?
text: The supply of an enterprise or part of an enterprise which is capable of separate operation as a going concern qualifies for the zero rate if all of the requirements are met under section 11(1)(e), and it must be clearly evident and agreed to in writing by the parties that the enterprise is disposed of as a going concern.

title: What happens if a non-resident supplier of electronic services is required to register for VAT in the RSA?
text: If the non-resident supplier of electronic services is required to be registered as a vendor, the supplies are not regarded as imported services taxable under section 7(1)(c). Instead, the non-resident supplier, being a vendor, will charge VAT under section 7(1)(a) as taxable supplies made in the course or furtherance of an enterprise carried on in the RSA. The vendor can deduct the VAT paid as input tax if acquired for taxable purposes.

title: When will SARS consider an application for VAT registration from a person who has submitted a tender for project work?
text: SARS will consider an application for VAT registration only once the tender outcome is final and the contract has been awarded to that person. The person will not be registered as a vendor in anticipation of being awarded the contract.

title: What are the two options available for conducting ADR proceedings?
text: You can have party-to-party ADR discussions with SARS or use a facilitator for the ADR proceedings.

title: When did the ADR 2 form stop being accepted for lodging appeals for VAT?
text: With effect from 14 October 2016, the ADR 2 form is no longer accepted for lodging appeals for VAT.

title: When did the new dispute resolution rules come into effect?
text: The new dispute resolution rules came into effect on 10 March 2023.

title: What is the binding effect of a Binding General Ruling (BGR)?
text: A Binding General Ruling (BGR) is binding on SARS and all qualifying taxpayers.

title: Are entertainment expenses for staff meals and refreshments denied input tax?
text: Yes. Input tax is denied on food and other ingredients purchased to provide meals to staff, clients and business associates, including year-end lunches and parties, hiring of venues for those functions, and expenses for free meals at workplace canteens or complimentary staff refreshments such as tea, coffee and other beverages or snacks provided to staff.

title: At what VAT rate can goods exported to clients outside the Republic be charged?
text: Goods exported to clients in an export country (any country outside of the Republic) may be charged with VAT at 0%. However, if delivery takes place in the Republic, the standard rate must be charged unless the goods are supplied under Sections A and B of Part Two of the Export Regulations, which allow the zero rate to be applied subject to certain requirements and at the discretion of the supplier.

title: What does the term 'adjusted cost' exclude when calculating input and output tax adjustments?
text: The term 'adjusted cost' excludes any costs incurred in acquiring assets which are not VAT inclusive (or deemed to include VAT). Examples include finance charges (exempt) or labour charges by a non-vendor (no VAT chargeable), and salary and wages incurred in the manufacture, assembly, construction or production of those goods or services.

title: What does the 10% upliftment represent in the calculation of Added Tax Value (ATV) for imports?
text: The 10% upliftment represents an amount in lieu of transport and insurance costs which is used for calculating the ATV. It is not an amount payable to SARS.

title: What must a vendor do if a specific ruling previously granted to them becomes inappropriate at a later stage?
text: If a specific ruling that has been granted to the vendor turns out to be inappropriate at a later stage, the vendor cannot make the choice to use the turnover-based method without requesting a further ruling from SARS to that effect.

title: Is VAT 404 – Guide for Vendors a binding general ruling?
text: No, VAT 404 – Guide for Vendors is not a binding general ruling (BGR) under section 89 of Chapter 7 of the TA Act nor a ruling under section 41B of the VAT Act unless otherwise indicated.

title: Who is now responsible for declaring the percentage of gold content in a valuable metal supplied?
text: The supplier of the valuable metal is responsible for declaring the percentage of gold content in a valuable metal supplied. This responsibility was transferred from the recipient to the supplier through amendments to regulations 2 and 3.

title: What documentation must a vendor possess when goods are imported and the vendor is listed as the importer on the bill of entry?
text: The vendor must be in possession of the bill of entry or other documentation prescribed by the Customs and Excise Act, together with the receipt for the payment of the VAT in relation to the importation of the goods and the EDI Customs Status 1 Release Message.

title: What is the only pre-approved method that may be used to apportion VAT for mixed purposes without specific prior written approval from the Commissioner?
text: The turnover-based method is the only pre-approved method which may be used to apportion VAT incurred for mixed purposes without specific prior written approval from the Commissioner.

title: What new provision was inserted into section 52 regarding pooling arrangements?
text: A new subsection (3) was inserted into section 52 to allow for pooling arrangements in industries other than agriculture and property rental. This provision is intended to deal with the VAT treatment of pooling arrangements established as a manner of complying with the provisions of legislation, regulations or rules of a professional body.

title: Can SARS reassess a vendor if the practice generally prevailing is altered after the initial assessment?
text: No. An assessment will always be viewed in accordance with the practice generally prevailing at the time of the assessment. SARS cannot reassess if the practice generally prevailing is altered subsequent to the assessment.

title: Under what circumstances may a vendor registered on the payments basis be required by SARS to change to the invoice basis?
text: SARS may require a vendor to change from the payments basis to the invoice basis if that vendor ceases to qualify for the payments basis. This occurs when a vendor who is a natural person or partnership of natural persons exceeds the R2.5 million threshold prescribed in the VAT Act at the end of the tax period in which the value of taxable supplies in any consecutive period of 12 months exceeded R2.5 million, or if that threshold is likely to be exceeded in the next 12 months. Additionally, a vendor may be disqualified if they decide to conduct business activities under a different legal entity such as a company.

title: What types of capital goods are considered entertainment expenses and denied input tax?
text: Capital goods such as hospitality boxes, holiday houses, yachts and private aircraft are considered entertainment expenses and are denied input tax.

title: What is required of a vendor who acquires goods or services used partly for taxable supplies and then supplies those same goods or services?
text: A vendor is required to account for output tax on the full consideration for the supply. In order to eliminate double taxation, the vendor is entitled to deduct the portion of VAT that was originally disallowed on the acquisition of the goods or services.

title: Can welfare organisations be regarded as enterprises under VAT even if they do not charge consideration for their supplies?
text: Yes, special provision is made in the VAT Act to regard welfare organisations as enterprises to the extent that they make supplies in the course of carrying out specific welfare activities, even if they do not charge a consideration for making those supplies.

title: What are the main topics covered under section 14.1 Assessments?
text: Section 14.1 Assessments covers the purpose of assessments, types of assessments, and other aspects.

title: What forms of consideration must be provided for the exemption of lodging or boarding services by educational institutions?
text: The exemption applies when lodging or boarding is supplied for a consideration in the form of school fees, tuition fees or payment for lodging or board and lodging.

title: What are the essential characteristics of a VAT type tax?
text: The generally accepted essential characteristics of a VAT type tax are: the tax applies generally to transactions related to goods or services; it is proportional to the price charged for the goods or services; it is charged at each stage of the production and distribution process; and the taxable person (vendor) may deduct the tax paid during the preceding stages on goods or services acquired, meaning the burden of the tax is on the final consumer.

title: Which fields in the VAT return form reflect the total amount of output tax?
text: Field 13 reflects the total amount of output tax and is the sum of fields 4, 4A, 9, 11 and 12. Fields 1 to 12 must be completed in respect of any output tax which you are required to account for in the relevant tax period.

title: What is the maximum duration for the temporary letting period under section 18D?
text: The 'temporarily applied' letting period must not exceed a period of 12 months, whether as a fixed or continuous period, or in aggregate.

title: For how long must a vendor use a selected fixed day or date consistently?
text: A vendor must use a selected fixed day or date consistently for a minimum period of 12 months.

title: What documentation is needed for tax charged on locally manufactured goods where excise duty or environmental levy has not been included in the selling price?
text: Prescribed Customs documentation and proof that the VAT has been paid to Customs are required.

title: Before which date must a public authority or constitutional institution have acquired capital goods or services for no adjustment to be applicable?
text: No adjustment is applicable where the vendor is a public authority or constitutional institution and the capital goods or services were acquired before 1 April 2005.

title: What must a qualifying purchaser do before submitting a claim for VAT refund to the VRA?
text: When the movable goods are exported, the goods must first be declared to a customs official at the exit point before submitting the claim for a refund of the VAT paid to the VRA.

title: Under what conditions can a supplier deduct previously calculated output tax as an adjustment?
text: If the supplier receives the documentation within a period of five years from the end of the tax period during which the original tax invoice for the supply should have been issued, the supplier may deduct the amount of output tax previously calculated as an adjustment in field 18 of the VAT return for the tax period in which this documentation is received, provided that certain requirements are met.

title: What types of private transactions are exempt from VAT?
text: Private occasional transactions are exempt from VAT, including the sale of domestic/household goods, personal effects, or a private motor vehicle.

title: Under what circumstances can the Commissioner deregister a vendor based on discretionary powers from 1 April 2014?
text: From 1 April 2014, the Commissioner has the discretion to deregister any vendor that fails to furnish a VAT return in respect of a tax period.

title: What are the methods available to submit supporting documents requested by SARS?
text: The supporting documents can be submitted via the eFiling channel, by post, or by delivery at the nearest SARS office. You may select the format accessible to you from these options.

title: What is the monthly rental amount for each shop in Company J's building, and is VAT charged?
text: Shops are rented for R12 000 each plus VAT at 15% per month.

title: What payment method must non-resident suppliers of electronic services use to pay their VAT?
text: Non-resident suppliers of electronic services may only use the EFT method to pay their VAT, and payment must be made using the SWIFT MT103 payment method.

title: What must be completed and submitted by the applicant from 1 August 2023 when applying for a VAT ruling?
text: From 1 August 2023, a sanitised template must be completed and submitted by the applicant upon application for a ruling, to assist in streamlining the sanitisation and publication process.

title: When will the interest rules under the TA Act come into effect?
text: The interest rules to be imposed by the TA Act will only come into effect once a public notice is issued by the Commissioner. Until such time, the current rules will still apply.

title: What is the threshold for supplying commercial accommodation before VAT registration may be required?
text: Supplying commercial accommodation in circumstances where the total value of those supplies made or reasonably expected to be made does not exceed R120 000 in any consecutive period of 12 months is exempt from VAT registration requirements.

title: What is the standard tax period allocated at registration and what are its categories?
text: The standard tax period is the two-monthly tax period, which requires submission of one return for every two calendar months. Category A ends on the last day of January, March, May, July, September and November, while Category B ends on the last day of February, April, June, August, October and December.

title: What are examples of zero-rated foodstuffs that are prepared for immediate consumption and therefore do not qualify for the zero rate?
text: Examples of zero-rated foodstuffs prepared for immediate consumption that do not qualify for the zero rate include a glass of milk served in a restaurant, a pre-packed salad with salad dressing purchased at a supermarket, and sandwiches and other take-away foods.

title: What does section 13.9 address?
text: Section 13.9 addresses electronic tax invoices, debit- and credit notes, and records.

title: What are the main topics covered in Chapter 8 of the VAT 404 Guide for Vendors?
text: Chapter 8 covers Input tax and other deductions, including what qualifies as input tax or a deduction, how deductions are made, when and what to deduct, apportionment, denial of input tax, petty cash payments, and pre-incorporation expenses.

title: What is output tax?
text: Output tax is the tax (VAT) charged by a vendor on a taxable supply of goods or services.

title: What is the role of the private company appointed by SARS?
text: To administer the VAT refund mechanism

title: What is the minimum turnover threshold for a voluntary VAT registrant to apply for deregistration?
text: A vendor that has no intention to continue as a voluntary VAT registrant may apply to be deregistered on the basis that the value of taxable supplies is less than R1 million in a 12-month period.

title: What is the VAT treatment of the supply of goods between BF to BB and between BB to its customers?
text: The supply of the goods between BF to BB will be zero-rated as well as the supply of the goods between BB to the various customers.

title: What was amended regarding the definition of 'enterprise'?
text: The definition of 'enterprise' was amended to include reference to the activities of an implementing agency in respect of the FDFP activities rather than to regard the FDFP as a 'person'.

title: How is field 34 calculated on the VAT return?
text: Field 34 is calculated by adding fields 25, 29 and 33 together and subtracting the total from the value in field 20.

title: What documentation is required to nominate another vendor's bank account for VAT refunds?
text: To nominate or use the bank account of another vendor, you must supply the necessary authority from the account holder (for example, company resolution) and provide indemnity to SARS against possible losses of amounts paid into such accounts on form VAT119i. This form is available from SARS offices or the SARS website.

title: What details must appear on a debit note or credit note?
text: The following details should appear on debit notes and credit notes: the words 'debit note' or 'credit note' (as the case may be), the name, address, and VAT registration number of the vendor, the name and address of the recipient (unless the supplier originally issued an abridged tax invoice), and the date on which the debit note or credit note is issued.

title: What is required for a vendor to deduct input tax on purchases for their enterprise?
text: A vendor must have a proper tax invoice or alternative documents approved by the Commissioner in order to deduct input tax on purchases for their enterprise.

title: What is the VAT treatment for the supply of services under a restraint of trade agreement concerning rights to be used outside the Republic?
text: The supply of services under a restraint of trade agreement concerning the exercise of rights listed in paragraph (i) of section 11(2)(m) for use outside of the Republic is zero-rated.

title: What happens to a suspended tax payment if no objection is lodged by the vendor?
text: The suspension is revoked with immediate effect from the date of the expiry of the relevant prescribed time period or any extension of the relevant time period under the TA Act.

title: What is one advantage of the invoice basis of accounting for VAT?
text: One advantage of the invoice basis of accounting is that vendors can deduct VAT incurred on purchases before payment.

title: What factors may SARS consider when deciding on a vendor's request to suspend payment of disputed tax?
text: SARS may consider the following factors: whether the recovery of the disputed tax will be in jeopardy or there will be a risk of dissipation of assets; the compliance history of the vendor; whether fraud is prima facie involved in the origin of the dispute; whether the vendor is able to provide adequate security for the payment of the amount involved; and whether payment of the amount involved would result in irreparable financial hardship to the vendor not justified by the prejudice to SARS or the fiscus if the disputed tax is not paid or recovered.

title: What percentage of C's business income is derived from taxable supplies according to the example?
text: According to Example 27, C determined that 60% of the business income is derived from taxable supplies.

title: What adjustment must a vendor that issues a debit note make, and what adjustment must the vendor receiving it make?
text: A vendor that issues a debit note is required to make an adjustment to output tax. The vendor receiving a debit note must make an adjustment to input tax.

title: What is the purpose of the penalty regime according to Chapter 11 of the VAT 404 Guide?
text: The purpose of the penalty regime is to ensure the widest possible compliance of the tax Acts in a way that is impartial and consistent and is proportional to the seriousness and duration of the incidence of non-compliance.

title: What do fields 14-18 on the VAT return represent?
text: Fields 14-18 are the fields which must be completed in respect of any input tax which you believe you are entitled to deduct against the output tax liability in field 13.

title: What should readers do if they are in doubt regarding any aspect of the information in this guide?
text: Readers who are in doubt regarding any aspect of the information should refer to the relevant legislation, or seek a formal opinion from a suitably qualified individual.

title: What are the SDL rates that employers must pay according to the SDL Act?
text: From 1 April 2000, employers must pay SDL at a rate of 0.5 per cent of the leviable amount, and from 1 April 2001, at a rate of one per cent of the leviable amount. If the Minister makes changes to the rate, the new rate will apply with effect from the date announced by the Minister by notice in the Government gazette.

title: How should remuneration for non-resident NEDs be treated for tax purposes?
text: Where the NED is a non-resident, the directors' fee received is regarded as remuneration and the employer must deduct employees' tax on the amount of directors' fees payable. The remuneration must reflect under code 3621 on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate.

title: What form must an employer submit to ascertain the amount of employees' tax to be deducted from gains made by the exercise of rights to acquire marketable securities under section 8A?
text: The employer must apply for an IRP3 tax directive, and an IRP3(a) application form must be submitted in respect of gains made by the exercise, cession or release of any right to acquire any marketable security as contemplated in section 8A.

title: Under what condition can an employee with a disability receive a scholarship or bursary without losing the exemption?
text: An employee with a disability can receive a scholarship or bursary without losing the exemption if the employee agrees to reimburse the employer for any scholarship or bursary granted to him/her if he/she fails to complete his/her studies for reasons other than death, ill-health or injury.

title: When does an amount constitute expenditure for the employer for tax purposes?
text: For purposes of determining taxable income, the stated amounts will constitute expenditure for the employer in the year that the employer pays that amount to the employee.

title: How are low-interest or interest-free loans granted by an employer to further an employee's studies classified for tax purposes?
text: Low-interest or interest-free loans granted by the employer to further the employee's studies are not regarded as bursaries, but as low or interest-free loans upon which no value is placed.

title: How is leave pay treated in relation to severance benefits?
text: Leave pay is a payment in respect of services rendered and the amount does not form part of a severance benefit.

title: From what date did dividends received through employee-based share schemes become subject to employees' tax deduction?
text: Effective from 1 March 2017 (2018 year of assessment), dividends received or accrued by way of dividend contemplated in the specified provisos must be included in remuneration and employees' tax MUST be deducted.

title: Under which code should the tax portion be reflected if the lump sum payment reflects under code 3915, 3920, 3921, 3922, 3923 (to 2024 year of assessment) and 3924?
text: Code 4115

title: What is the exemption limit per relative for bursaries granted by an employer?
text: The exemption limit for bursaries is R20,000 per relative.

title: What are the three main contact methods for obtaining more information from SARS?
text: The three main contact methods are: Visit the SARS website www.sars.gov.za, Call the SARS Contact Centre on 0800 00SARS (7277), or Visit your nearest SARS branch.

title: What is the tax treatment for remuneration received by ship officers or crew members engaged in international transportation?
text: Remuneration derived by an officer or crew member of a ship engaged in international transportation for reward of passengers or goods is exempt from tax if such person was outside the Republic for a period or periods exceeding 183 full days in aggregate during the year of assessment.

title: What income source code should be used to reflect taxable lump sum proceeds from employer-owned insurance policies on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate?
text: Lump sum proceeds in respect of employer-owned insurance policies that should be taxable must be reflected under income source code 3907 on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate.

title: When does a new interest rate take effect if the repurchase rate or equivalent rate changes?
text: The new rate of interest applies from the first day of the month following the date on which that new purchase rate or equivalent rate came into operation.

title: Who is personally liable if an employer fails to deduct or withhold the full amount of Employees' tax and/or UIF contributions?
text: An employer who fails to deduct or withhold the full amount of Employees' tax and/or UIF contributions is personally liable for the shortfall.

title: What is the annual exemption limit for bursaries for higher education (NQF level 5 to 10)?
text: R60,000 in respect of higher education (NQF level 5 up to 10).

title: In the example provided, what is the total employees' tax deductible for week 3 in June when a monthly production bonus is paid to a weekly remunerated employee?
text: The employees' tax deductible for week 3 in June is R3 684. This is calculated by determining the tax on the bonus for one week (R108) and multiplying it by the weeks in which it was earned (R108 x 4 = R432), then adding it to the tax on the weekly wage (R3 252 + R432 = R3 684).

title: What code should be used for local dividends linked to foreign services according to paragraph (ii) of the proviso to section 10(1)(k)(i)?
text: Code 3769 MUST only be used for local dividends linked to foreign services.

title: What happens to PAYE obligations when a person ceases to be a director but remains an employee?
text: When a person ceases to be a director but remains an employee of the company, section 7B will no longer be used and PAYE must be deducted from remuneration that is actually paid or is payable to the employee. Only one IRP5/IT3(a) needs to be issued for the year.

title: What source code should be used for lump sum payments from a Pension fund with a date of accrual prior to 1 March 2009?
text: Code 3902 must be reflected on the certificate for lump sum payments from a Pension, Pension preservation or RAF with a date of accrual prior to 1 March 2009.

title: What deductions are allowed when calculating the leviable amount for SDL purposes?
text: SDL is determined on the balance of remuneration after the deduction of all allowable deductions, which include pension or provident fund contributions, RAF contributions, and donations. All remuneration not included in the definition of remuneration for SDL purposes should be excluded from the balance of remuneration result.

title: How is the quarterly commission divided to calculate the tax on commission for one month?
text: The quarterly commission is divided by the months in which it was earned. In this example, R13 500 ÷ 3 = R4 500.

title: When did the provisions regarding vesting of equity instruments become applicable?
text: These provisions are only applicable to any equity instrument acquired on or after 26 October 2004.

title: What is the employer's personal liability regarding employees' tax and UIF contributions?
text: An employer who fails to deduct or withhold the full amount of Employees' tax and / or UIF contributions is personally liable for the shortfall.

title: Which transfer costs are exempt from employees' tax according to Section 10(1)(nB)?
text: The following transfer costs are exempt from employees' tax: (1) Transportation of the employee, members of his/her household and their personal goods and possessions from the previous place of residence to the new place of residence; (2) Costs incurred by the employee in respect of sale of his/her previous residence and in settling in permanent residential accommodation at the new place of residence; and (3) Cost of renting temporary residential accommodation for the employee and members of his/her household during a period which ends 183 days after the transfer took place or after the date of appointment.

title: What is the liability of a representative employer?
text: The representative employer is not relieved from any liability, responsibility or duty of the employer and is therefore subject to the same duties, responsibilities and liabilities as the employer.

title: What is the order in which payments made by an employer for Employees' Tax, SDL and UIF are allocated?
text: Payments are allocated in the following order: Penalty; Interest, to the extent to which the payment exceeds the amount of penalty; Employees' tax or additional penalty, to the extent to which the payment exceeds the amount of penalty and interest.

title: What are employers prohibited from doing regarding tax directives?
text: Employers may not act upon photocopies of directives and may under no circumstances deviate from the instructions of the directive.

title: What is the definition of an annual payment according to the guide?
text: An annual payment is an amount of net remuneration that is, in accordance with the employee's conditions of service or the employer's practice, paid in a lump sum to the employee, or that is calculated without reference to a period.

title: What are the day requirements for an employee to qualify for tax exemption on remuneration for services rendered outside the Republic?
text: An employee must be outside the Republic for a period or periods exceeding 183 full days in aggregate during any 12 month period, and for a continuous period exceeding 60 full days during that period, with services rendered during those periods.

title: What does the guide state about amounts that must be included in an employee's taxable income?
text: Certain amounts must be included in the employee's taxable income and these amounts are normally referred to as allowances.

title: What is the deadline for paying employees' tax, SDL and UIF contributions to SARS?
text: Employees' tax and UIF contributions as well as SDL must be paid over to SARS within seven days after the end of the month during which the amount was deducted or due or such longer period as the Commissioner determines. Where the seventh day falls on a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday, the payment must be made not later than the last business day prior to such day.

title: Which Government Gazette notice contains instructions for the application forms used by fund administrators for lump sum benefit tax directives?
text: The relevant application forms are furnished in accordance with the instructions contained in the Government Gazette No. 22577 (notice no. 1893) dated 24 August 2001.

title: How should a production bonus be treated if it is paid during the same pay period in which it was earned?
text: If the production bonus was paid during the same pay period in which it was earned, it must be added to the salary and employees' tax must be determined according to the appropriate weekly, fortnightly or monthly table.

title: What are the validity requirements for an exemption certificate?
text: An exemption certificate will only be valid if it is not outdated, bears a labour broker reference number beginning with a 7, has been computer printed, the labour broker is in possession of the original, and has not been altered in any way.

title: What is the timeframe for notifying the Commissioner after ceasing to be an employer?
text: Every person who is registered as an employer shall within 14 days after ceasing to be an employer, notify the Commissioner in writing of the fact that the employer has ceased to be an employer.

title: When does a fringe benefit constitute expenditure for the employer for tax purposes?
text: For purposes of determining taxable income, fringe benefits will constitute expenditure for the employer in the year that the employer pays that amount to the employee.

title: What condition must be met for an estimate assessment to NOT be subject to objection and appeal?
text: Both employer and Commissioner must agree in writing to the estimate assessment(s) in terms of section 95(3) of the Tax Administration Act.

title: What must an employer do to determine the amount of employees' tax to be deducted for an arbitration award?
text: An employer must apply for a tax directive to determine the amount of employees' tax to be deducted in respect of the amount payable to an employee or former employee as a result of any arbitration award.

title: When must employers submit a directive application for backdated salaries accrued in previous years?
text: Employers must submit a directive application when employees' tax relates to taxable accrued in the previous year/s, or when a settlement agreement, arbitration award, court-order, or Labour Court dispute relates to previous years. SARS will determine the employees' tax to be deducted in the same manner as tax on a bonus, using the current year's tax deduction tables.

title: Can an employer and employee agree not to deduct employees' tax or UIF contributions?
text: No. Employer and employee may under no circumstances conclude an agreement whereby the employer undertakes not to deduct or withhold employees' tax or UIF contributions. Such agreement is void in terms of Paragraph 7 of the Fourth Schedule.

title: Which form should be used for severance benefits paid by an employer?
text: IRP3(a) should be used for severance benefits paid by employer (e.g. death/retirement/retrenchment). The form must also be used for share options without obligation, Two Pot Savings withdrawal benefit or other lump sums.

title: What amounts relating to restricted equity instruments must be included in a taxpayer's income?
text: Any amount received by or accrued to a taxpayer during year of assessment relating to a restricted equity instrument must be included in taxpayer's income, if that amount does not constitute: a return of capital or foreign return of capital by way of distribution of a restricted equity instrument; a dividend or foreign dividend in respect of that equity instrument; or any amount that must be taken into account for purposes of calculating a gain or loss for purposes of section 8C.

title: What happens to bursaries if an employee's remuneration exceeds R600,000 per annum?
text: If the employee's remuneration exceeds R600,000 per annum, the bursaries will be taxable in full.

title: What are the medical scheme fees tax credit amounts for the 2026 tax year?
text: For the 2026 tax year, the medical scheme fees tax credit amounts are: R364 in respect of benefits to the taxpayer; R364 in respect of benefits to the taxpayer's first dependent; and R246 in respect of benefits to each additional dependent.

title: What are the consequences for an employer who fails to deduct employees' tax from remuneration or pay tax to the Commissioner within the prescribed period?
text: Any person will be guilty of an offence and liable on conviction to a fine or imprisonment for failing to deduct employees' tax from remuneration or to pay tax to the Commissioner within the prescribed period.

title: How should employer contributions made on behalf of an employee be treated for tax purposes?
text: A contribution made by the employer on behalf of an employee is a taxable fringe benefit in the hands of an employee. These contributions are deemed to be paid by the employee and the same value included as a taxable benefit (code 3810) should be added to the value of the contributions made by the employee (code 4005).

title: Who collects UIF contributions and how often are they payable?
text: Since 1 April 2002, UIF contributions deducted and payable by employers have been collected by SARS on a monthly basis and are paid over to the UIF which is managed by the UI Commissioner.

title: Which tax tables should be used to determine employees' tax withholding for standard employment?
text: The weekly, fortnightly and monthly tables as published each year after the Budget Speech must be used to determine the amount of employees' tax to be withheld from the balance of remuneration for each pay period. The annual table must be used at the end of the tax period or year of assessment to determine the final amount of employees' tax payable for the full year or period of assessment.

title: What types of events do employer-owned insurance policies make payments for?
text: Employer-owned insurance policies make payment upon the death, disability or severe illness of an employee of such employer.

title: What constitutes an offence regarding the use of deducted employees' tax?
text: Using or applying employees' tax deducted or withheld for purposes other than the payment of such amount to the Commissioner constitutes an offence.

title: What guidelines are available to help employers determine whether an employee is an independent contractor?
text: Guidelines are available in Interpretation Note 17, which can be obtained on the SARS website www.sars.gov.za. These guidelines assist the employer with the responsibility of determining whether or not the employee is an independent contractor.

title: Under what circumstances will the exemption in section 10(1)(k)(i) not apply to dividends received by a person in respect of services rendered through employment or office holding?
text: The exemption in s10(1)(k)(i) will not apply to any dividend received by or accrued to a person in respect of services rendered or to be rendered in respect of or by virtue of employment or the holding of any office, other than a dividend received or accrued in respect of a restricted equity instrument as defined in s8C held by that person or in respect of a share held by that person.

title: When should tax deductions commence for employees who are 75 years or older?
text: Employees' tax deductions for persons 75 years or older must be made according to the tables from the beginning of tax period (e.g. 1 March) during which employee turns 75 and not as from the month qualifying age of 75 is attained.

title: When did the deduction tables and instructions in this guide come into effect?
text: The deduction tables and instructions in this guide came into effect on 1 March 2025.

title: What are examples of job-related training that does not represent a taxable benefit to employees?
text: Examples include computer and word processing courses, management and administration courses, bookkeeping courses, sales courses, courses in operating office and technical equipment, and language courses for employees.

title: What source code and tax code should be used for commutation of a living annuity with effect from 1 March 2011?
text: With effect from 1 March 2011, source code 3915 with tax code 4115 must be used on the certificate for commutation of a living annuity, to which the retirement lump sum rate of tax is applicable.

title: Are government officers and employees required to contribute to UI?
text: No, employees and employers in the national and provincial spheres of Government who are officers or employees as defined in Section 1(1) of the Public Service Act 1994 are not required to contribute.

title: What is the tax treatment of special uniforms provided by an employer to an employee?
text: Where it is a condition of employment that an employee is required to wear a special uniform which is clearly distinguishable from ordinary clothing whilst on duty, the value of such uniform given to the employee or any allowance made by the employer in lieu of such uniform as is reasonable, is exempt from tax. This should be reflected under code 3714 on the IRP5/IT3(a).

title: When will an independent contractor's income be subject to Employees' Tax according to the Fourth Schedule?
text: An independent contractor's income will be deemed to be remuneration and subject to Employees' Tax if the services are required to be performed mainly at the premises of the person by whom the remuneration is paid/payable or of the person to whom such services were or are to be rendered, or if the person who renders the service is subject to the control and supervision of any other person as to the manner in which his/her duties are performed or as to his/her hours of work.

title: What is the primary tax rebate amount for individuals in 2026?
text: The primary rebate applicable to individuals in 2026 is R17,235.

title: What should an employer do if a commission-earning employee is not in possession of a tax directive?
text: If the employee is not in possession of a tax directive, the employer MUST combine the salary and commission and deduct employees' tax according to the applicable tax deduction tables and a PAYE calculation must be done at the end of the tax year or tax period. Under no circumstances may 25% or any other percentage be deducted from remuneration, unless the tax directive so directs.

title: From which date did the medical scheme fees tax credit become applicable to all taxpayers?
text: Effective from 1 March 2014, the medical scheme fees tax credit applies to all taxpayers.

title: When did paragraph (kk) of the proviso to section 10(1)(k)(i) come into operation?
text: Paragraph (kk) came into operation on 1 March 2017.

title: What must an employer do regarding employees' tax deduction on dividends from 1 March 2018?
text: From 1 March 2018 in terms of paragraph 11A(4) of the Fourth Schedule, the employer must ascertain from the Commissioner the amount of employees' tax to be deducted or withheld.

title: List three examples of annual payments or payments made without reference to a period.
text: Three examples of annual payments are: annual bonus, incentive bonus, and leave pay on resignation. Other examples include encashment of leave, merit awards, and backdated payments in respect of salary/wages accrued in previous tax years.

title: What is the primary purpose of this employer guide?
text: The purpose of this document is to assist employers in understanding their obligations relating to Employees' Tax, Skills Development Levy (SDL) and Unemployment Insurance Fund (UIF) contributions.

title: What is the primary commitment of an independent contractor according to the contract for services?
text: The independent contractor only commits himself/herself to deliver the product or end result of that capacity.

title: What is the effective date of the Guide for Employers in respect of Employees' Tax?
text: The effective date is 12 March 2025.

title: What is the document reference number for this guide?
text: The document reference number is PAYE-GEN-01-G20.

title: What tax deduction rate applies to non-standard employment workers earning more than R383 per day when required to work at least 5 hours per day?
text: A 25% deduction applies to non-standard employment workers earning more than R383 per day when required to work at least 5 hours per day.

title: Under what conditions are lump sum payments from insurance policies exempt from income tax according to section 10(1)(gH)?
text: Lump sum payments accruing on or after 1 March 2012 in respect of a policy of insurance are exempt from income tax under section 10(1)(gH) if: (1) the policy relates to death, disablement or severe illness of an employee or director, or former employee or director of the policy holder; and (2) no amount of premiums payable for that policy was tax deductible with effect from 1 March 2012 from the income of the policy holder (employer).

title: What is the maximum deduction limit for current contributions under section 11F?
text: The deduction must be limited to the lesser of: R350,000; OR 27.5% of the higher of remuneration as defined in Fourth Schedule (excluding retirement fund lump sum benefit, retirement lump sum withdrawal benefit and severance benefit) or taxable income before allowing any deduction under ss11F, 6quat(1C) and 18A (excluding retirement fund lump sum benefit, retirement lump sum withdrawal benefit and severance benefit).

title: What expenses qualify for tax exemption when an employer bears the costs of an employee's transfer to a new place of residence?
text: The following expenses qualify for exemption: transportation of the employee, members of his/her household and their personal goods from the previous place of residence to the new place of residence; costs incurred by the employee in respect of sale of the previous residence and in settling in permanent residential accommodation at the new place of residence; and cost of renting temporary residential accommodation for the employee and members of his/her household during a period which ends 183 days after the transfer took place or after the date of appointment.

title: What is the date of accrual if an employee is deceased before the date of payment?
text: Where the employee is deceased before the date of payment, the date of accrual is deemed to be the day before the employee's death.

title: From what date were the tax harmonization reforms for retirement funds implemented?
text: The tax harmonization reforms for Pension fund, Provident fund and Retirement Annuity fund were implemented from 1 March 2016.

title: How is employees' tax on overtime payments calculated?
text: Employees' tax on overtime payments is not calculated differently from tax on salaries. Any overtime payment must be added to the salary for the specific period and the employees' tax must be determined by using the applicable tax deduction tables.

title: What is the process for calculating tax on a fortnightly paid employee's partial pay period?
text: First, determine the decimal portion by dividing days worked by 14 (e.g., 8 ÷ 14 = 0.5714). Then calculate the annual equivalent by dividing the partial pay by the decimal portion and multiplying by 26 pay periods. Find the tax on the annual equivalent using the annual tax table, then multiply that tax by the decimal portion and divide by 26 to get the tax for the partial period.

title: What is the minimum number of days an officer or crew member of a ship must be outside the Republic to qualify for tax exemption on their remuneration?
text: An officer or crew member must be outside the Republic for a period or periods exceeding 183 full days in aggregate during the year of assessment to qualify for the tax exemption.

title: What code should be reflected on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate for remuneration paid to a labour broker with an exemption certificate?
text: The remuneration should be reflected under code 3619 on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate in the case of a labour broker with exemption certificate.

title: How is employees' tax calculated on backdated salary increases?
text: Employees' tax on backdated salary increases is calculated by first determining the tax on the increased monthly amount, then multiplying that by the number of months the increase is backdated. In the example, the tax on R300 is R79 per month, and for 5 months of backdated increase, the total tax is R395 (R79 x 5).

title: What specific employee records must an employer maintain according to the regulations?
text: The employer must maintain records including: the amount of remuneration paid; employees' tax deducted/withheld on all remuneration; UIF contributions; Income Tax reference number of that employee; and such further information as the Commissioner may prescribe.

title: What does Section 7B of the Act establish regarding the timing of variable remuneration?
text: Section 7B matches the timing between accrual and payment of various forms of remuneration. Amounts are deemed to have accrued to the employee when they are paid by the employer.

title: How is the contribution deduction adjusted when remuneration is paid for only part of the year?
text: The amount of contribution to be deducted must not exceed an amount that bears to the amount stipulated in section 11F(2)(a) the same ratio as the period during which remuneration was paid by employer to employee bears to a whole year.

title: What percentage of a travel allowance is subject to employees' tax deduction?
text: 80% of the travel allowance is subject to the deduction of employees' tax, unless the employer is satisfied that at least 80% of the motor vehicle use for the year will be for business purposes, in which case only 20% of the allowance will be subject to employees' tax.

title: What constitutes an offence regarding the use of deducted employees' tax?
text: Using or applying employees' tax deducted or withheld for purposes other than the payment of such amount to the Commissioner constitutes an offence.

title: What is the maximum percentage limit for donations deductible from an employee's remuneration under normal circumstances?
text: The deduction may not exceed 5% of remuneration after deducting pension, provident and retirement annuity fund contributions.

title: In the weekly paid employee example, what was the annual equivalent salary calculated to be?
text: The annual equivalent salary was calculated as R132 275, determined by the formula: R1 090 ÷ 0.4285 x 52.

title: When are amounts deemed to have accrued to an employee?
text: Amounts are deemed to have accrued to the employee when they are paid by the employer.

title: When should an employer issue IRP5/IT3(a) forms if a seasonal employee's employment extends across two tax years?
text: If the season extends over the following tax year, the employer must issue two IRP5/IT3(a) in respect of the two tax periods. For example, if a season extends from 15 November to 20 May, two IRP5/IT3(a)s must be issued for the two periods (15 November to 28 February for the 1st tax year and 1 March to 20 May for the 2nd tax year) as the employee has two tax periods in one service period.

title: What is the employer's obligation when paying remuneration to an employee?
text: Where an employer pays or becomes liable to pay remuneration to an employee, the employer has an obligation to deduct or withhold employees' tax from the remuneration and pay the tax deducted or withheld to the South African Revenue Service (SARS) on a monthly basis.

title: What contact number can be used to reach the SARS Contact Centre from within South Africa?
text: If calling from within South Africa, the SARS Contact Centre can be contacted on 0800 00 SARS (7277).

title: Under which code should overtime payments be reflected on the IRP5/IT3(a)?
text: Overtime payments should be reflected under code 3607 on the IRP5/IT3(a).

title: What is the maximum tax deduction limit for retirement fund contributions under the tax harmonization reforms?
text: The maximum tax deduction for retirement fund contributions is the lesser of: (a) R350,000; OR (b) 27.5% of the higher of remuneration as defined in the Fourth Schedule or taxable income before allowing certain deductions; OR (c) taxable income before allowing certain deductions and before inclusion of any taxable capital gain. All amounts exclude retirement fund lump sum benefit, retirement lump sum withdrawal benefit and severance benefit.

title: From what date are provident fund contributions allowable as a deduction?
text: Only provident fund contributions made on or after 1 March 2016 are allowable as a deduction. Contributions made pre-1 March 2016 are not allowed as a deduction.

title: When does an unrestricted equity instrument vest?
text: An unrestricted equity instrument vests when the employee acquires it.

title: What are the conditions for an employee to be deemed in standard employment?
text: An employee is deemed to be in standard employment where such employee renders services to the employer for 22 hours or less in every completed week, and the employee furnishes the employer with a written declaration stating that he/she does not or will not render services to another employer during the period he/she holds such employment at the relevant employer.

title: How should director's fees received by a resident NED be treated for tax purposes?
text: The director's fees received by a resident NED are not regarded as remuneration and the employer is not obligated to deduct/withhold employees' tax. However, the director can request a voluntary deduction of employees' tax, and if requested, this income must reflect under code 3620 on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate.
