title: What document must an employee with a disability submit to their employer to qualify for a bursary exemption?
text: The employee must submit the signed ITRDD form to the employer to confirm their disability is as defined in section 6B(1).

title: What is special remuneration paid to proto teams and when is it subject to employees' tax deduction?
text: Special remuneration paid to proto teams is any amount received by or accrued to any mineworker over and above his/her normal remuneration and any regular allowance in respect of special services rendered in combating fire, flood, subsidence or other disaster in a mine, rescuing persons trapped in a mine, or performing hazardous tasks during emergencies in a mine. Amounts paid to proto team members as special remuneration are subject to the deduction of employees' tax.

title: What code should be used to reflect the taxable portion of bursaries for Grade R and NQF levels 1 to 4?
text: Code 3829 should be used to reflect the taxable portion of bursaries for Grade R and NQF levels 1 to 4.

title: What is the effective date for the tax treatment of bursaries or scholarships granted by an employer that are subject to salary sacrifice?
text: The effective date is 01 March 2021. If the bursary or scholarship granted by the employer to the relative of the employee is subject to an element of salary sacrifice, the amount granted by the employer is taxable in the hands of the employee, although the employer can still claim the deduction in respect of this bursary or scholarship.

title: What happens to a bursary granted subject to repayment conditions if the employee fails to fulfill the stipulated conditions?
text: In the tax year in which non-compliance provisions are invoked, the amount of the bursary will be regarded as a loan and any benefit which an employee may have received by way of an interest-free or low-interest loan will constitute a taxable benefit in terms of Paragraph 2(f) of the Seventh Schedule.

title: What happens when a new purchase rate or equivalent rate is determined?
text: A new rate of interest applies from the first day of the month following the date on which that new purchase rate or equivalent rate came into operation.

title: Within what timeframe must an employer apply for registration for employees' tax purposes with SARS?
text: An employer must apply for registration for employees' tax purposes with SARS within 21 business days after he/she becomes an employer, unless none of the employees are liable for normal tax.

title: What happens to the vesting event gain or loss?
text: The vesting event (i.e. a gain or loss) will continue to remain in the hands of the employee/director.

title: What is the monthly fringe benefit percentage rate for employer-owned provided vehicles with maintenance plans included in the purchase price?
text: 3.25% per month of the vehicle's determined value

title: When does accrued leave become taxable remuneration?
text: Accrued leave becomes taxable remuneration only when it is paid or encashed. No value is placed on accrued leave until the encashment thereof. Leave can be encashed when an employee dies and accrued leave is paid to the employee's estate, employment is terminated for any reason, too much leave has accrued, the employee becomes insolvent, or it is encashed due to internal arrangements such as promotion.

title: How long must records be retained for retirement fund contributions?
text: Records need not be retained after a period of five years from the date of submission of the return and after a period of five years from the end of the relevant tax period. However, if records are relevant to an audit, investigation, objection or appeal, they must be retained until the audit is concluded or the assessment or decision becomes final.

title: What was the monthly UIF contribution amount effective from 1 June 2021?
text: R17 712 per month (R212 544 annually)

title: What is the timeframe within which a registered employer must notify the Commissioner after ceasing to be an employer?
text: Every person who is registered as an employer shall within 14 days after ceasing to be an employer, notify the Commissioner in writing of the fact that the employer has ceased to be an employer.

title: When will the exemption NOT apply to dividends derived from restricted equity instruments?
text: Dividends in respect of restricted equity instruments will not be exempt if the value of the underlying shares is liquidated in full or in part by means of a distribution before the restrictions on the shares are lifted.

title: What is the minimum number of hours an employee must work per month for the employer and employee to be required to contribute to UI?
text: An employee and employer are not required to contribute if the employee is employed for less than 24 hours a month.

title: Is unpaid leave or forfeited leave considered remuneration?
text: No, unpaid leave taken or forfeited leave do not constitute remuneration, as there is no value attached thereto.

title: What is the default tax deduction rate for non-standard employment income when no tax directive is in place?
text: Employees' tax must be calculated and deducted at 25% on the balance of remuneration for non-standard employment income.

title: What are the offences related to IRP5/IT3(a) certificates?
text: Offences related to IRP5/IT3(a) certificates include: failing to deliver them to employees or former employees within prescribed periods; permitting a false IRP5/IT3(a) to be issued or knowingly being in possession of or using a false IRP5/IT3(a); altering an IRP5/IT3(a) issued by another person; not being an employer and without authority issuing or causing to be issued any document purporting to be an IRP5/IT3(a); and failing to comply with conditions prescribed by the Commissioner regarding their use, surrender of unused stocks, or accounting for used, unused and spoiled certificates.

title: What are the conditions for an employee to be deemed in standard employment if they work less than 22 hours per week?
text: An employee working less than 22 hours per week will be deemed to be in standard employment if the employee furnishes a written declaration/affidavit to the employer that he/she will not render services to any other employer during the period of such employment.

title: What is the maximum exempt amount for a death lump sum benefit paid under the Compensation for Occupational Injuries and Diseases Act?
text: A maximum amount of R300 000 will be exempt.

title: What tax rate applies to taxable income between R237 101 and R370 500 for the 2025/2026 tax year?
text: For taxable income between R237 101 and R370 500, the tax is R42 678 plus 26% of taxable income above R237 100.

title: What is the reference code that should be reflected on the certificate according to the vesting of equity instruments guidance?
text: Code 3717 should be reflected on the certificate.

title: What is the effective date of this Guide for Employers in respect of Employees' Tax?
text: The effective date is 12 March 2025.

title: What should be done when backdated salary relates to periods in the current tax year?
text: Where the backdated salary/pension relates to periods in the current tax year, the employees' tax must be calculated by adding the backdated salary/pension to the remuneration received. The employer does not have to submit a directive application.

title: How do you calculate the decimal portion of a pay period for a weekly paid employee who works only part of the week?
text: You divide the number of days worked by the total number of days in the week. For example, if an employee works 3 days out of a 7-day week, the decimal portion is calculated as 3 ÷ 7 = 0.4285.

title: What is the tax threshold for persons 75 years and older in 2026?
text: The tax threshold for persons 75 years and older in 2026 is R165 689.

title: What are the main elements covered in Section 7 of the guide?
text: Section 7 covers determining the employees' tax, SDL and UIF liability, including elements required before employees' tax may be deducted, annual equivalent calculation, deductions to determine the balance of remuneration (retirement fund contributions, donations, and medical scheme fees tax credit), employees' tax deduction, SDL liable amount, and UIF liable amount.

title: What is the employer's right regarding PAYE paid on deemed remuneration of a director?
text: The employer has the right to recover the PAYE on the deemed remuneration paid by the company from the director. This recovery may be recovered from any amount which is or may become payable by the company to the director.

title: What should be excluded from the severance benefit amount on the tax directive application form?
text: Any amount paid or payable due to services rendered should not be included in the severance benefit amount on the tax directive application form. This includes amounts in terms of paragraph (c) or (f) of gross income, bonuses, pro-rata bonuses, notice pay, and leave pay. Leave pay is a payment in respect of services rendered and does not form part of a severance benefit.

title: What is the basis for calculating employees' tax deduction?
text: Employees' tax deduction is calculated on the balance of remuneration after the deduction of all allowable deductions such as Retirement annuity fund contribution, provident fund contribution, pension fund contribution and donations.

title: What is the treatment when an employee exchanges a qualifying equity share for another equity share?
text: If an employee disposes of a qualifying equity share in exchange solely for any other equity share, that other equity instrument in exchange is deemed to be a qualifying equity share which was acquired by the employee on the date of grant of the qualifying equity share disposed of in exchange, and acquired for a consideration equal to any consideration given for the qualifying equity share disposed of in exchange.

title: What is the difference in reporting between backdated salary relating to current year versus previous years on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate?
text: Code 3601 is used for the portion of salary which relates to periods in the current tax year, while Code 3907 is used for the portion of salary which relates to periods in previous tax years (a tax directive application must be submitted to SARS).

title: What is the total remuneration for December when an employee receives a R300 monthly salary increase backdated from 1 July?
text: The total remuneration for December is R29 800, which consists of the December salary of R28 300 (R28 000 + R300 increase) plus the backdated increased salary for July to November of R1 500 (R300 x 5 months).

title: What must an employer retain if they do not deduct employees' tax from a payment to a labour broker?
text: An employer who does not deduct employees' tax from a payment to a labour broker must be in possession of a certified copy of an exemption certificate (IRP30) that must be retained for inspection purposes.

title: What is the employees' tax deductible for June?
text: The employees' tax deductible for June is R4 570.

title: What is the difference in tax treatment between leave advances and accrued leave encashment?
text: Where leave is taken and an advance is paid for the leave period, the advance must be treated as advanced salary. Accrued leave that is encashed must be taxed in the same manner as a bonus.

title: What is the effective date of this guide for employers in respect of employees' tax?
text: The effective date is 12 March 2025.

title: What penalty is imposed on late payments or outstanding amounts of Employees' tax, SDL or UIF contributions?
text: A penalty equal to 10% in addition to the interest will be imposed on late payments or outstanding amounts.

title: What code should be used when accrued leave has been encashed due to encashment or resignation?
text: Accrued leave which has been encashed must reflect under code 3605 if it accrued as a result of encashment or resignation.

title: Under what circumstances will SARS not issue an exemption certificate to a labour broker?
text: SARS shall not issue an exemption certificate if more than 80% of the gross income of labour broker during the tax year consists of amounts received from any one client, unless the labour broker employs three or more full-time employees throughout the tax year who are not connected persons in relation to that labour broker, or who are on a full-time basis engaged in the business of that labour broker of providing persons to or procuring persons for clients of that labour broker. Additionally, SARS will not issue an exemption certificate if the labour broker provides to any of its clients the services of any other labor broker, or if the labour broker is contractually obliged to provide a specified employee of the labour broker to render any service to such client.

title: What is the current UIF contribution threshold effective from 1 June 2021?
text: The UIF contribution threshold effective from 1 June 2021 is R17 712 per month (R212 544 annually).

title: Is income earned by an independent contractor included in the definition of remuneration?
text: No, income earned by an independent contractor is specifically excluded from the definition of remuneration in Paragraph 1 of the Fourth Schedule.

title: What should an employer do if a labour broker does not have a valid exemption certificate?
text: If a labour broker is not in possession of a valid exemption certificate (IRP30), all payments made to the labour broker will be subject to employees' tax, and employees' tax must be deducted according to the applicable deduction tables.

title: From what date does the definition of employee include directors of private companies and members of close corporations?
text: The definition of employee includes a director of a private company, and any remuneration paid or payable to a director of a private company or a member of a close corporation is subject to the deduction of Employees' Tax from 1 March 2002.

title: How are stated amounts treated for determining the employer's taxable income?
text: For purposes of determining taxable income, the stated amounts will constitute expenditure for the employer in the year that the employer pays that amount to the employee.

title: What is the threshold amount for SDL registration based on leviable payroll?
text: An employer is not required to register for SDL purposes if there are reasonable grounds for believing that the total leviable amount paid or payable to all employees during the following 12 month period will not exceed R500 000.

title: What is the abatement amount for residential accommodation in the 2026 tax year?
Under what circumstances must employees' tax be deducted from gains made on the disposal of qualifying equity shares acquired through a broad-based employee share plan?
text: Employees' tax must be deducted from any amount received by or accrued to the employee during the year from any gain made from the disposal of any qualifying equity share or any right or interest in a qualifying equity share that was acquired in terms of a broad-based employee share plan and is disposed of by the employee within 5 years from the date of grant, unless the disposal is in exchange for another qualifying equity share, on the death of the employee, or on the insolvency of the employee.

title: Does the employment income exemption for officers or crew members of a ship apply to holders of public office?
text: No, the exemption does not apply in respect of any remuneration derived by the holder of any public office.

title: What codes should be used to reflect the non-taxable portion of bursaries for different education levels?
text: Code 3815 should be used for grade R to 12, and code 3821 should be used for NQF levels 5 to 10.

title: What are some examples of casual employment mentioned in the context?
text: Examples of casual employment include casual commissions paid such as spotter's fees, casual payments to casual workers for irregular services rendered or occasional services, fees paid to part-time lecturers, and honoraria paid to office bearers of organisations and clubs.

title: How is a bursary or scholarship taxed when it involves salary sacrifice and granted by an employer to a relative of the employee?
text: If the bursary or scholarship granted by the employer to the relative of the employee is subject to an element of salary sacrifice, the amount granted by the employer as a bursary or scholarship is taxable in the hands of the employee. However, the employer can still claim the deduction in respect of this bursary or scholarship. The effective date is 01 March 2021.

title: Which section of the Act provides the exemption for officers or crew members of a ship?
text: Section 10(1)(o) of the Act provides the exemption for officers or crew members of a ship.

title: What are the circumstances under which a restricted equity instrument vests immediately?
text: A restricted equity instrument vests immediately before the employee disposes of it (except for disposals discussed hereunder), after it terminates if it is an option, or before the employee dies if all restrictions relating to that equity instrument are or may be lifted on or after death.

title: What code should be used on the certificate for lump sum payments from a Provident fund with a date of accrual prior to 1 March 2009 in respect of withdrawal?
text: Lump sum payments from a Provident or Provident preservation fund with a date of accrual prior to 1 March 2009 must be reflected under code 3904 on the certificate.

title: How is employees' tax on an annual payment basically determined?
text: The employees' tax on an annual payment is basically determined by calculating the annual equivalent of the remuneration earned during the tax period by the employee and adding the annual payment to the result. The difference between tax on the total result (annual equivalent plus annual payment) and tax on the annual equivalent will result in employees' tax deductible from the annual payment.

title: What form must be used to apply for separate registration of branches?
text: Application must be made on an EMP102 form.

title: What is the prescribed formula for calculating annual equivalent?
text: The prescribed formula for calculating annual equivalent is: Total remuneration received/accrued × Total pay periods in tax year ÷ Total pay periods worked

title: According to paragraph 13(2)(bA) of the Seventh Schedule, what type of insurance premiums paid by an employer have no taxable benefit value?
text: Insurance premiums indemnifying an employee solely against claims arising from negligent acts or omissions on the part of the employee in rendering services to the employer have no value placed on any taxable benefit.

title: When must an employer submit a directive application according to Paragraph 9(3) of the Fourth Schedule?
text: According to Paragraph 9(3) of the Fourth Schedule, the employer must submit a directive application before paying out a lump sum to the employee.

title: What is a seasonal worker according to this guide?
text: A seasonal worker is a person who is only employed during a peak period for a specific period, such as persons employed on a fruit farm during the picking season, on a sheep farm during lambing or shearing, or in factories requiring additional help during the canning season.

title: In the provided example, what is the annual equivalent for a monthly paid employee who worked 7 full months and received R110,000?
text: The annual equivalent is R188,571, calculated as follows: R110,000 ÷ 7 × 12 = R188,571

title: What is the purpose of a tax directive issued by SARS?
text: A tax directive is an authorisation issued by SARS to the employer/fund administrator on how to deduct employees' tax from certain payments.

title: How should one distinguish between an employee and an independent contractor?
text: One must commence with an analysis of the employment contract and establish the object of the contract, which includes the parties' rights and obligations. The object of the contract is not a mere indicator but determines the legal nature of the contract. If the object is the surrender of productive capacity, it is an employment contract; if the object is the acquisition of the result of deployed productive capacity, it is an independent contractor contract.

title: What must an employer do to determine the amount of employees' tax to be deducted from a gain in respect of the vesting of an equity instrument?
text: The employer must apply for an IRP3 tax directive in order to ascertain the amount of employees' tax to be deducted or withheld from any gain in respect of the vesting of any equity instrument as defined in section 8C.

title: What source code must be used on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate when severance benefit reasons are used on the IRP3(a) directive application form?
text: Source code 3901 must be used on the IRP5/IT3(a) certificate when the employer uses the severance benefit reasons on the IRP3(a) directive application form. The full amount should be reflected under code 3901 and the tax under code 4115 on the IRP5 certificate. If an IT3(a) certificate is issued, reason code 04 must be used.

title: Which types of employers are exempt from paying the SDL levy?
text: Public service employers in the national or provincial sphere of Government and national or provincial public entities if 80% or more of their expenditure is paid directly or indirectly from funds voted by Parliament are exempt from paying the SDL levy.

title: Which government employees are not applicable for tax exemption on services rendered?
text: Tax exemption is not applicable to any person employed in the national, provincial or local sphere of government, any constitutional institution, a public entity or a municipality entity in respect of services rendered or work or labour performed.

title: What must an employer do if an employee who works on commission is in possession of a tax directive?
text: If the employee is in possession of a tax directive, the employer MUST deduct employees' tax according to the instructions on the tax directive and the employees' tax deducted must reflect as PAYE on the IRP5/IT3(a).

title: What must a labour broker do if they undergo a change of name while holding a valid exemption certificate?
text: If a labour broker is in possession of a valid exemption certificate and undergoes a change of name, the original certificate must be returned to the relevant SARS.

title: What are the conditions under which a severance benefit qualifies for tax purposes?
text: A severance benefit qualifies if: (1) the person has attained the age of 55 years; (2) the relinquishment, termination, loss, repudiation, cancellation or variation is due to the person becoming permanently incapable of holding their office or employment due to sickness, accident, injury or incapacity through infirmity of mind or body; or (3) the termination or loss is due to the employer ceasing to carry on or intending to cease carrying on the trade, or the person becoming redundant due to a general reduction in personnel or reduction of a particular class, unless the person held more than five per cent of issued share capital or members' interest in the company.

title: Within how many business days must an employer inform SARS of changes to registered particulars?
text: An employer must inform SARS in writing within 21 business days of any change in registered particulars.

title: When is the time of disposal of an equity instrument determined?
text: The time of disposal of an equity instrument is the time that the equity instrument vests in the beneficiary as contemplated in section 8C.

title: When is an unrestricted equity instrument deemed to vest?
text: An unrestricted equity instrument is deemed to vest when the employee acquires it.

title: What types of retirement funds are eligible for employer deduction according to the context?
text: The employer must deduct contributions made by the employee to any pension fund, provident fund, and/or retirement annuity fund (RAF) which the employer is entitled or required to deduct from the employee's remuneration.

title: What types of allowances may be paid by an employer to an employee according to the guide?
text: The following types of allowances may be paid by an employer to an employee: Travel allowance, Subsistence allowance, Allowance to a holder of a public office, and Other allowances received by virtue of the employee's office or duties (e.g. uniform allowance, etc.).

title: What anti-avoidance provision was included regarding equity instruments acquired from employees or directors?
text: An anti-avoidance provision was included to guard against situations where co-employees and directors collude to avoid the deferment of taxation that section 8C achieves. With effect from 1 January 2011, rollover treatment will apply to equity instruments acquired from employees or directors of same employer.

title: Which employers must register with the UI Commissioner?
text: Employers who are not required to register for employees' tax purposes at SARS, employers who have not registered voluntarily as an employer for employees' tax purposes at SARS, and employers who are not liable for the payment of SDL must register with the UI Commissioner.

title: What is the tax amount deducted on R6 500 for 15 days worked in the first month?
text: R453. This is calculated by taking the tax on the annual equivalent (R10 879), dividing by 12 months, and multiplying by 0.5 (representing half a month): R10 879 ÷ 12 x 0.5 = R453

title: What types of benefits are excluded when calculating the remuneration or taxable income for contribution deduction purposes?
text: Retirement fund lump sum benefit, retirement lump sum withdrawal benefit, and severance benefit are excluded from both the remuneration as defined in Fourth Schedule and the taxable income calculations for contribution deduction purposes.

title: What types of employment-related amounts are subject to Employees' Tax deduction?
text: Amounts awarded in respect of termination of an employment contract prior to its expiry date are remuneration as defined in the Fourth Schedule and are therefore subject to the deduction of Employees' Tax. Additionally, amounts received or accrued in respect of the relinquishment, termination, loss, repudiation, cancellation or variation of any office or employment are subject to such deduction.

title: How are severance benefits treated when they become payable following a person's death?
text: Any amount which becomes payable in consequence of or following upon the death of a person must be deemed to be an amount which accrued to such person immediately prior to his or her death.

title: When is a low-interest or interest-free loan from an employer considered a taxable benefit?
text: When the employee is not required to repay the loan, they will have received a taxable benefit in terms of Paragraph 2(h) of the Seventh Schedule (payment of employee's debt or release employee from obligation to pay debt), and employees' tax must be deducted.

title: What is the specific requirement for using Code 3771?
text: Code 3771 MUST only be used for local dividends linked to foreign services.

title: What is the effective date of this guide for employers?
text: The effective date of this guide is 12 March 2025.

title: What is the definition of standard employment in terms of working hours?
text: Standard employment is any employment where an employee is required to render services to a single employer for a period of at least 22 hours in every full week, provided that periods of temporary absence due to leave or exceptional circumstances, or temporary reduction in working hours due to reduced demand, are accommodated.

title: From what date must night shift allowance and standby allowance be included in an employee's remuneration?
text: From 1 March 2020, any night shift allowance and any standby allowance must be included in the employee's remuneration in the year when it is actually paid to the employee.

title: What is the applicable treatment for lump sum payments due to retrenchment accruing before 1 March 2009?
text: Lump sum payments due to retrenchment accruing before 1 March 2009 must be dealt with as withdrawal benefit and the average rate in terms of section 5(10) of the Income Tax Act is applicable. Either source code 3902 or 3904 must be used.

title: What particulars must an employee supply to their employer according to Paragraph 14(1) of the Fourth Schedule?
text: An employee must supply the following particulars to their employer: surname and full names; address; South African Identity number or passport number and date of birth; Income Tax reference number (if any); and a written declaration where required.

title: Under which code should UIF contributions be reflected?
text: Code 4141

title: What is the tax treatment when an employee disposes of a restricted equity instrument to a connected person through a non-arm's length disposal?
text: Where a taxpayer disposes of an acquired restricted equity instrument to any person by either a non-arm's length disposal or disposal to a connected person, this will not be regarded as vesting of the equity instrument and will not attract a taxable gain or loss.

title: What is Form A & D used for according to the guide?
text: Form A & D is used for lump sum benefits paid by pension and/or provident fund, such as death before retirement, retirement due to ill health, or retirement.

title: What is advance salary and under which code should it be reflected on the IRP5/IT3(a)?
text: Advance salary is an amount of future remuneration paid by the employer prior to the actual date on which such remuneration becomes payable. An advance payment will be subject to the deduction of employees' tax when it is paid by the employer to the employee, and it should be reflected under code 3601 on the certificate.

title: Which division of the South African Revenue Service prepared this brochure?
text: Legal and Policy Division

title: In Example 3, how much of X's foreign interest income is exempt from tax?
text: R2 400 of X's foreign interest income is exempt from tax, calculated as R3 200 (total exemption) less R800 (foreign dividend exemption already applied).

title: What are the two main conditions for an employee of a foreign diplomatic or consular mission in South Africa to be exempt from normal tax?
text: The two main conditions are: (1) the employee is stationed in South Africa for the sole purpose of holding office in South Africa as an official of a foreign government; and (2) the employee is not ordinarily resident in South Africa.

title: What documentation is required to claim a rebate for foreign taxes paid?
text: A resident must submit proof of foreign taxes paid or payable. An assessment or the equivalent thereof, tax receipts or an official document will generally be accepted as proof of foreign tax paid or payable.

title: What is the starting point in calculating taxable income?
text: The starting point in calculating taxable income is to determine the amount of gross income, which represents the total amount, in cash or otherwise (excluding receipts or accruals of a capital nature), received by or accrued to a person.

title: Are South African residents employed by foreign diplomatic or consular missions in South Africa exempt from normal tax?
text: No, South African residents who are employed by foreign diplomatic or consular missions in South Africa (locally-recruited employees) are not exempt from normal tax on their income received from that mission.

title: What notification requirement exists for persons responsible for founding, organising or facilitating a specified activity in South Africa?
text: Any person who is primarily responsible for founding, organising or facilitating a specified activity in South Africa and who will be rewarded directly or indirectly for that function must notify SARS of the performance within 14 days of concluding the agreement and provide SARS with the details relating thereto as may be required by SARS.

title: What is the requirement for a pension to be taxable in South Africa based on years of service?
text: At least two years out of the last 10 years of services (before the accrual of the pension) must have been rendered in South Africa. The taxable portion is calculated in the ratio which the number of years' service rendered in South Africa bears to the total period during which services were rendered.

title: How is the balance of foreign dividends treated in a resident's taxable income?
text: The balance of foreign dividends will be included in the resident's taxable income.

title: How is the capital gain or loss on foreign immovable property determined when acquired and disposed of in the same foreign currency?
text: The capital gain or capital loss is determined in the foreign currency and translated to rand by applying either the average exchange rate for the tax year in which the asset was disposed of, or the spot rate on the date of disposal of the asset.

title: What was the main change to South Africa's income tax system that took effect from tax years commencing on or after 1 March 2001?
text: South Africa moved from a source-based system of taxation to a residence basis of taxation. Under this new system, all worldwide income of a natural person who is a resident is subject to normal tax, subject to certain exclusions.

title: When did the tax on foreign entertainers and sportspersons come into effect?
text: The tax on foreign entertainers and sportspersons came into effect on 1 August 2006.

title: What percentage of taxable capital gain is included in a natural person's taxable income?
text: 25% of the taxable capital gain, after deducting the annual exclusion, is included in the natural person's taxable income and is subject to normal tax at their marginal rate of tax.

title: What is the threshold for a South African resident to be exempt from section 9D imputation?
text: A resident is exempt from section 9D imputation when the resident (together with any connected person) in aggregate holds less than 10% of the participation rights and may not exercise at least 10% of the voting rights in that company.

title: What is the maximum period for which excess foreign tax credits can be carried forward?
text: Excess foreign tax credits may be carried forward for a maximum of seven years calculated from the tax year when it was for the first time carried forward.

title: How is the ratio of net income determined for each resident under section 9D?
text: The ratio of the net income to be determined for any one resident is the proportion that the resident's participation rights bears to all the participation rights in the company.

title: How is remuneration taxed when an employee receives it during one tax year for services rendered over multiple tax years?
text: The remuneration will be taxed evenly over the period during which those services were rendered.

title: In what currency must the net income calculation for a CFC be translated to, and what exchange rate should be applied?
text: The net income calculation must be translated to South African Rand by applying the average exchange rate for the tax year during which the net income is included in the resident's income.

title: What are the two components of 'participation rights' as defined in section 9D(1)?
text: According to section 9D(1), participation rights consist of: (a) the right to participate directly or indirectly in the share capital, share premium, current or accumulated profits or reserves of that company, whether or not of a capital nature; or (b) in the case where no person has any right as contemplated in paragraph (a) or no such rights can be determined for any person, the right to exercise any voting rights in that company.

title: What is the maximum limit for foreign tax credits and how long can excess amounts be carried forward?
text: Foreign tax credits can never exceed the South African normal tax payable on the total amount of foreign taxable income in the hands of a resident during a tax year. Excess amounts may be carried forward for possible setting off in succeeding years but may not be carried forward for more than seven years calculated from the tax year when it was for the first time carried forward.

title: What are the three requirements that must be met for a non-resident's employment income to not be subject to normal tax in South Africa?
text: The three requirements are: (1) He or she is present in South Africa for a period or periods in aggregate not exceeding 183 days in any 12-month period (not necessarily a tax year); (2) His or her remuneration is paid by, or on behalf of an employer who is not a resident of South Africa; and (3) His or her remuneration is not borne by a 'permanent establishment' that the employer has in South Africa.

title: What is the tax treatment of business income received by a non-resident from carrying on a trade or business within South Africa?
text: Business income received by or accrued to a non-resident from carrying on a trade or business within South Africa is subject to normal tax in South Africa. The taxability of the income may be affected by an agreement for the avoidance of double taxation.

title: What is the 330-day rule for residents in South Africa?
text: A natural person who qualified as a resident in terms of the physical presence test ceased to be a resident if he or she was physically outside the Republic for a continuous period of at least 330 full days. He or she will be a non-resident as from the commencement date of the 330-day period which began on the day after the day on which he or she left the Republic.

title: What credit can residents claim in respect of foreign dividends subject to normal tax in South Africa?
text: Residents may claim a credit in respect of foreign taxes paid by them against their normal tax liability in South Africa in respect of the foreign dividends and/or interest subject to normal tax in South Africa.

title: What defines a controlled foreign company (CFC)?
text: A controlled foreign company (CFC) is any foreign company where more than 50% of the total participation rights in that foreign company are held, or more than 50% of the voting rights in that foreign company are directly or indirectly exercisable, by one or more residents.

title: What is the definition of a 'permanent establishment' according to the context?
text: A 'permanent establishment' means in essence a fixed place of business through which the business of the employer is wholly or partly conducted.

title: What type of residence qualifies as 'ordinarily resident' in terms of its nature?
text: A residence that is settled and certain and not temporary and casual qualifies as 'ordinarily resident', or where a person normally resides, apart from temporary or occasional absences.

title: What are the three main topics covered in this guide on residence basis of taxation?
text: The guide provides general guidelines dealing with: (1) foreign income received by or accrued to an individual who is a South African resident; (2) income received by or accrued to an individual who is not a South African resident from a source within South Africa; and (3) the treatment of capital gains in the hands of a resident or a non-resident.

title: Which division of the South African Revenue Service prepared this brochure?
text: This brochure was prepared by the Legal and Policy Division of the South African Revenue Service

title: What is the withholding tax rate applied to know-how payments received by non-residents for intellectual property use in South Africa?
text: The withholding tax rate is 12%, or a rate determined in the relevant agreement for the avoidance of double taxation.

title: What items can be deducted from the estimated provisional tax payable for a provisional tax period?
text: Employees' tax, any foreign taxes paid or proved to be payable by the provisional taxpayer to the government of another country, and any provisional tax already paid during that year of assessment can be deducted from the estimated provisional tax that is payable for the relevant provisional tax period.

title: What is the timeframe within which an individual who becomes liable for income tax must apply to SARS for registration?
text: An individual who becomes liable for any income tax or who must submit an income tax return must apply to SARS for registration as a taxpayer within 21 business days of becoming liable. A business day means a day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday.

title: What are the benefits of electronic submission of income tax returns promoted by SARS?
text: SARS promotes electronic submission of income tax returns as it allows for the quick generation and submission of income tax returns, easy payments, and interaction with SARS in a secure online environment.

title: What documents should an individual verify against the pre-populated income tax return?
text: An individual must verify the pre-populated information against any IRP5/IT3(a) certificate(s) and any other relevant tax certificates, such as those issued by a medical scheme or retirement annuity fund.

title: Which taxation amendments are included in this 2024/25 guide?
text: This guide takes into consideration the amendments up to and including the Taxation Laws Amendment Act 42 of 2024, Tax Administration Laws Amendment Act 43 of 2024, and the Revenue Laws Second Amendment Act 44 of 2024, which were all promulgated on 24 December 2024.

title: What are the methods available to submit an income tax return to SARS?
text: An individual can submit the income tax return electronically to SARS via eFiling or through the SARS MobiApp, or online at one of the SARS service centres by appointment only.

title: How does the Commissioner communicate who is required to furnish an income tax return?
text: Every year, the Commissioner announces by way of a public notice in the Government Gazette who is required to furnish an income tax return for the assessment of tax. The notice will also prescribe the period within which the income tax return must be furnished for the years of assessment specified in that notice.

title: What is the capital gain threshold for residents who must submit an income tax return?
text: A resident must submit an income tax return if they had a capital gain or capital loss exceeding R40 000.

title: What is the foreign asset threshold for South African residents required to submit an income tax return?
text: A resident in South Africa must submit an income tax return if they held or owned any funds in foreign currency or assets outside South Africa, if their total value exceeded R250 000 at any time during the year of assessment.

title: What is employees' tax and how is it also referred to?
text: Employees' tax is the tax that employers are required to deduct or withhold from the remuneration of employees (for example, salaries, wages and bonuses etc) and pay over to SARS on a monthly basis. It is also referred to ordinarily as 'Pay As You Earn' (PAYE).

title: What is another name for an employees' tax certificate?
text: An employees' tax certificate is also known as an IRP5.

title: What is the total gross income received by the employee in the example?
text: The total gross income received is R323 000, which comprises pensionable salary (R230 000), overtime pay (R17 000), bonus (R25 000), interest from South African banks (R24 000), and gross rental income on fixed property (R27 000).

title: When is the first provisional tax payment due?
text: The first provisional tax payment is due six months after the beginning of the particular year of assessment. If the last day of August falls on a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday, the payment must be made no later than the last business day before the Saturday, Sunday or public holiday.

title: Can a Notice of Appeal form be emailed or posted to SARS?
text: No, a Notice of Appeal form cannot be emailed or posted to SARS. Only electronic submission via eFiling or at the SARS service centre is allowed.

title: What is the tax threshold amount for individuals aged 65 years but under 75 in the 2025 year of assessment?
text: The tax threshold amount for individuals aged 65 years but under 75 in the 2025 year of assessment is R148 217.

title: What is the tax threshold for persons aged 75 years or older?
text: The tax threshold for persons aged 75 years or older is R165 689.

title: What happens if a person fails to provide all particulars and documents requested by SARS during registration?
text: A person who fails to provide all particulars and documents requested may be regarded as not having applied for registration until all the required details have been submitted.

title: What should an individual do if they cannot submit an original return within 40 business days after an auto-assessment?
text: An individual may request an extension to file the return. SARS may extend the period to submit if SARS receives the request for extension, together with reasonable grounds for requesting the extension, before the expiry of the 40 business days.

title: What is the tax rate for a person with taxable income of R250 000 in the 2024/25 year of assessment?
text: For taxable income exceeding R237 100 but not exceeding R370 500, the tax is R42 678 plus 26 per cent of the amount by which taxable income exceeds R237 100. Therefore, the tax would be R42 678 plus 26% of (R250 000 - R237 100) = R42 678 + R3 354 = R46 032.

title: What should an individual do if they are unsure whether they need to submit an income tax return?
text: An individual who is unsure as to whether an income tax return must be submitted may consult the SARS website, which contains an interactive set of questions designed to assist in deciding whether an income tax return is required.

title: What documents should be provided to support business travel expenses claims?
text: A logbook and other documents in support of business travel expenses should be provided if the individual is in receipt of a travel allowance.

title: What is the maximum number of months that fixed amount penalties can increase for if an individual's address is known to SARS?
text: The penalty increases every month by the same amount, up to a maximum of 35 months if the individual's address is known to SARS.

title: What dates does the 2025 year of assessment cover for an individual?
text: The 2025 year of assessment started on 1 March 2024 and ended on 28 February 2025.

title: When is the voluntary third or top-up provisional tax payment due for the 2025 year of assessment?
text: The voluntary third or top-up provisional tax payment for the 2025 year of assessment is due on or before 30 September 2025. This payment is made seven months after the end of the year of assessment on the last business day of the month.

title: What are the acceptable methods for submitting a Notice of Objection (DISP01)?
text: The Notice of Objection (DISP01) can only be submitted via eFiling or at a SARS service centre (by appointment only). The DISP01 cannot be e-mailed or posted to SARS.

title: What are the penalties for filing a return without authority to SARS?
text: A person convicted of filing a return without authority could be subject to a fine or imprisonment for a period not exceeding two years.

title: What methods can an auto-assessed individual use to view their Notice of Assessment?
text: An individual can view the Assessment on eFiling or the SARS MobiApp (if an eFiler), on WhatsApp (0800 11 72 77), or by dialing *134*7277#

title: Where can comments on the Guide on Income Tax and the Individual be sent?
text: Comments on this guide may be e-mailed to policycomments@sars.gov.za.

title: What is the foreign asset threshold for South African residents?
text: A resident in South Africa must submit an income tax return if they held or owned any funds in foreign currency or assets outside South Africa, if their total value exceeded R250 000 at any time during the year of assessment.

title: What are the five types of understatements that trigger understatement penalties?
text: The five types of understatements that trigger understatement penalties are: failure to submit a return; an omission from a return; an incorrect statement in a return; if no return is required, the failure to pay the correct amount of tax; and an impermissible avoidance arrangement.

title: What are the conditions for requesting an extension after 40 business days from the date of estimated assessment?
text: A request for extension received after 40 business days from the date of estimated assessment may only be granted if: (1) the individual's request is submitted to SARS within 21 business days after the expiry of the 40 business days and outlines reasonable grounds for not requesting the extension in time, or (2) the individual's request is submitted to SARS within three years after the expiry of the 40 business days and outlines exceptional circumstances for not requesting an extension in time.

title: According to the Minister of Finance, how can the income tax payable by individuals be reduced?
text: The income tax payable by individuals can be reduced if everyone pays his or her fair share of income tax – the more of us who pay, the less we all have to pay.

title: What is the deadline for submitting an objection to a SARS assessment?
text: An objection must be submitted within 80 business days after the date of the assessment or SARS decision. If an individual has requested reasons for the assessment, the objection must be submitted within 80 business days after either the date of the notice sent by SARS that adequate reasons have been provided, or the date SARS provided the individual with the reasons or further reasons.

title: What is the primary rebate amount for the 2025 year of assessment?
text: The primary rebate for the 2025 year of assessment is R17 235.

title: Must a non-resident who carried on trade outside South Africa submit an income tax return?
text: No. A non-resident must submit an income tax return only if they carried on any trade (other than solely as an employee) in South Africa, not outside South Africa.

title: What penalty applies if a provisional taxpayer pays the first or second provisional payment after the due date?
text: A provisional taxpayer who pays the first or second provisional payment after the due date for payment is liable to pay a penalty of 10% of the amount unpaid by the due date.

title: What are the penalties for filing a return with a forged signature to SARS?
text: A person convicted of submitting a return or other document to SARS under a forged signature could be subject to a fine or imprisonment for a period not exceeding two years.

title: What are the circumstances under which interest may be charged on income tax?
text: Interest at the prescribed rate may be charged under two circumstances: (a) Late payment - if an individual is late in paying income tax due on assessment, or if provisional tax is not paid in full within the applicable prescribed period; and (b) Underpayment - if the provisional tax paid for a year of assessment is not sufficient to offset the individual's assessed final income tax liability in full.

title: How much of the interest income is exempt from tax in this example?
text: South African interest is limited to R23 800, which is the amount that qualifies as exempt income. The employee received R24 000 in interest, but only R23 800 is exempt.

title: When can the final income tax payable by an individual be calculated?
text: The final income tax payable by an individual can only be calculated once the total taxable income earned by the individual for the full year of assessment has been determined. This is normally only carried out after the end of the year of assessment when the income tax return has been processed and an assessment has been issued.

title: What are examples of biometric recognition methods?
text: Examples of biometric recognition methods include fingerprint recognition, facial recognition, vocal recognition, and iris or retina recognition.

title: What happens when an individual is auto registered by SARS?
text: An individual who is auto registered will receive an SMS and a letter from SARS notifying him or her of the registration and informing him or her of how to access SARS's services and what his or her rights and obligations are.

title: How is tax calculated on retirement fund lump sum withdrawal benefits for taxable income exceeding R726,000 but not exceeding R1,089,000?
text: R125,730 plus 27 per cent of the amount by which taxable income exceeds R726,000

title: How can someone get more information about the objections and appeals process?
text: For more information on the objections and appeals process, one can visit the SARS website, call the SARS Service Centre, or visit the nearest SARS service centre by appointment only.

title: What is the local phone number to contact the SARS Service Centre?
text: If calling locally, the SARS Service Centre can be contacted on 0800 00 7277.

title: What form must be completed for disability-related tax claims?
text: The Confirmation of Diagnosis of Disability (ITR-DD) form must be completed where applicable.

title: Who is eligible for the tertiary rebate and what is its amount for 2025?
text: An individual who is 75 years of age or older is eligible for the tertiary rebate. The tertiary rebate for the 2025 year of assessment is R3 145.

title: What happens to foreign tax credits that are not allowed in a particular year of assessment?
text: Any foreign tax credits not allowed in a particular year of assessment may be carried forward to the subsequent year of assessment and are subject to certain restrictions.

title: What does the GREEN indicator ribbon mean for a VAT return?
text: The GREEN indicator means 'Return was received'. For example, the return for tax period ending February was received by the due date in March.

title: What VAT201 return statuses will be displayed in the menu?
text: VAT201 Returns with the following statuses will be displayed: Submitted to SARS, Processed by SARS, Manually Submitted, and Selected for Audit.

title: Which industries qualify for the Offshore diesel refund?
text: Commercial fishing, coastal shipping, offshore mining and the National Sea Rescue Institute will qualify for the Offshore diesel refund.

title: What types of storage locations can be browsed when selecting documents to upload?
text: The option allows you to browse for documents saved on the computer, external storage devices and networks.

title: What is the effective date of the VAT201 Return External Guide?
text: The effective date is 12 May 2025.

title: What documentation is required before completing Field 15 for other goods and/or services supplied?
text: A prescribed document, for example a valid tax invoice, must be held before completing any amount in Field 15.

title: What is the first step to register on eFiling for VAT purposes?
text: Navigate to www.sars.gov.za and click "Login".

title: What must be reported in Field 5 regarding accommodation supply?
text: Field 5 must contain the VAT exclusive amount of commercial accommodation supplied exceeding 28 days. Specifically, commercial accommodation provided for a full period of more than 28 days must be reported in Field 5, and the amount charged must be an all-inclusive charge that includes the charge for domestic goods and services.

title: When must a vendor complete the Customs Code field on the VAT201 form?
text: The vendor must complete the Customs Code field if fields 2A, 14A or 15A (or any combination thereof) are completed.

title: What does a minus sign (-) indicate in Field 20 of the VAT201 Return?
text: If the difference between Field 13 and 19 is less than zero, a minus (-) sign must be added in the first block before the amount entered in Field 20. The minus sign (-) will indicate that a refund is due.

title: Which banks support payment submission via eFiling for VAT201 returns?
text: eFiling is supported by: Absa, Bidvest Bank, Capitec Bank, Citibank, FNB, HSBC, Investec, Nedbank, Standard Bank, Standard Chartered, Mercantile Bank, AlBaraka, Sasfin, and HBZABSA.

title: What details must a vendor provide when requesting a VAT201 Return in person at a SARS office?
text: When requesting the VAT201 Return in person at a SARS office, the vendor must provide: the business' trading name, name and surname of the requester, the VAT registration number, the tax period for which the return will be made, business postal address, and customs code (if applicable).

title: What must a vendor do when requesting a manual correction to a VAT201 Return?
text: When a vendor intends to adjust any VAT201 Return to reflect the correct amounts, they must: (i) Request a new VAT201 Return for the relevant tax period from SARS, which will have a pre-populated PRN that is the same as the original VAT201 Return; (ii) Complete the new VAT201 Return for the specific tax period where the correction needs to be made; (iii) Complete the full amounts on the new VAT201 Return (NOT the difference between the amounts completed on the current and previous Return); and (iv) Make any additional payment using the same PRN.

title: Where will the Assessment Notice (VAT217) be displayed to reflect Section 95(1)(c) estimates?
text: The Assessment Notice (VAT217) will be displayed on the VAT Work Page to reflect the Section 95(1)(c) estimates and reason(s) for assessment.

title: What does the Payment Reference Number (PRN) in the Refund Details section represent?
text: The Payment Reference Number (PRN) is a number that will be generated by SARS when the VAT201 Return is issued on eFiling.

title: When should relevant material be uploaded to SARS?
text: Relevant material must only be uploaded if a letter is received from SARS requesting the vendor to submit relevant material.

title: What types of VAT payable can be entered in Field 12 for other and imported services?
text: Only VAT payable in respect of the following may be entered in Field 12: Debit notes issued; Credit notes received; Recovery of irrecoverable debts; Barter transactions; Services imported by you for purposes of making non taxable supplies.

title: What are the restrictions on adjusting amounts in Part A and Part B of a VAT201 Return?
text: Amounts under Part A can be increased or decreased, however amounts under Part B can only be decreased.

title: How is the Total refund amount calculated if a vendor is registered for diesel?
text: If registered for diesel and amounts are entered in the diesel section, the Total field is calculated as Fields 20 – (25 + 29 + 33).

title: Which banking institutions accept payments?
text: Payment can be made at any ABSA, Capitec, FNB, Nedbank or Standard Bank branch.

title: What is mandatory for Field 14A - Capital goods imported by you?
text: The Customs Code field is mandatory for Field 14A. This field applies to capital goods imported in respect of which a bill of entry valid release document and receipt for the payment of the VAT issued by Customs is held.

title: What should you do if you have forgotten your eFiling username or password?
text: If you have forgotten your username and/or password, click on the 'Forgot Your Username?' or 'Forgot Your Password' hyperlinks.

title: Which fields should vendors use to deduct input tax for capital goods and services with a 14% VAT rate?
text: Vendors should use field 18 – "Other" of the VAT201 to deduct the input tax for all capital goods and services where VAT rate of 14% is applicable.

title: What types of acquisitions are examples of goods and/or services that should be completed in Field 15?
text: Examples include accounting fees, advertisements, commission paid, cleaning materials, short term insurance premiums, membership fees, rent, repairs, second-hand goods (notional input tax), stationery, stock purchases, telephone, and water and lights.

title: What are the two scenarios in which a vendor can request a correction on a filed VAT201 Return?
text: The two scenarios are: i) Overstated Return, where the vendor wants to decrease the return for a specific tax period, and ii) Understated Return, where the vendor wants to increase the return for a specific tax period.

title: What is the deadline for SARS to receive a VAT201 return and payment when both are submitted via SARS eFiling?
text: When both the VAT201 return and payment are submitted via SARS eFiling website, SARS must receive both by the last business day of the month.

title: What is the difference between Field 23 and Field 26 in the VAT201 Return?
text: Field 23 represents eligible purchases (onland) calculated as total purchases less non-eligible purchases, while Field 26 represents the total amount of diesel purchases in litres for both eligible and non-eligible purchases in the offshore section.

title: What information will accompany the VATSOA?
text: The VATSOA will always be accompanied by notes to assist the reader on the interpretation of the account.

title: What are some examples of zero-rated supplies that should be reported in Field 2?
text: Examples of zero-rated supplies include brown bread, eggs of domesticated chickens, edible legumes and pulses, fresh/frozen fruit and vegetables, dried beans, illuminating kerosene, lentils, maize meal, milk and dairy products, pilchards/sardines in tins or cans, vegetable oil (excluding olive oil), fuel levy goods such as petrol and diesel, the sale of a business as a going concern, services for goods temporarily admitted for repair or servicing, and international travel.

title: What contact information is mandatory for the person responsible for completing the VAT201 Return?
text: Either the business telephone number or cell phone number must be provided. The business telephone number must commence with the relevant area code. If the business number is not provided, the cell phone number is mandatory.

title: What are three examples of exempt supplies?
text: Three examples of exempt supplies are: financial services, donated goods or services by an association not for gain, and residential accommodation.

title: What types of capital goods and services are examples for Field 14 in the VAT201 Return?
text: Examples of capital goods and services that should be completed in Field 14 include: Office equipment, Furniture, Trucks, and Land and buildings. A prescribed document, such as a valid tax invoice, must be held before completing any amount in this field.

title: How is the letter from SARS requesting relevant material issued and accessed?
text: The letter is issued via eFiling and can be accessed under 'SARS Correspondence'.

title: What is the structure of the 19-digit Payment Reference Number (PRN) on the VAT201 Return?
text: The 19-digit PRN is structured as follows: Digits 1-10 represent your VAT registration number, and digits 11-19 are systematically allocated by SARS. For example: 4123456789VC2011091

title: What must be inserted in the Y box?
text: An X must be inserted in the Y box.

title: What are the main sections covered in the guide for completing the VAT201 return?
text: The main sections include: Requesting the VAT201 Return, Registering on eFiling for VAT purposes, Viewing VAT Filing Control Table, Requesting a VAT201 Return on eFiling, Completing the VAT201 Return (including sections on Diesel, Vendor Details, Contact Details, Output Tax, Input Tax, Diesel Refund, Payment Details, and Refund Details), Submitting the VAT201 Return, Additional/Revised Estimated VAT Assessments, Making a Payment, Request for Correction, Submitting Supporting Documents, Viewing Historic Returns, and Requesting a Statement of Account.

title: What are examples of exempt supplies under VAT?
text: Examples of exempt supplies include: financial services, donated goods or services by an association not for gain, residential accommodation, the letting of leasehold land, the sale or letting of land situated outside the Republic, transport of fare-paying passengers by road or railway, the supply of educational services, membership contributions to employee organisations such as trade unions, and the supply of childcare services.

title: How is Field 4 calculated on the VAT201 Return?
text: Field 4 must be equal to Field 1 x (r/ (100 + r)), where r is the current VAT rate of 15%. This amount is automatically calculated on the VAT201 Return but will be editable if the vendor disagrees with the automatic calculations.

title: What should be remembered when completing the remainder of the VAT201 Return after VDP is granted?
text: When completing the remainder of the return, do not forget to include all amounts applicable for the tax year for which VDP was granted, including the previously omitted information.

title: Is output tax levied on exempt supplies?
text: No, no output tax is levied in respect of exempt supplies.

title: What error message is displayed when a vendor attempts to request an extension again after SARS has already received their initial request but they haven't received the Confirmation Notice?
text: The error message displayed is: 'The Request for Extension previously received is under review and SARS will communicate the outcome of the request'.

title: How can a vendor obtain a VATSOA for a single tax period?
text: Where a vendor requires a VATSOA for a single tax period, the vendor must select the same tax period as both the 'From' and the 'To' tax period. This statement will display all the transactions related to the selected period.

title: Will SARS officials assist vendors by analyzing their financial data and calculating amounts for the VAT201 Return?
text: No. SARS officials will not be able to assist by analysing the vendor's financial data and calculating the amounts to be entered in the relevant fields of the VAT201 Return. The vendor/representative will have to be present to sign the completed VAT201 Return to authorise the submission at the SARS office.

title: What types of supplies must be completed in Field 1A of the VAT201 Return?
text: Field 1A must be completed with the VAT inclusive amount of goods and/or services supplied at the standard rate, but only for capital goods and/or services. This includes the sale of capital goods and/or services (such as sale of land and buildings, plant and machinery, intellectual property) and VAT on assets upon termination of registration.

title: What are the channels available for a vendor to submit a VAT201 Return?
text: A vendor can submit a VAT201 Return through two channels: eFiling or at a SARS Office. All vendors should make an appointment on the SARS website.

title: How is Field 9 calculated on the VAT201 Return?
text: Field 9 must be equal to Field 8 x (r/100). This amount is automatically calculated on the VAT201 Return. Field 9 is calculated by applying the tax rate (i.e. 15%) instead of the tax fraction (15/115) to Field 8.

title: When is the VDP application number a mandatory field?
text: The VDP application number is a mandatory field if the Y block is selected.

title: What is the purpose of the notes that accompany the VATSOA?
text: The notes are provided to assist the reader on the interpretation of the account and provide explanations for tax periods indicated as those for which the vendor was not registered.

title: What additional fields were introduced in the new VAT201 Return in landscape format?
text: The new VAT201 Return in landscape format contains the same fields as the previous VAT201 Return but with the following additional fields: Demographic information and the declarant's signature.

title: How is Field 33 calculated in the Rail & Harbour Services section?
text: Field 33 is calculated as the value of field 32 multiplied by the current diesel rate for rail in cents per litre, divided by 100. This amount is automatically calculated on the VAT201 Return.

title: What does the RED indicator ribbon represent in the VAT201 Return system?
text: The RED indicator represents 'Return is outstanding and overdue'. For example, the return for the tax period ending April is outstanding and overdue.

title: What is the time limit for vendors to revise the VAT portion of a VAT201 Return?
text: The vendor will be allowed to do a revision on the VAT portion of the VAT201 Return for any tax period within the last 5 years.

title: What happens to a payment that is abandoned during the payment process?
text: If a payment was abandoned during the payment process, the payment entry will be created and listed as 'General Unpaid' in the Payments menu. You will be able to confirm the payment by ticking the 'Pay' box.

title: What is the status of credit push transactions once they are approved?
text: Credit push transactions are irrevocable once approved.

title: How is the Total amount calculated on the VAT201 Return for vendors registered for diesel?
text: If you are registered for diesel and entered any amounts in the diesel section, then the Total field is calculated as Fields 20 – (25 + 29 + 33) + Penalties and Interest (where applicable).

title: What payment method must be used for any additional payment?
text: Any additional payment must be made using the same PRN.

title: Where can relevant material be uploaded after opening the SARS letter?
text: After opening the letter, another container will open on the VAT201 work page under SUPPORTING DOCUMENTS from where the relevant material can be uploaded.

title: What is the effective date of the VAT201 Return guide?
text: The effective date is 12 May 2025.

title: What happens when a VAT return is submitted at a SARS office for a registered eFiler?
text: If a VAT return is submitted at a SARS office, the submitted return, including estimations processed by SARS and the corresponding assessment notice (VAT217) will be sent to eFiling if the vendor is a registered eFiler. The VAT return status will be indicated on the VAT Work Page as 'Filed through another channel'.

title: What is the status of a VAT201 Return when a vendor requests a revision?
text: When a vendor requests for a revision, the selected filed return is presented in a SAVED status and is allocated a new version.

title: What is the key difference between Field 2 and Field 2A on the VAT201 Return?
text: Field 2 is for zero-rated supplies excluding exported goods, while Field 2A is specifically for zero-rated supplies of goods that have been exported from the RSA. Additionally, the Customs Code field is mandatory if Field 2A is completed.

title: What types of entities can register as an eFiler for VAT purposes?
text: Individuals, tax practitioners, and organisations (such as companies, close corporations, trusts, and exempt institutions) can register as eFilers for VAT purposes.

title: When is a vendor allowed to submit a VAT201 Return?
text: A vendor will only be allowed to submit a VAT201 Return on the 1st day of the month following the tax period for which the return must be made.

title: What document should vendors refer to for registration and user profile management for eFiling?
text: Vendors should refer to document GEN-ELEC-18-G01 titled 'How to register for eFiling and manage your user profile – External Guide'.

title: What happens when you click the 'Cancel' button during the payment process?
text: If you click on the 'Cancel' button, you will be redirected to the Return Search page.

title: What are the channels through which a vendor can request a VAT201 Return?
text: A vendor can request a VAT201 Return through eFiling or the Contact Centre.

title: What does the notes section explain regarding tax periods?
text: The notes will provide explanations for tax periods indicated as those for which the vendor was not registered for VAT and those for which the vendor was registered but on which no transactions were processed.

title: What happens when you click the 'Cancel' button during payment initiation?
text: If you click on the 'Cancel' button, you will be redirected to the Return Search page.

title: What status will be displayed on the supporting documents work page after documents have been submitted to SARS?
text: Once the documents have been submitted to SARS, the supporting documents work page will be updated with a "Submitted" status.

title: For which tax periods can a vendor request a VAT201 Return?
text: A vendor can request a VAT201 Return for the current tax period, the tax period following the current period, and past tax periods.

title: What is the time period for which a vendor can request a VATSOA on eFiling?
text: The vendor will only be able to request VATSOAs within the last 5 years from the date of request for statement. VATSOAs after March 2007 can be obtained on eFiling, while prior to this date the VATSOA must be obtained from a SARS branch.

title: What is the purpose of the GEN-ELEC-04-G01 guide?
text: The purpose of this guide is to assist vendors/tax practitioners to complete the Value-Added Tax (VAT201) return accurately and honestly. The guide includes the steps on how to request, complete and submit the VAT201 return and make payments.

title: What information is displayed on the work page of a submitted VAT201 Return?
text: The work page of a submitted VAT201 Return contains the following information: Return type (VAT201 Return), Tax period and Version (indicating the version number of the last submitted VAT201 Return), Status (indicating the current status), Submission date (indicating the date on which that particular version was submitted), Submission Channel (indicating the channel by which that version was submitted), and Last updated by (indicating the name of the person who last updated the VAT201 Return).

title: What information should be entered in Field 35 for Peak Power Plant diesel purchases?
text: Field 35 should contain the total amount of non-eligible litres in respect of diesel purchases for Peak Power Plant.

title: What requirements must payments at banking institution branches comply with?
text: These payments must comply with the same PRN requirements as EFT payments.

title: What is the effective date of the VAT201 Return guide?
text: The effective date is 12 May 2025.

title: What is the formula for calculating Total Output Tax in Field 13?
text: Total Output Tax in Field 13 must be equal to the sum of Fields (4+4A+9+11+12). This amount is automatically calculated on the VAT201 Return.
